# Backy page and backend completeness audit

Date: 2026-05-10

This audit tracks whether each Backy admin/public surface has the necessary backend and editor behavior expected from a WordPress/Wix/Canva-like product. It is intentionally strict: a page is not complete until it persists through backend APIs, exposes the right public/admin contract, handles loading/empty/error states, and supports the workflows users expect from a production CMS/editor.

## Completion legend

- **Ready**: backend-backed, validated, user-facing behavior is complete for the current product scope.
- **Partial**: usable path exists, but important backend/product behavior is missing.
- **Prototype**: mostly local/mock UI or incomplete workflow.
- **Missing**: route or workflow is absent.

## Current product increment

- 2026-05-28: Completion-status now explains the remaining four Partial rows in both default no-artifact mode and artifact-backed release mode. `apps/public/src/lib/completionStatusClosure.ts` adds a shared `backy.partial-closure-readiness.v1` handoff consumed by public manifest `data.contract.completionStatus`, site OpenAPI `x-backy-completion-status`, and authenticated `GET /api/admin/settings data.settings.completionStatus`. The handoff keeps the published audit at `41 Ready / 4 Partial / 0 Prototype / 0 Missing`, maps `/settings` and Settings admin APIs to `artifacts/backy-settings-provider-certification.json`, maps `/products` and `/orders` to `artifacts/backy-commerce-provider-certification.json`, and publishes the exact artifact-required doctor command that turns the provider closure block from default `0 Ready / 4 Partial` to artifact-accepted `4 Ready / 0 Partial` after fresh no-secret artifacts pass. `npm run test:partial-gate-preflights` passed on 2026-05-28 before this handoff update, proving the aggregate preflight/source/RBAC/provider contract gates remain green.
- 2026-05-28: `/products` catalog command-center secondary actions now expose the same ready/blocked action contract as Pages, Blog, and the Storefront API panel. `apps/admin/src/routes/products.tsx` adds a shared `products-command-secondary-action-status` region, group metadata on the default-collapsed More actions disclosure, and labelled action metadata for Copy manifest, Download JSON, Export CSV, CSV template, Import CSV, and Storefront page, including busy, permission, missing collection, and empty-export disabled reasons. `apps/admin/scripts/commerce-smoke.mjs` source-guards the command contract and rendered-guards that the collapsed secondary group plus all six catalog buttons publish `aria-describedby`, `data-action-state`, `data-action-status`, and ready/blocked `data-disabled-reason` consistency in the admin-ready path. `node --check apps/admin/scripts/commerce-smoke.mjs`, `BACKY_COMMERCE_SOURCE_ONLY=1 npm run test:commerce --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `npm run typecheck --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, and `BACKY_COMMERCE_CDP_PORT=9850 BACKY_ADMIN_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:5173 BACKY_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3001 BACKY_ADMIN_MFA_CODE=<dev-mfa-code> BACKY_ADMIN_2FA_CODE=<dev-mfa-code> npm run test:commerce --workspace @backy-cms/admin` passed on 2026-05-28. Count unchanged in default no-artifact mode: the four remaining Partial rows are still the external live Settings/Commerce provider certification artifacts.
- 2026-05-28: `/pages` command-center secondary actions now expose the same ready/blocked action contract as Blog, Dashboard, and the editor controls. `apps/admin/src/routes/pages.tsx` adds a shared `pages-command-secondary-action-status` region, group metadata on the default-collapsed More actions disclosure, and labelled action metadata for Copy handoff, Download JSON, and Export CSV, including busy, permission, and empty-export disabled reasons. `apps/admin/scripts/pages-list-smoke.mjs` source-guards the contract and rendered-guards that the collapsed secondary group plus all three buttons publish `aria-describedby`, `data-action-state`, `data-action-status`, and empty `data-disabled-reason` in the admin-ready path, then re-filters to zero pages and verifies Export CSV becomes blocked with the explicit empty-export reason while copy/download stay ready. `node --check apps/admin/scripts/pages-list-smoke.mjs`, `BACKY_PAGES_LIST_SOURCE_ONLY=1 npm run test:pages-list --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `npm run typecheck --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, and `BACKY_PAGES_LIST_ROW_ACTION_LABEL_SMOKE=1 BACKY_PAGES_LIST_CDP_PORT=9846 BACKY_ADMIN_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:5173 BACKY_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3001 BACKY_ADMIN_MFA_CODE=<dev-mfa-code> BACKY_ADMIN_2FA_CODE=<dev-mfa-code> npm run test:pages-list --workspace @backy-cms/admin` passed on 2026-05-28. Count unchanged in default no-artifact mode: the four remaining Partial rows are still the external live Settings/Commerce provider certification artifacts.
- 2026-05-28: `/blog` command-center secondary actions now expose the same ready/blocked action contract as the primary New post link and newer dashboard/editor controls. `apps/admin/src/routes/blog.tsx` adds a shared `blog-command-secondary-action-status` region, group metadata on the collapsed More actions disclosure, and stable labelled action metadata for Copy handoff, Download JSON, and Export CSV, including busy, permission, and empty-export disabled reasons. `apps/admin/scripts/blog-list-smoke.mjs` source-guards the command contract and rendered-guards that the collapsed secondary group plus all three buttons publish `aria-describedby`, `data-action-state`, `data-action-status`, and empty `data-disabled-reason` in the admin-ready path. The rendered smoke also now keeps the exact seeded revision-note proof before opening the focused editor while allowing a newer post-editor revision to become latest. `node --check apps/admin/scripts/blog-list-smoke.mjs`, `BACKY_BLOG_LIST_SOURCE_ONLY=1 npm run test:blog-list --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `npm run typecheck --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `BACKY_BLOG_BULK_STATUS_SMOKE=1 BACKY_BLOG_LIST_CDP_PORT=9844 BACKY_ADMIN_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:5173 BACKY_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3001 BACKY_ADMIN_MFA_CODE=<dev-mfa-code> BACKY_ADMIN_2FA_CODE=<dev-mfa-code> npm run test:blog-list --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, and `BACKY_BLOG_LIST_CDP_PORT=9845 BACKY_ADMIN_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:5173 BACKY_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3001 BACKY_ADMIN_MFA_CODE=<dev-mfa-code> BACKY_ADMIN_2FA_CODE=<dev-mfa-code> npm run test:blog-list --workspace @backy-cms/admin` passed on 2026-05-28. - 2026-05-27: `/` dashboard quick-create coverage now matches the global sidebar for daily builder work. `apps/admin/src/routes/index.tsx` adds permission-aware `New product` and `New form` actions to the dashboard command center and workflow grids, gates them with `commerce.edit` and `forms.create`, and routes them through `/products?siteId=<site>&quickCreate=product` and `/forms?siteId=<site>&quickCreate=blank` so those buttons open the existing product/form creation intents instead of acting like plain list links. `apps/admin/scripts/dashboard-smoke.mjs` source-guards the new permissions/search contract and rendered-guards the command center status plus quick-create hrefs. `node --check apps/admin/scripts/dashboard-smoke.mjs`, `BACKY_DASHBOARD_SOURCE_ONLY=1 npm run test:dashboard --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `npm run typecheck --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `BACKY_DASHBOARD_COMMAND_ACTION_STATUS_SMOKE=1 BACKY_DASHBOARD_CDP_PORT=9841 BACKY_ADMIN_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:5173 BACKY_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3001 BACKY_ADMIN_MFA_CODE=<dev-mfa-code> BACKY_ADMIN_2FA_CODE=<dev-mfa-code> npm run test:dashboard --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, and `BACKY_DASHBOARD_CDP_PORT=9842 BACKY_ADMIN_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:5173 BACKY_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3001 BACKY_ADMIN_MFA_CODE=<dev-mfa-code> BACKY_ADMIN_2FA_CODE=<dev-mfa-code> npm run test:dashboard --workspace @backy-cms/admin` passed on 2026-05-27. - 2026-05-27: The global admin sidebar quick-create launcher now covers the four daily creation flows instead of only pages and posts. `apps/admin/src/components/layout/sidebarModel.ts` adds `New product` and `New form` actions with `commerce.edit` and `forms.create` permission gates, `apps/admin/src/components/layout/Sidebar.tsx` merges action-specific create intent search params with the active site, `apps/admin/src/routes/products.tsx` consumes `quickCreate=product` to open a clean new product draft, and `apps/admin/src/routes/forms.tsx` consumes `quickCreate=blank` to prepare the blank-form creation command without silently writing a record. `apps/admin/scripts/login-smoke.mjs` now source-guards and rendered-guards all four shortcuts, including route transitions through `/pages/new`, `/blog/new`, `/products`, and `/forms`. `node --check apps/admin/scripts/login-smoke.mjs`, `BACKY_LOGIN_SOURCE_ONLY=1 npm run test:login --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `BACKY_FORMS_SOURCE_ONLY=1 npm run test:forms --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `BACKY_COMMERCE_SOURCE_ONLY=1 npm run test:commerce --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `npm run typecheck --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `BACKY_LOGIN_SIDEBAR_CREATE_SMOKE=1 BACKY_LOGIN_CDP_PORT=9832 BACKY_ADMIN_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:5173 BACKY_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3001 BACKY_ADMIN_MFA_CODE=<dev-mfa-code> BACKY_ADMIN_2FA_CODE=<dev-mfa-code> npm run test:login --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `BACKY_RELEASE_CERTIFICATION_DOCTOR_REQUIRED=1 BACKY_PROVIDER_CERTIFICATION_ARTIFACTS_REQUIRED=1 BACKY_SETTINGS_CERTIFICATION_ARTIFACT_PATH=tmp/backy-settings-provider-certification.json BACKY_COMMERCE_CERTIFICATION_ARTIFACT_PATH=tmp/backy-commerce-provider-certification.json npm run doctor:release-certification --silent`, `git diff --check`, and the focused diff secret scan passed on 2026-05-27. Default no-artifact doctor mode still reports the provider rows as Partial, while attached fresh redacted artifacts verified `4 Ready / 0 Partial / 0 Prototype / 0 Missing` for the provider closure block.
- 2026-05-27: Products and Orders live-provider certification command builders now expose ready/blocked action metadata on every provider-family toggle and provider selector, not only the copy/download actions. `apps/admin/src/routes/products.tsx` and `apps/admin/src/routes/orders.tsx` add shared certification control props, per-family disabled reasons, and `data-provider-certification-family` metadata so operators can see whether payment, tax, shipping, discount, catalog, subscription, webhook, and fulfillment families are ready, blocked by workspace busy state, or disabled until selected. `apps/admin/scripts/commerce-smoke.mjs` and `apps/admin/scripts/orders-smoke.mjs` now guard that control contract, and `apps/admin/scripts/commerce-smoke.mjs` adds a focused rendered Products provider-certification mode so this panel can be verified without running the unrelated public checkout-total path. `apps/admin/package.json` exposes it as `test:commerce-provider-certification-rendered`. `node --check apps/admin/scripts/commerce-smoke.mjs && node --check apps/admin/scripts/orders-smoke.mjs`, `BACKY_COMMERCE_SOURCE_ONLY=1 npm run test:commerce --workspace @backy-cms/admin && BACKY_ORDERS_SOURCE_ONLY=1 npm run test:orders --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `npm run typecheck --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `BACKY_COMMERCE_CDP_PORT=9820 BACKY_ADMIN_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:5173 BACKY_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3001 BACKY_ADMIN_MFA_CODE=backy-dev-mfa BACKY_ADMIN_2FA_CODE=backy-dev-mfa npm run test:commerce-provider-certification-rendered --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `BACKY_ORDERS_CDP_PORT=9821 BACKY_ADMIN_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:5173 BACKY_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3001 BACKY_ADMIN_MFA_CODE=backy-dev-mfa BACKY_ADMIN_2FA_CODE=backy-dev-mfa npm run test:orders --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `npm run doctor:release-certification --silent`, `git diff --check`, and the focused diff secret scan passed on 2026-05-27. Count unchanged: the four remaining Partial rows are still external live Settings/Commerce provider certification artifacts.

Count unchanged: the four remaining Partial rows are still external live Settings/Commerce provider certification artifacts.

- 2026-05-27: `/products` live-provider certification buttons now publish the same ready/blocked action-status contract as the rest of the commerce admin UI. `apps/admin/src/routes/products.tsx` adds a shared `products-provider-certification-action-status` region to the Products provider-certification panel, labels the panel as `Products provider certification actions`, and wires Copy provider handoff, Copy CI command, Download provider JSON, Copy env template, Copy guarded command, and Copy evidence packet to explicit `aria-describedby`, `data-action-state`, `data-action-status`, and `data-disabled-reason` metadata. - 2026-05-27: `/settings` now makes the remaining Partial closure path visible directly in the Backy completion status panel instead of requiring operators to open nested runbooks first. `apps/admin/src/routes/settings.tsx` adds a two-card closure summary for the Settings live provider certification gate and Commerce live provider certification gate, maps each gate to the exact audit rows it closes (`/settings` + Settings admin APIs, `/products` + `/orders`), shows the related runbooks, redacted artifact paths, verifier command, live gate state, and copyable certification command with action-status metadata. `apps/admin/scripts/settings-smoke.mjs` now source-guards and rendered-guards that closure summary while accepting the valid `ready-to-run` and `blocked-missing-inputs` gate states from different runtime evidence configurations. `node --check apps/admin/scripts/settings-smoke.mjs`, `BACKY_SETTINGS_SOURCE_ONLY=1 npm run test:settings --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `npm run typecheck --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `BACKY_SETTINGS_CDP_PORT=9805 BACKY_ADMIN_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:5173 BACKY_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3001 BACKY_ADMIN_MFA_CODE=backy-dev-mfa BACKY_ADMIN_2FA_CODE=backy-dev-mfa npm run test:settings --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `npm run doctor:release-certification --silent`, `git diff --check`, and the focused diff secret scan passed on 2026-05-27. - 2026-05-27: The page/blog editor component library now keeps the left rail focused on daily builder work instead of rendering every category button up front. `apps/admin/src/components/editor/ComponentLibrary.tsx` keeps Essentials, All, Recent, Favorites, and Saved visible in the primary category grid, moves Basic, Content, Media, Layout, Form, and Advanced into a native default-collapsed `More categories` disclosure, and preserves the existing category button hooks, action-status metadata, counts, search, recents, favorites, saved-section actions, drag/add, and keyboard-add behavior. `apps/admin/scripts/editor-drag-smoke.mjs` now source-guards the primary/collapsed-secondary hierarchy and the rendered editor-library smoke verifies the disclosure starts closed, expands before secondary category use, and marks secondary selection state while preserving library filtering, previews, favorites, recents, reusable-section actions, text editing, and quick-add coverage. `node --check apps/admin/scripts/editor-drag-smoke.mjs`, `BACKY_EDITOR_SOURCE_ONLY=1 npm run test:editor-drag --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `npm run typecheck --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `BACKY_CDP_PORT=9798 BACKY_ADMIN_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:5173 BACKY_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3001 BACKY_ADMIN_MFA_CODE=backy-dev-mfa BACKY_ADMIN_2FA_CODE=backy-dev-mfa npm run test:editor-library --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `npm run doctor:release-certification --silent`, `git diff --check`, and the focused diff secret scan passed on 2026-05-27. The in-app Browser path was attempted and reported `Browser is not available: iab`, so repo CDP validation was used as the rendered fallback. This reduces editor-left-panel overload and does not change the count: the four remaining Partial rows are still external live Settings/Commerce provider certification artifacts.
- 2026-05-27: `/sites/:siteId` now treats the site workspace as an operator-first page instead of leading with handoff/export controls. `apps/admin/src/routes/sites.$siteId.tsx` moves `Open public site` and `Site settings` into a dedicated primary command row with shared action-status metadata, moves Copy API URL, Copy handoff, and Download JSON behind a default-collapsed `More actions` disclosure, and fixes the first-render permission-matrix race that could leave navigation, redirects, SEO, frontend design, and webhooks panels permanently marked unavailable before permissions loaded. `apps/admin/scripts/site-detail-smoke.mjs` now source-guards the command-center hierarchy and rendered-smoke verifies primary/secondary action order, ready/blocked metadata, collapsed handoff/API disclosure, no duplicated primary handoff controls, and the recovered permission-aware panel hydration while preserving readiness, domain, theme/publish, webhooks, navigation, frontend design contract, template registry, redirects, SEO, audit, forms, comments, contacts, and screenshot coverage. `node --check apps/admin/scripts/site-detail-smoke.mjs`, `BACKY_SITE_DETAIL_SOURCE_ONLY=1 npm run test:site-detail --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `npm run typecheck --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, and `BACKY_SITE_DETAIL_CDP_PORT=9796 BACKY_ADMIN_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:5173 BACKY_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3001 npm run test:site-detail --workspace @backy-cms/admin` passed on 2026-05-27. This improves the site workspace UX and fixes a real rendered hydration bug, but the count remains unchanged: the four remaining Partial rows are still external live Settings/Commerce provider certification artifacts.
- 2026-05-27: `/comments` now leads the command center and selected-comment toolbar with moderation work instead of export/handoff controls. `apps/admin/src/routes/comments.tsx` adds a `Review pending` primary action with shared command action-status metadata, keeps `Refresh comments` beside it, moves `Export CSV`, Copy manifest, and Download JSON behind a default-collapsed `More actions` disclosure, and reorders selected-comment bulk actions so Approve, Reject, Spam, Block, and Resolve reports come before CSV export. `apps/admin/scripts/comments-smoke.mjs` now source-guards that hierarchy and rendered-smoke verifies primary/secondary action order, ready/blocked metadata, collapsed handoff/export disclosure, no duplicated primary handoff controls, and the moderation-first bulk toolbar while preserving policy, captcha, reports, delivery retry, thread, blocklist, audit, and screenshot coverage. Completion-status hardening also now exposes Settings/Commerce artifact-output plus artifact-doctor commands in the admin and public Settings completion APIs, locks Settings workflow verify-before-upload ordering, tightens Commerce Product/Order evidence packet guard assertions, and aligns the Orders env template heading with the shared Commerce provider certification contract. `node --check apps/admin/scripts/comments-smoke.mjs`, `BACKY_COMMENTS_SOURCE_ONLY=1 npm run test:comments --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `npm run test:settings-provider-certification-preflight-contract`, `npm run test:commerce-provider-certification-preflight-contract`, `npm run typecheck --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `npm run test:settings-admin-contract --workspace @backy/public`, `BACKY_SETTINGS_SOURCE_ONLY=1 npm run test:settings --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `npm run typecheck --workspace @backy/public`, and `BACKY_COMMENTS_CDP_PORT=9792 BACKY_ADMIN_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:5173 BACKY_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3001 npm run test:comments --workspace @backy-cms/admin` passed on 2026-05-27. This improves the moderation workflow and remaining Partial handoff consistency, but the count remains unchanged: the four remaining Partial rows are still external live Settings/Commerce provider certification artifacts.

- 2026-05-26: Commerce certification artifacts now make selected-site proof explicit at the artifact boundary. `npm run ci:commerce-provider-certification` writes non-secret `target.siteId` plus `target.siteSelectorEnv: BACKY_COMMERCE_CERTIFY_SITE_ID`, and the release doctor rejects saved Commerce artifacts when that top-level target is missing or does not match `apiHandoffs.siteId` even if nested product/order handoffs look valid. Completion-status `surfaceRunbooks[].artifactVerifier.validates` now lists `commerceArtifactSiteTargetReady`, `commerceArtifactTargetSiteId`, `commerceArtifactSiteSelectorEnvReady`, and `commerceArtifactSiteSelectorEnv` beside the existing public/product/order API-handoff checks, so external admin consoles can show the exact selected-site artifact proof needed before `/products` or `/orders` can leave Partial.

- 2026-05-26: `/forms` primary creation entry points now share a `forms-create-action-status` contract across the command-center New blank form button, template-panel blank form button, empty-state blank form button, account registration creation, standard form templates, and captured frontend form templates. Opening `/pages/:pageId/edit?focus=canvas`, `/blog/:postId?focus=canvas`, or `/blog/new?focus=canvas` now starts on a compact canvas-first workspace with the Components and Inspector columns hidden by default, while the in-canvas context bar still exposes Components, Layers, Inspector, and Show panels recovery actions. `apps/admin/scripts/page-create-smoke.mjs`, `apps/admin/scripts/blog-create-smoke.mjs`, `apps/admin/scripts/blog-editor-smoke.mjs`, `apps/admin/scripts/pages-list-smoke.mjs`, and `apps/admin/scripts/blog-list-smoke.mjs` now guard this contract; `node --check` for those scripts, `npm run typecheck --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `BACKY_PAGE_CREATE_STARTER_FILTER=blank BACKY_ADMIN_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:5173 BACKY_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3001 npm run test:page-create --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `BACKY_ADMIN_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:5173 BACKY_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3001 npm run test:blog-create --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, and `BACKY_ADMIN_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:5173 BACKY_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3001 npm run test:blog-editor --workspace @backy-cms/admin` passed on 2026-05-26.
- 2026-05-25: `/orders` default UX now keeps the Order API/security packet, provider execution readiness, operation action plan, cron/reconciliation diagnostics, live provider-certification runbooks, backend contract cards, API response contracts, analytics, provider execution analytics, and delivery-event diagnostics behind collapsed disclosures instead of placing the full operations runbook before the order queue. The visible default flow now moves from Order command center to active site, order metrics, queue, bulk actions, and the sticky editor, while expanded disclosures still expose checkout/admin/status-handoff URLs, provider handoff/certification exports, env templates, evidence packets, private backend contracts, and delivery-event refresh controls. `apps/admin/scripts/orders-smoke.mjs` now guards the compact disclosure contract and verifies the hidden DOM handoff still contains custom-frontend API contracts, provider readiness, operation impact, certification evidence, analytics, delivery diagnostics, import controls, and the authenticated admin API action while the rendered orders smoke still creates orders, imports CSV, updates customer profile state, validates status handoff privacy, exercises provider operations, and checks no horizontal overflow.
- 2026-05-25: `/products` default UX now keeps the Storefront API, product API response contracts, checkout/order-intake handoff, page-template briefs, commerce analytics, and live provider-certification runbooks behind a collapsed storefront/provider disclosure instead of placing the entire provider/API packet before the catalog. The visible default flow now moves from Catalog command center to active site, catalog metrics, product grid, and editor, while the expanded handoff still exposes API snippets, copy/export/import actions, storefront page shortcuts, provider reconciliation, selected-site certification target, evidence packet, runtime credential readiness, and product page-template creation. `apps/admin/scripts/commerce-smoke.mjs` now guards the compact disclosure contract and verifies the hidden DOM handoff still contains custom-frontend API contracts, page/editor binding contracts, live provider certification evidence, customer analytics, provider sync, and page-template buttons while the rendered commerce smoke still creates products, imports CSV, executes order intake, updates customer profile state, validates public catalog delivery, and checks no horizontal overflow.
- 2026-05-25: `/blog` list UX now keeps the workflow map, connected editorial surfaces, API handoff, and taxonomy manager behind collapsed disclosures so the default page prioritizes New post, readiness, site/status controls, bulk actions, filters, and the post DataGrid. The advanced controls remain present for users who expand them: the taxonomy manager still creates/edits/deletes categories and tags through backend APIs, the Blog API snippets still expose public/admin/custom-frontend handoff URLs, and the focused row Edit action still opens the Canva-like post canvas. `apps/admin/scripts/blog-list-smoke.mjs` now guards the collapsed disclosure contract, opens taxonomy only when exercising taxonomy CRUD, and verifies the rendered page has no overflow, keeps API/taxonomy/advanced panels collapsed by default, preserves SEO/comment/revision row controls, and still publishes/delivers public blog feeds.
- 2026-05-25: `/pages` list UX now keeps advanced starter, connected-workflow, and API handoff material behind collapsed disclosures instead of putting every operational surface ahead of the actual page library. The visible default flow prioritizes New Page, refresh/delivery actions, readiness summary, site/status controls, filters, and the DataGrid/empty state, while preserving direct starter links, connected workflow links, frontend API snippets, readiness details, and data-binding contract metadata for users who expand the advanced panels. `apps/admin/scripts/pages-list-smoke.mjs` now guards the advanced disclosure contract, and the rendered Pages smoke confirms the starter catalog/API details stay collapsed while create, table, bulk publish, delivery, RBAC, and responsive visual checks still pass.
- 2026-05-25: Correct-workspace admin smoke verification confirms the current `/pages/new` and `/blog/new` create flows are backend-backed and working in `/Users/devanshvarshney/backy`. The login screen now advertises browser credential-manager hints for email/password, treats MFA as a string-capable workspace code rather than a numeric-only OTP, and shows the local seeded MFA phrase in dev. `apps/admin/scripts/login-smoke.mjs` now guards those login attributes, string MFA challenge entry, cookie/session survival across reload and dense-route navigation, lazy header search hydration, and the existing auth recovery/security paths so the "signed out after a few seconds" regression stays covered.
- 2026-05-25: Admin shell/sidebar usability now treats role-default navigation as immediately usable while backend permission details sync in the background, instead of making the left rail look late or unavailable. The collapsed sidebar rail now renders one fixed, unclipped tooltip outside the scroll container on hover/focus/move, preserving compact dense/editor surfaces without hiding labels. Dashboard smoke now collapses the sidebar, waits for the width transition, targets the Pages rail icon, verifies the fixed tooltip and route metadata, then re-expands before desktop/mobile visual capture; login smoke source guards the role-default readiness and rail tooltip contract.
- 2026-05-23: Public media file delivery and image-transform redirects now support custom-frontend cache revalidation. `GET /api/sites/:siteId/media/:mediaId/file` and `/transform` compute stable media-variant `ETag` plus `x-backy-cache-revision` headers, return `304` for matching `If-None-Match` only after site visibility, private signed-token, storage-path, type, and quarantine checks pass, and skip byte streaming/redirect delivery analytics on cache hits. Site OpenAPI and media upload contract guards document the `304` responses and cache headers.
- 2026-05-23: The JS SDK now exposes the same media delivery cache contract to custom frontends. `mediaFileCached()` fetches public or signed file bytes with `If-None-Match` and returns `ETag`, `x-backy-cache-revision`, content, site, and media metadata, while `mediaTransformCached()` uses manual redirect handling to revalidate optimizer redirects without following them. Standalone `buildBackyMediaTransformPath()` and `buildBackyMediaTransformUrl()` mirror the existing file URL builders, and SDK smoke uses a mocked fetch to prove 200/307 first reads plus 304 revalidation behavior.
- 2026-05-23: SDK page/blog creation helpers now let external Wix/Canva-style builders create editable content from a rich design envelope instead of hand-authoring Backy's nested payload shape. `buildBackyContentDesignPayload()` normalizes `customCss`/`customJs`, hydrates elements from `contentDocument`, refreshes `metadata.editorComposition`, and preserves assets, animation timelines, interactions, data bindings, editable maps, SEO, token refs, metadata, and canonical content documents. `buildBackyAdminPageCreateInput()` and `buildBackyAdminBlogPostCreateInput()` infer `frontendDesignTemplateId` and copy the same data into `meta.frontendDesign*` provenance before `createAdminPage()` / `createAdminBlogPost()` calls, with SDK runtime assertions plus frontend-contract source guards covering the helpers.
- 2026-05-23: Page and blog admin APIs now accept the same direct custom-frontend design envelope without requiring SDK shaping. `POST/PATCH /api/admin/sites/:siteId/pages` and `/blog` normalize `design`, `frontendDesign`, `designEnvelope`, or `frontendDesignEnvelope` into canonical `content`, keep explicit `content` overrides, infer frontend template provenance after stored-template seeding, and persist `frontendDesign*` metadata for content documents, elements, canvas size, custom CSS/JS, theme-token refs, assets, animation timelines, interactions, data bindings, editable maps, SEO, metadata, and binding hints before validation/readiness/audit/cache invalidation runs. OpenAPI now advertises `design` and `frontendDesign` as `FrontendDesignTemplateContent` aliases on page/blog update payloads.
- 2026-05-23: Reusable sections now share the same direct design-envelope path as pages and blog posts. `POST/PATCH /api/admin/sites/:siteId/reusable-sections` normalizes `design`, `frontendDesign`, `designEnvelope`, or `frontendDesignEnvelope` into reusable-section `content`, persists the same design provenance under `metadata.frontendDesign*`, and keeps existing version/audit/cache/webhook behavior. The SDK adds `buildBackyAdminReusableSectionCreateInput()` so external builders can save editable shared blocks from the same rich envelope used for page/blog creation.
- 2026-05-23: SDK direct design-envelope helpers now cover update flows too. `buildBackyAdminPageUpdateInput()`, `buildBackyAdminBlogPostUpdateInput()`, and `buildBackyAdminReusableSectionUpdateInput()` preserve optimistic conflict fields while normalizing rich design envelopes into canonical content plus `meta.frontendDesign*` / `metadata.frontendDesign*` provenance, so external builders can revise pages, posts, and saved sections without manually reshaping animation/assets/bindings/editor-state JSON.
- 2026-05-23: Product and collection-record APIs now accept the same direct custom-frontend design envelope. `POST/PATCH /api/admin/sites/:siteId/collections/:collectionId/records` normalizes `design`, `frontendDesign`, `designEnvelope`, or `frontendDesignEnvelope` after template seeding, then stores the editor state under `values.design` plus `values.frontendDesign*` aliases before validation, media sync, audit, webhooks, and cache invalidation. The SDK adds `buildBackyAdminCollectionRecordCreateInput()`, `buildBackyAdminCollectionRecordUpdateInput()`, `buildBackyAdminCommerceProductCreateInput()`, and `buildBackyAdminCommerceProductUpdateInput()` so custom storefront builders can create and revise editable product/detail pages without hand-authoring Backy's internal record-value provenance.
- 2026-05-23: Interactive component controls now preserve richer editor values for custom Canva-like blocks. Registry select/radio options keep typed string/number/boolean option values instead of flattening every choice to strings, textarea/code controls render as multi-line editors, JSON controls parse valid structured objects back into canvas props, and the sandboxed canvas preset/public registry now advertise color, textarea, and JSON controls. Editor source guards cover the typed option path and the public registry so custom interactive components can expose real styling/configuration controls without losing values during save/reopen.
- 2026-05-23: Live-management manifest/OpenAPI discovery now advertises the actual `props.*` paths used by Backy's editor for rich button/link/text appearance controls. External builders can discover and patch `props.backgroundColor`, `props.color`, border width/style/color/radius, padding/margin/opacity/box shadow, text alignment/transform/decoration/font style, button `actionPreset`/`actionValue`, link underline, and matching tablet/mobile responsive prop overrides instead of guessing from the broader `styles.*` aliases. SDK and frontend-contract source guards now fail if those custom frontend editable targets disappear.
- 2026-05-23: SDK editable-element descriptors now expose valid unset controls for common custom frontend elements, not only fields already present in saved JSON. `listBackyContentElements()` advertises default button action/background/border controls, link underline/target controls, form-field required/disabled controls, media fields, and tablet/mobile responsive prop targets with typed `string`/`number`/`boolean`/`color`/`asset` hints, so an external builder can render Wix-like controls before a user has saved the first value. The same smoke guard patches unset button/link/input controls through `patchBackyContentEditableFields()` and verifies font family remains a font-picker target while font weight/style stay string controls.
- 2026-05-23: SDK/live-management editable discovery now treats animation, actions, data bindings, and binding slots as first-class controls even before an element has existing behavior JSON. `listBackyContentElements()` advertises unset `animation.type`, duration/delay/easing/direction/trigger, scroll-trigger start/end/scrub, custom `from`/`to`, motion token refs, `actions`, `dataBindings`, and `bindingSlots` with typed string/number/boolean/JSON hints. Public manifest/OpenAPI live-management targets include the same behavior paths, and SDK smoke patches those unset fields into canonical `backy.content.v1` element state so custom page builders can expose animation and behavior panels on newly inserted elements.
- 2026-05-23: SDK/live-management editable discovery now also exposes unset theme style and token-reference controls for custom frontend editors. `listBackyContentElements()` advertises `styles.color`, font/spacing/radius/shadow style aliases, `tokenRefs.styles.*`, and tablet/mobile responsive style/token-ref overrides before those fields exist, with token-reference values typed as string token paths. Manifest/OpenAPI discovery now includes the same `styles.boxShadow`, `tokenRefs.styles.boxShadow`, and responsive style-token targets, keeping external design-token panels aligned with Backy's Settings theme contract and public renderer.
- 2026-05-23: SDK editable descriptors now expose unset layout and responsive geometry controls for imported/custom elements that do not yet carry explicit canvas coordinates. `listBackyContentElements()` advertises `layout.x/y/width/height/zIndex/rotation` plus `responsive.tablet/mobile` geometry, visibility, and lock targets with number/boolean hints even when those values are absent, and SDK smoke patches those paths into canonical content. This keeps external drag/resize/layer panels available for every element, not only elements originally created by Backy's canvas with complete geometry.
- 2026-05-23: Frontend-design contract token persistence now keeps custom motion token groups for animation-aware external builders. `normalizeFrontendDesignContract()` preserves `tokens.motion` plus typography/breakpoint/layout token groups, partial site/frontend-design PATCH merges nested token objects deeply, and the template-registry smoke proves adding one motion duration does not wipe existing duration/easing/preset motion tokens. The same path now accepts canvas-style `tokens.customCSS` as an alias for canonical `tokens.customCss`, keeping imported custom frontend style tokens from disappearing because the site contract used a different casing than page/blog content payloads. This keeps custom frontend animation timing and custom CSS metadata round-trippable through site Settings and `/api/admin/sites/:siteId/frontend-design`.
- 2026-05-23: Completion-status artifact verifier metadata now mirrors the stricter selected-site proof required by the release doctor. Public manifest `data.contract.completionStatus`, site OpenAPI `x-backy-completion-status`, authenticated `GET /api/admin/settings`, and SDK contract fixtures list `BACKY_SETTINGS_CERTIFY_SITE_ID`/nested `BACKY_COMMERCE_CERTIFY_SITE_ID` for Settings runbooks plus `BACKY_COMMERCE_CERTIFY_SITE_ID` for Products/Orders, and their `surfaceRunbooks[].artifactVerifier.validates` arrays name `settingsApiHandoffSiteTargetReady`, `settingsApiHandoffTargetSiteId`, `settingsApiHandoffSettingsSiteSelectorEnv`, `settingsApiHandoffCommerceSiteSelectorEnv`, `siteSettingsApiHandoffReady`, `commerceArtifactSiteTargetReady`, `commerceArtifactTargetSiteId`, `commerceArtifactSiteSelectorEnvReady`, `commerceArtifactSiteSelectorEnv`, `productApiHandoffSiteTargetReady`, `productApiHandoffTargetSiteId`, `orderApiHandoffSiteTargetReady`, `orderApiHandoffTargetSiteId`, and `commerceApiHandoffSiteSelectorEnv` before a saved provider artifact can close the four remaining Partial rows.
- 2026-05-23: Settings release artifact verification now rejects stale or schema-only Settings artifacts unless `GET /api/admin/settings?certificationSiteId={siteId}` returns a provider evidence packet tied to the selected site and the matching `GET /api/admin/sites/:siteId/settings` handoff is present. The Settings certification runner records `targetSiteId`, `settingsSiteSelectorEnv`, `commerceSiteSelectorEnv`, and command-preview selector coverage in `apiHandoffs.settingsAdminApi`, while the release doctor reports separate Settings schema readiness and site-target readiness booleans before accepting an artifact for `/settings` or Settings admin APIs.
- 2026-05-23: Settings provider evidence packets now type the selected-site API target for custom admin clients. `/settings`, `GET /api/admin/settings`, site OpenAPI, and generated SDK contracts expose `operatorEvidencePacket.target.settingsAdminApi`, `siteScopedSettingsApi`, `settingsApi`, `settingsSiteSelectorEnv`, and `commerceSiteSelectorEnv`, so certification consoles can fetch the global admin Settings handoff and selected site-scoped Settings handoff without relying on untyped extra fields.
- 2026-05-23: Central Media placement handoffs now include a `backy.media-editor-binding.v1` block for Wix/Canva-style editor placement. The selected asset JSON exposes recommended editor element types, compatible element types, `assetIds`, editable target paths, responsive target paths, an editor-ready `propsPatch`, and the media bind endpoint/request template, so custom frontend builders can place uploaded images, fonts, videos, audio, and downloadable files while preserving reopening/editing metadata instead of copying only HTML/React snippets.
- 2026-05-23: Frontend-design editable maps now preserve the rich control metadata already promised by the AI frontend schema instead of collapsing entries to selector/element/binding fields. Site frontend design contracts keep `field`, `targetPath`, `token`, `editable`, `permission`, `label`, `valueType`, `scope`, collection/record ids, source fields, and custom keys through normalization, OpenAPI, manifest schema, and SDK source typing so imported custom frontends can round-trip color/toggle/media/text controls for later Backy editing.
- 2026-05-23: Frontend-design capture now auto-infers editable control maps from captured elements, not only explicit editable-map JSON. Captured page/blog/form/product/section templates advertise layout, visibility, text, button/link, form-field, media/file, style, token-ref, responsive, animation, action, data-binding, and binding-slot target paths with `valueType`, `targetPath`, `field`, labels, source fields, and `editable: true`, so external Wix/Canva-style builders can construct control panels for imported templates before every value exists in saved JSON.
- 2026-05-23: The inferred frontend-design editable controls are now written back into each captured template's `content.editableMap`, then carried into page/blog clone content and `frontendDesignEditableMap` provenance metadata. New pages created from imported frontend templates therefore keep local editable controls for colors, files, responsive variants, actions, and animations instead of relying on the site-level design contract alone.
- 2026-05-23: SDK design-envelope builders now auto-infer and persist the same rich editable-map controls when custom frontends create or update pages, blog posts, reusable sections, collection records, and commerce products. `buildBackyContentDesignPayload()` merges explicit editable-map entries with inferred layout, visibility, text, file/media, style, token-ref, responsive, animation, action, data-binding, and binding-slot controls, then `buildBackyAdmin*FromDesignInput()` helpers store the merged map in content plus `frontendDesignEditableMap` provenance so SDK-created designs reopen with Wix/Canva-style control panels intact.
- 2026-05-23: Direct admin API custom-frontend design envelopes now match the SDK editable-map behavior. Raw `design`/`frontendDesign` payloads sent to page, blog, reusable-section, collection-record, and product admin APIs merge explicit editable maps with inferred layout, file/media, responsive, animation, action, and data-binding controls in `content.editableMap` and `frontendDesignEditableMap` provenance, so non-SDK custom admin clients do not lose rich editor controls.
- 2026-05-23: SDK and raw admin direct-design imports now synthesize stable frontend-design provenance ids when an external builder omits `templateId`. Page/blog/reusable-section imports derive `frontendDesignTemplateId` from the content document, slug, title, or direct-design identity; collection/product imports derive the same from record title/slug/SKU fallback metadata. Public page and blog APIs now use the shared provenance reader, so stored `frontendDesign*` content documents, elements, editable maps, animations, custom CSS/JS, and binding hints remain discoverable for imported designs even when the original custom frontend did not provide a named template.
- 2026-05-23: Repository-backed public page and blog APIs now expose the same first-class design-state fields that Backy persists on canonical `backy.content.v1` documents. Public page/post `content` responses include `themeTokenRefs`, `assets`, `animations`, `interactions`, `seo`, `dataBindings`, `editableMap`, `metadata`, and `contentDocument` alongside elements/canvas/custom CSS/JS, so custom frontend editors can reopen saved pages/posts from `/api/sites/:siteId/pages` and `/blog` without reverse-engineering nested content documents or relying only on render payloads.
- 2026-05-23: Public commerce catalog product design contracts now preserve record-shaped `assets`, `animations`, and `interactions` as well as array-shaped payloads. Product `designReadiness` counts both shapes, keeping product page templates imported from external builders editable when their animation timelines, asset manifests, or interaction maps are keyed records instead of lists.
- 2026-05-23: Reusable-section, frontend-design provenance, collection-template, and render-payload contracts now preserve record-shaped design-state maps for `assets`, `animations`, and `interactions` instead of forcing every imported builder payload into arrays. Direct design envelopes, reusable section content/metadata, public page/blog reads, render payloads, OpenAPI schemas, generated SDK types, and admin collection authored-template capture all accept array or keyed-record design state, so Wix/Canva-style builders can round-trip named animation timelines and asset/interaction maps without losing editor state.
- 2026-05-23: Schema form authoring now exposes first-class style controls for label/help colors, field fill, field border color/radius, and submit fill/text/radius. The full editor smoke applies those controls to a form schema, checks the live canvas preview, verifies persisted form props after save, and source-guards the public renderer so custom frontend pages reopen with the same editable form appearance state.
- 2026-05-23: Commerce release artifact verification now rejects stale or incomplete `/products` and `/orders` API handoff evidence unless the saved product provider-sync and order analytics evidence packets are tied to the selected `BACKY_COMMERCE_CERTIFY_SITE_ID`. The Commerce certification runner records product/order `targetSiteId`, `siteSelectorEnv`, and command-preview site-selector coverage in `apiHandoffs`, and `npm run doctor:release-certification` reports separate schema readiness and site-target readiness booleans before accepting a Commerce artifact for the remaining Partial rows.
- 2026-05-23: `/products` and `/orders` provider-certification command builders are now site-targeted for multi-site custom admins. The Products and Orders live-provider panels expose a read-only Certification site id, copied commands/env templates include `BACKY_COMMERCE_CERTIFY_SITE_ID`, API evidence packets from product provider-sync and order analytics include `operatorEvidencePacket.target.siteId` plus `siteSelectorEnv`, and completion-status runbooks now list the site selector beside the external base URL/admin-key target inputs. This keeps Commerce provider proof tied to the selected site instead of a static demo target while the rows remain Partial until credentialed live-provider certification runs.
- 2026-05-23: `/settings` provider-certification command builders are now multi-site aware. The Settings UI exposes a Certification site id control, and both the rendered command builder plus `GET /api/admin/settings` provider-certification handoff now emit `BACKY_SETTINGS_CERTIFY_SITE_ID`, fallback `BACKY_SETTINGS_CERTIFICATION_SITE_ID`, and nested `BACKY_COMMERCE_CERTIFY_SITE_ID` target inputs with redacted evidence-packet target metadata for `/api/admin/sites/{siteId}/settings`. This keeps Settings and nested Commerce live-provider proof tied to the same selected site instead of leaving custom admin operators to patch site selectors by hand.
- 2026-05-23: SDK content patch helpers now attach central uploaded files to page/blog button or link elements without custom builders hand-authoring every `props.*` path. `buildBackyContentDownloadFilePatch()` converts Backy's downloadable-file prop contract into an element patch, and `patchBackyContentElementDownloadFile()` applies it to nested content while preserving the existing asset-reference synchronization. External editors can now take a media id from the central library and patch a selected element into a downloadable link in one SDK call before saving through live-management APIs.
- 2026-05-23: SDK media helpers now generate editor-compatible downloadable-file design state for custom frontend builders. `buildBackyMediaFilePath()`, `buildBackyMediaFileUrl()`, and `mediaFileUrl()` produce canonical public/signed file delivery URLs, while `buildBackyMediaDownloadLinkProps()` and `mediaDownloadLinkProps()` emit the same `href`, `download`, `fileId`, `fileIds`, `fileMediaId`, `downloadMediaId`, plural id arrays, attachment disposition, file URL aliases, and signed-url endpoint hint that Backy's full editor and hosted live overlay persist. External builders can now attach central uploaded files to buttons/links without manually reconstructing Backy's private design-state fields.
- 2026-05-23: SDK editable-element helpers now classify central downloadable-file media targets correctly for custom builder UIs. `listBackyContentElements()` reports single `fileMediaId` targets as `asset` and plural `downloadMediaIds` / responsive file-download media-id arrays as `assetList`, while `patchBackyContentElement()` and `patchBackyContentEditableFields()` synchronize those references into `assetIds`. This lets external Wix/Canva-style frontends choose an uploaded-file picker instead of treating downloadable-file bindings as generic rich-text arrays.
- 2026-05-23: Live-management discovery now advertises downloadable central-file bindings as first-class editable targets for custom frontend editors. Public manifest and site OpenAPI `backy.live-management.v1` include link target/download flags plus root, tablet, and mobile file/download id arrays, file URL aliases, file name/type/visibility metadata, attachment disposition, signed-URL-required booleans, signed-URL endpoint hints, and file-name targets. SDK/frontend contract guards verify those fields, so external builders can render uploaded-file pickers and private-file delivery controls before a button/link has saved download state.
- 2026-05-23: Hosted live-management link/button editing now preserves the same private downloadable-file metadata advertised by manifest/OpenAPI. When a quick overlay edit keeps the selected uploaded media id, Backy carries forward `fileMediaName`, `fileMediaType`, `fileMediaVisibility`, `fileName`, `fileSignedUrlRequired`, and existing signed-URL endpoint metadata instead of rewriting the link to id/url-only state, while still clearing stale metadata when downloads are disabled or a different file id is selected.
- 2026-05-23: Hosted live-management link/button editing now supports downloadable central-file bindings. The public overlay exposes a Download uploaded file toggle plus Backy file media-id input, infers file ids from Backy file id aliases, file/download id arrays, and media URLs, writes `fileId`, `fileIds`, `fileMediaId`, `downloadMediaId`, plural id arrays, attachment URLs, signed-url endpoint hints, `download: true`, and synchronized `assetIds`, and clears replaced file refs when authors switch back to ordinary links. Quick hosted edits can now preserve central uploaded documents/files the same way the full editor's download-file picker does.
- 2026-05-23: Public rendered download anchors now expose generic `data-backy-file-id` and resolve `data-backy-file-media-id` from `fileId`/`fileIds` as well as `fileMediaId`/`downloadMediaId` aliases. This keeps custom hosted frontends and browser-side integrations able to detect central downloadable-file identity even when a builder uses the generic file id fields from the SDK/helper contract.
- 2026-05-23: The full admin editor's downloadable-file picker now persists the same alias set as the SDK and live overlay: `fileId`, `fileIds`, `fileMediaId`, `fileMediaIds`, `downloadMediaId`, and `downloadMediaIds`, and its asset-id collector/cleanup reads those arrays when replacing or clearing files. Full editor saves, hosted quick edits, SDK patches, and public rendered anchors now agree on central uploaded-file identity for custom frontend handoff.
- 2026-05-23: Editor canvas metrics now classify the same plural media/file/download/font/background/poster/fallback asset-reference fields used by core migrations, repository sync, and the SDK. This keeps custom frontend design manifests from under-reporting media-bound layers when an element stores file/download identity through array aliases such as `fileMediaIds` or `downloadMediaIds`.
- 2026-05-23: The full editor canvas preview now exposes the same downloadable-file `data-backy-file-*` attributes as public rendering, including generic `data-backy-file-id` resolved from `fileId`/`fileIds` aliases and signed-URL metadata. Authors and custom admin integrations can inspect selected button/link file bindings directly on the design surface instead of waiting for hosted render output.
- 2026-05-23: Public rendered download anchors now infer `data-backy-file-signed-url-required="true"` from `fileMediaVisibility: "private"` even when an imported/custom frontend design omitted the explicit `fileSignedUrlRequired` boolean. Private central files stay discoverable as signed-delivery bindings from the hosted output with the same fallback behavior as the SDK and editor preview.
- 2026-05-23: The full editor preview now also normalizes boolean-like `fileSignedUrlRequired` values (`"true"`, `1`, `on`, `yes`) before exposing signed-download metadata. Imported or custom-generated frontend design JSON no longer has to use a native boolean for the editor surface to mark a selected download as signed-delivery required.
- 2026-05-23: Full editor preview and hosted live-management now normalize boolean-like `download` flags (`"true"`, `1`, `on`, `yes`) before rendering download anchors or toggling uploaded-file editing controls. Imported/custom frontend design JSON can round-trip downloadable button/link state without requiring a native JavaScript boolean.
- 2026-05-23: Custom frontend layer visibility and locking now normalize boolean-like values through the core content migration, SDK editable/command helpers, full admin editor canvas/Layers panel/inspector controls, hosted live overlay, and public renderer. Values such as `"false"` for `visible`, `"true"` for `locked`, and string/number `props.hidden` now round-trip through page/blog design state without accidentally exposing hidden layers or enabling locked-layer commands.
- 2026-05-23: Hosted live-management media editing now preserves central media identity instead of flattening image/video edits to URLs. The public overlay exposes Backy media-id fields for image sources, video sources, and poster images, infers media ids from Backy media URLs, writes `props.mediaId`/`props.mediaIds` and `props.posterMediaId`/`props.posterMediaIds`, and synchronizes element `assetIds` while clearing replaced media references. This keeps quick hosted-page edits aligned with the central organized media library and custom frontend API contracts.
- 2026-05-23: Settings provider certification now proves the selected site's site-scoped Settings API handoff as part of the redacted Settings artifact. The runner accepts `BACKY_SETTINGS_CERTIFY_SITE_ID` (default `site-demo`), records `apiHandoffs.siteScopedSettingsApi`, validates `GET /api/admin/sites/:siteId/settings` for `backy.site-settings-scope.v1`, `admin-site-settings-api` media-storage handoff, and `backy.frontend-database-certification.v1` evidence, and the release doctor now rejects stale Settings artifacts unless both global and site-scoped Settings API handoffs are present. This keeps the remaining Settings admin APIs Partial row tied to real multi-site custom-admin evidence instead of assuming the site-scoped API from the global Settings response.
- 2026-05-23: Public render payload font assets now use the same uploaded-family model as the public font manifest and editor font catalog. `/api/sites/:siteId/render` exposes grouped `assets.fonts[]` entries with `assetIds` plus per-variant media id, URL, weight, style, `font-display`, fallback, and CSS-family metadata, and `PageRenderer` emits an `@font-face` rule for every variant. Custom frontends can now hydrate and edit multi-file uploaded font families from the render API without collapsing regular/bold/italic faces to the first uploaded file.
- 2026-05-23: Right-pane rich-text font selection now persists central media font identity at the selected text-span level. Uploaded font choices write `fontMediaId`, `fontMediaIds`, `fontRegistration`, `fontSource`, and `fontFileUrl` marks into affected Slate text nodes, expose font source/media/face-count metadata on the selector options, and clear stale media marks when authors switch back to system, Google, or inherited fonts. This keeps custom frontend/API editors aligned with Backy's canvas editor for span-level uploaded font design state instead of only whole-element font bindings.
- 2026-05-23: Uploaded font variants now preview in the page/blog editor with the same stored family, weight, style, and `font-display` metadata that Backy's public font manifest publishes. The editor font catalog keeps a single selectable family for text controls while carrying every uploaded face behind that family, so regular/bold/italic uploaded files and display modes such as `optional` are reflected in the live canvas instead of collapsing to one hard-coded `swap` face.
- 2026-05-23: Advanced editor library blocks now seed tablet/mobile responsive geometry when inserted into pages. Registry-backed interactive figures, simulations, data explorers, sandboxed canvas/code components, and the embed/html/table blocks all carry default responsive sizes, and the source-only editor smoke now includes a catalog-wide guard requiring every stable-id preset or advanced custom block with a default size to define `defaultResponsive`. This keeps custom frontend/API extension blocks from starting as desktop-only objects on mobile pages.
- 2026-05-23: The same component-library responsive coverage now applies to ordinary drag-in blocks too. Basic text/heading/button/link/list/quote/icon, media image/video, layout box/section/header/nav/footer/divider/spacer/columns, form primitives, map, repeater, and comments blocks all seed tablet/mobile `defaultResponsive` sizes on insertion, and the catalog-wide source smoke now requires every component-library item with `defaultSize` to define `defaultResponsive` before it can ship.
- 2026-05-23: Programmatic starter/template element creation now uses the same catalog defaults as drag-in insertion. `createCanvasElement()` clones default styles, responsive overrides, and binding slots from the component catalog before applying explicit overrides, so page/blog/site templates and custom frontend starter documents do not drop authored mobile/tablet behavior when they create elements by type.
- 2026-05-23: Programmatic element creation now merges partial responsive overrides with catalog defaults per breakpoint. Starter/template calls that only override mobile position or props keep catalog tablet/mobile width/height/style metadata unless they explicitly change it, preventing generated page sections and filters from accidentally losing default responsive sizing.
- 2026-05-23: Programmatic element creation now also merges catalog default props and styles before explicit template overrides. Starter fields, buttons, media, forms, and layout elements keep baseline behavior such as input types, border styling, media defaults, form flags, and button defaults while still allowing templates to override the specific values they author.
- 2026-05-23: Component-library insertion now applies the same merge behavior on top of each item preset. `createCanvasElementFromLibraryItem()` combines item defaults with caller overrides for props, styles, responsive breakpoints, and binding slots before calling the element factory, and only replaces default children when explicit override children are supplied. This keeps drag/drop, nested insertions, and future template helpers from flattening editable preset design metadata.
- 2026-05-23: Page and blog creation from connected frontend templates now feed the full captured design envelope into the canonical content serializer instead of only passing custom CSS. New pages/posts seeded from frontend contracts persist custom JS, theme token refs, assets, interactions, animation metadata, data bindings, editable maps, SEO, content-document provenance, canvas size, and template metadata into both the saved `content` payload and `meta.frontendDesign*` provenance, with page/blog source guards covering the path.
- 2026-05-22: Editor component-library hero and feature-grid composed sections now seed authored tablet/mobile geometry instead of desktop-only placement. The presets carry root responsive canvas sizes plus child overrides for hero headline/copy/CTA/media, nested hero media/text, feature-grid heading/copy, responsive card stacking, and nested card text, and the source-only editor drag smoke guards those defaults so common Wix-like blocks stay editable after insertion on narrower canvases.
- 2026-05-22: The component-library lead-capture form preset now seeds authored tablet/mobile geometry for the form container and each editable child field. Name/email inputs, the message textarea, and submit button get breakpoint-specific positions and widths, and the editor drag source smoke guards the preset so inserted lead forms stay readable and tappable on mobile instead of inheriting desktop field sizing.
- 2026-05-22: The component-library member-registration form preset now carries the same responsive treatment for public signup flows. Its form container, full-name/email/phone/member-type fields, consent checkbox, and create-account button seed tablet/mobile geometry with extra mobile checkbox height for wrapped consent copy, keeping reusable member registration blocks aligned with the Forms/Users account-registration handoff.
- 2026-05-22: `/settings` notification webhook test and admin service-key issuance now use reachable inline validation. The test webhook action stays available while blank/invalid webhook URLs render `settings-notification-webhook-url-error`, and Issue key stays available for key managers while blank service-key labels render `settings-admin-service-key-label-error`; both paths block invalid provider/test/key metadata before notification delivery attempts or hash-only service-key grants are created.
- 2026-05-22: `/blog` taxonomy manager now uses reachable inline validation for category/tag names. Create/Save category and tag actions stay available for editors while blank taxonomy names render stable `blog-category-name-error` / `blog-tag-name-error` messages with `aria-invalid`/`aria-describedby`, and the save handlers block invalid taxonomy metadata before blog archive filters, feeds, SDK taxonomy reads, or custom frontend chips change.
- 2026-05-22: `/comments` official reply composer now uses reachable inline validation. Moderators can submit the composer even when the reply body is blank, the body textarea reports `aria-invalid`/`aria-describedby` and stable `comments-reply-content-*` ids after submit, and the existing handler blocks empty admin-authored replies before page/blog comment thread data or delivery events are persisted.
- 2026-05-22: `/contacts` saved-list and selected-contact sync actions now use reachable inline validation. Saved lead-list Save stays available for contact managers while blank list names render stable `contacts-saved-list-name-*` errors, and Sync selected stays available once contacts are selected while blank/invalid webhook URLs render stable `contacts-sync-webhook-url-error` before contact-list settings or outbound contact-sync delivery events are persisted.
- 2026-05-22: `/pages/:pageId/edit` reusable-section capture now keeps the editor Save/Rename section action reachable while custom validation handles blank names. The dialog tracks submit attempts, marks the reusable-section name field with `aria-invalid`/`aria-describedby`, renders stable `editor-reusable-section-name-*` ids, and blocks invalid saved-block metadata before the selected canvas tree is persisted into the reusable-section library.
- 2026-05-22: `/products` variant and gallery authoring controls now avoid disabled-only validation. Manual variant Add, option-matrix Generate, and gallery Add URL stay reachable for product editors, validate required variant identity, numeric price/stock, matrix option groups, and gallery URL/duplicate inputs inline with `aria-invalid`/`aria-describedby`, and expose stable variant/matrix/gallery error ids before sellable product choices or storefront gallery metadata are persisted.
- 2026-05-22: `/products` scheduled publishing now follows the same reachable inline-validation model as product identity saves. The Save/Create action stays available for product editors, invalid scheduled publish dates are marked on the datetime field with `aria-invalid`/`aria-describedby`, `products-scheduled-at-error` renders focused copy after submit, and the existing save handler blocks invalid scheduled storefront/API state before product records or provider-sync handoffs are persisted.
- 2026-05-22: `/orders` manual line-item entry now matches the reachable inline-validation model used by the order save form. The Add action stays available for editors instead of being disabled by a blank item title, validates line item title, whole-number quantity, and non-negative price, marks invalid controls with `aria-invalid`/`aria-describedby`, renders stable line-item field error ids, and blocks invalid structured item JSON updates before order totals and custom frontend order-status data are persisted.
- 2026-05-22: `/pages/:pageId/edit` Page Settings now uses reachable inline validation for page identity and metadata edits. The modal keeps Save available for editors while custom validation marks title, slug, scheduled publish date, and JSON-LD controls with `aria-invalid`/`aria-describedby`, renders stable field-level error ids, and blocks invalid route/metadata saves before page settings and custom frontend SEO/social handoff data are persisted. `BACKY_EDITOR_SOURCE_ONLY=1 npm run test:editor-drag --workspace @backy-cms/admin` and `npm run test:editor-smoke-coverage --workspace @backy-cms/admin` guard the source.
- 2026-05-22: The shared editor media picker now matches the central Media page's reachable inline folder validation. Its Create folder action stays reachable for permitted users even when the folder name is blank, marks the name field with `aria-invalid`/`aria-describedby`, renders `media-library-create-folder-name-error` for required or duplicate sibling names, and blocks invalid mutations before calling the media folder API. `BACKY_MEDIA_SOURCE_ONLY=1 npm run test:media --workspace @backy-cms/admin` guards the modal source so page/blog/product editors keep the same organized media-folder behavior as `/media`.
- 2026-05-22: `/settings` Appearance now renders a source-guarded `backy.settings-theme-design-impact.v1` panel and `GET /api/admin/settings` returns the same `data.settings.themeDesignImpact` contract for custom admin shells. The handoff packages the resolved `backy.theme.v1` token payload, CSS variables, editable color/type/layout/motion controls, `content.themeTokenRefs`, `element.animation.tokenRefs.*` binding paths, preserved custom frontend design fields, manifest/OpenAPI/admin settings pointers, and privacy flags without exporting admin keys, provider credentials, secrets, or private content.
- 2026-05-22: `/orders` now renders a source-guarded `backy.order-operation-impact.v1` panel in the selected-order status workspace and embeds the same object in `backy.order-status-handoff.v1`. The impact handoff summarizes recommended quote/label/tracking/fulfillment/refund action safety, provider-ready/manual/blocked counts, customer-safe frontend projection readiness, safe and masked binding paths, boolean-only payment/provider references, and privacy boundaries without exporting raw customer contact, payment references, provider execution ids, provider payloads, addresses, or internal notes.
- 2026-05-22: `/products` now renders a source-guarded `backy.product-sellability-impact.v1` panel inside selected-product launch readiness and embeds the same impact inside `backy.product-storefront-handoff.v1`. The handoff summarizes publish/archive/provider-sync action safety, launch blockers, pricing, inventory, delivery, checkout mode, custom frontend design readiness, provider-sync state, and privacy boundaries without exporting provider secrets, private orders, customer payloads, or digital delivery URLs.
- 2026-05-22: `/blog/:postId` now renders a source-guarded `backy.blog-publish-impact.v1` panel in the Publish workspace before status changes. The handoff summarizes route verification, taxonomy/author assignment, featured media readiness, site comment policy, moderation counts, readiness blockers, action disable reasons, and privacy boundaries without exporting canvas or raw comment content, so custom frontend article editors can copy publish impact before publishing or archiving.
- 2026-05-22: `/pages/:pageId/edit` now renders a source-guarded `backy.page-publish-impact.v1` panel in the Publish workspace before status changes. The handoff summarizes backend route verification, route conflicts, parent page, direct child pages, descendant status counts, navigation placement/label, readiness blockers, action disable reasons, and privacy boundaries without exporting canvas content. Authors and custom admin shells can copy this impact JSON before publishing, unpublishing, or archiving pages with nested navigation relationships.
- 2026-05-22: `/settings` Appearance now exposes source-guarded inline validation for global theme token controls. Color swatches and editable hex fields have stable test ids, invalid hex values are marked with `aria-invalid`/`aria-describedby` and field-level error copy, base font size/radius/spacing numeric controls expose the same accessible range validation, and the motion preset select is guarded so custom frontend design tokens remain editable without relying only on a top-level settings alert or backend rejection.
- 2026-05-22: `/comments` now renders source-guarded operator anti-abuse presets plus a destructive bulk-action safety review for large or cross-filter selections. The policy panel exposes preset buttons for community, strict-launch, and members-only moderation defaults; the moderation handoff exports preset and `backy.comments-bulk-safety.v1` data; and reject/spam/block bulk actions require a moderation reason when a selected set crosses the large-scope threshold or includes hidden filtered comments.
- 2026-05-22: `/sites/:siteId` site settings now uses source-guarded inline validation for existing workspace identity updates. The settings form uses `noValidate`, keeps Save reachable for users with site configure access, validates site name, URL slug, and custom domain, marks invalid controls with `aria-invalid`/`aria-describedby`, normalizes saved custom domains, and blocks invalid settings updates before site routing/domain API state is changed.
- 2026-05-22: `/sites/new` now uses source-guarded inline validation for multi-site workspace creation instead of browser-required fields and disabled-only invalid states. The create form uses `noValidate`, keeps the create action reachable for permitted users, marks site name, URL slug, custom domain, database team id, Vercel project/team/production-domain fields, billing email, and template import URL with `aria-invalid`/`aria-describedby`, and blocks invalid site creation before the site and starter-page APIs are called.
- 2026-05-22: `/teams` workspace create, edit, and invite forms now use source-guarded inline validation instead of browser-only required fields or broad submit errors. Team creation validates name length, team editing validates both name and slug format, invites validate email and role, invalid controls are marked with `aria-invalid`/`aria-describedby`, and stable field-level/alert test ids block bad multi-site workspace mutations before team APIs are called.
- 2026-05-22: `/users` CSV import now has source-guarded inline preflight before preview/import requests reach the backend. The admin UI rejects non-CSV files, empty files, missing `full_name`/`email` headers, missing rows, invalid emails, invalid role/status values, and duplicate CSV emails with a stable `role="alert"` error linked to the file input and import controls, so account imports fail with actionable copy before mutating workspace users.
- 2026-05-22: `/forms` standalone form-definition editing now uses source-guarded inline validation before saving custom frontend API schemas. The builder tracks submitted state, keeps the Save action reachable for editable dirty standalone forms, marks invalid machine names, notification/delivery URLs, consent emails, retention windows, spam limits, contact-share identity, collection targets, field keys, labels, options, defaults, and validation rules with `aria-invalid`/`aria-describedby`, and blocks silent key/default normalization before the public form definition and submission APIs are changed.
- 2026-05-22: `/collections` record editing now uses source-guarded inline validation for scheduled publish time and schema-driven CMS fields instead of relying on browser-required controls or a disabled scheduled save button. The record form uses `noValidate`, keeps Save reachable for users with collection edit access, marks scheduled time plus required/invalid field editors with `aria-invalid`/`aria-describedby`, renders stable record-field error ids, and blocks invalid JSON, number, URL, email, date, required-list, and media-count payloads before collection records are persisted through admin APIs.
- 2026-05-22: `/media` folder organization now uses source-guarded inline validation instead of disabled-only create/rename controls. The central file library keeps Add and folder-rename Save reachable for permitted users, validates required folder names plus duplicate sibling names, marks invalid controls with `aria-invalid`/`aria-describedby`, renders stable create/rename error ids, and blocks invalid folder mutations with focused copy so media, fonts, downloadable files, and image libraries can stay organized without ambiguous banner-only failures.
- 2026-05-22: `/reusable-sections` now pairs reusable block creation with source-guarded custom frontend design provenance and inline save validation. Frontend section templates now snapshot custom JS, content documents, theme-token refs, assets, animations, interactions, data bindings, editable maps, SEO, and template metadata into reusable-section metadata while preserving the same envelope in section content. The manual JSON editor uses `noValidate`, keeps Save reachable for editors, marks section name and content JSON with `aria-invalid`/`aria-describedby`, renders stable inline error ids, and blocks invalid saves with focused copy so users can repair reusable blocks without losing advanced design state.
- 2026-05-22: Canonical page/blog content elements now preserve grouped child scope as a first-class `parentId` field as well as the existing `metadata.parentId` fallback. Core content normalization assigns child parent ids from inline `children`, the admin saved-canvas loader reconstructs parent ids from inline tree structure or legacy metadata, and the public render payload schema documents `parentId`, `visible`, `locked`, and element `metadata`. Core content smokes plus the editor smoke coverage guard this so grouped custom frontend designs can reopen in page/blog editors and external builders without losing layer scope.
- 2026-05-22: Public form submission/contact security was tightened so custom frontends cannot override admin-configured contact sharing or request rate-limit bypasses. The public submission route now ignores `contactShareOverride`, repository and demo contact creation use only `form.contactShare`, public OpenAPI/SDK form/comment helper contracts no longer expose bypass or contact-share override fields, and the audit-log repository pushes valid actor filters into the database query while returning an empty result for invalid actor ids. Public security, frontend-contract, generated SDK type, public, SDK, and DB typecheck guards pass for this hardening pass.
- 2026-05-22: `/pages/new` now exposes field-level page creation validation instead of relying on browser required fields or a fully disabled create action. The form uses `noValidate`, keeps Create Page reachable after route checks complete, marks target site, title, navigation label, parent page, canonical path, JSON-LD, dataset collection, and schedule fields with `aria-invalid`/`aria-describedby`, and renders stable error ids after submit. The page-create source guard now fails if page identity, SEO/frontend metadata, dataset, or schedule validation loses the accessible inline path.
- 2026-05-22: `/blog/new` now matches the page creation validation model for article publishing. The create form uses `noValidate`, keeps Save draft/Publish/Schedule reachable for users with edit access, marks post title, URL slug, canonical path, and schedule date with `aria-invalid`/`aria-describedby`, and renders stable inline error ids after submit while preserving the existing route-conflict banner and backend persistence checks. The blog-create source guard now fails if article identity, route, SEO canonical, or scheduled-publish validation regresses to a disabled-only path.
- 2026-05-22: `/orders` now exposes inline order validation for required order number, customer, email, total, and item payload fields instead of hiding save behind disabled identity/contact fields. The order form uses `noValidate`, keeps save reachable for custom validation, marks invalid inputs with `aria-invalid` and `aria-describedby`, renders stable order/customer/email/total/items error ids, and blocks save with the focused "Fix required order fields before saving" message. The Orders source guard now fails if the inline validation path, accessible field wiring, or reachable save gate regresses.
- 2026-05-22: `/products` now exposes field-level product identity validation for the required storefront title and SKU instead of relying on a disabled save button plus a broad banner. The product form runs custom validation with `noValidate`, keeps the save action reachable, marks invalid title/SKU inputs with `aria-invalid` and `aria-describedby`, renders stable `products-title-error` and `products-sku-error` copy, and blocks save with the focused "Fix product identity fields before saving" message. The Commerce source guard now fails if the inline validation path or custom save gating regresses.
- 2026-05-22: The page editor handoff, revision comparison JSON, and visual revision diff now follow the live canvas state instead of the initially loaded canvas snapshot. `/pages/:pageId/edit` tracks current canvas elements and size through `CanvasEditor.onChange`, resets that tracker on page/revision reloads, refreshes it after saves, and builds the custom frontend design envelope from the current edited layers. The source-only page editor guard now fails if the handoff stops using live elements/size, so copied API payloads include newly edited design, grouping, animation, and editable-map state before and after save.
- 2026-05-22: `/products` now makes connected frontend product-template design state visible at the creation point instead of only after a product is saved.
