Each captured product template card renders a source-guarded `backy.product-design-readiness.v1` summary for editable design state, including element, animation, asset, and binding counts plus missing template/content/editable-binding keys. The copied `backy.frontend-product-template.v1` schema now includes the same readiness block, so custom storefront builders can verify animation and editable-map coverage before creating a product record from a frontend contract.
- 2026-05-22: Hosted live-management overlays now expose selected-element animation editing, closing the last visible gap between stored custom frontend motion state and quick hosted-page edits. Authors can update animation type, trigger, direction, duration, delay, easing, scroll trigger start/end/scrub, custom `from`/`to` JSON objects, and duration/easing token refs from the live overlay; saves patch the canonical element `animation` object through the authenticated page/blog live-management bridge. The frontend contract source guard now fails if the overlay loses the animation editor, JSON validation, scroll-trigger persistence, token-ref persistence, or save control.
- 2026-05-22: Reusable section templates now preserve the full custom frontend design-state envelope in section content, not only metadata provenance. Captured section templates that store visual defaults under nested `content.section` now inherit root-level content documents, theme-token refs, assets, animations, interactions, data bindings, editable maps, SEO, metadata, custom CSS, and custom JS when seeded through admin/custom APIs. The demo-store normalizer, admin reusable-section types/UI parser, OpenAPI `BackyReusableSectionContent`, generated SDK types, template-registry smoke, reusable-section smoke, and frontend contract guard now cover those fields so advanced section blocks remain editable after creation, import/export, and public list/detail reads.
- 2026-05-22: Completion-status runbooks now expose structured durable provider-certification artifact verifier metadata for every remaining Partial row. Public manifest `data.contract.completionStatus`, site OpenAPI `x-backy-completion-status`, and authenticated `GET /api/admin/settings data.settings.completionStatus` include `evidenceArtifacts` with artifact key, workflow, alternate workflow uploaders, artifact name, file path, artifact schema version, producer env, `requiredForReady: true`, and `includesSecretValues: false`. They also include `artifactVerifier` with the `npm run doctor:release-certification` command, required/path env aliases, expected schema version, validation checks, and `includesSecretValues: false` so custom admin clients can verify redacted workflow attachments before treating them as closure evidence. `/settings` and Settings admin APIs point at `artifacts/backy-settings-provider-certification.json` / `backy-settings-provider-certification-evidence`; `/products` and `/orders` point at `artifacts/backy-commerce-provider-certification.json` / `backy-commerce-provider-certification-evidence`. SDK smoke, generated types, OpenAPI schema, manifest schema, and source guards now fail if custom admin clients lose those structured artifact fields.
- 2026-05-22: Commerce provider certification now produces a durable non-secret evidence artifact for the remaining `/products` and `/orders` Partial rows. `ci:commerce-provider-certification` accepts `BACKY_COMMERCE_CERTIFICATION_OUTPUT`, writes `backy.commerce-provider-certification-artifact.v1` JSON beside the existing stdout result, and the standalone Commerce workflow, nested Commerce step in the Settings provider workflow, and top-level release workflow upload `artifacts/backy-commerce-provider-certification.json` as `backy-commerce-provider-certification-evidence` after the Commerce provider gate passes. The artifact preserves target mode, requested provider families, readiness booleans, runtime provider selections, and diagnostic counts while excluding external target URLs, admin keys, provider credentials, payment references, webhook bodies, and customer/order payloads.
- 2026-05-23: Commerce provider certification now validates the actual `/products` and `/orders` API handoff surfaces in addition to runtime provider-family readiness. The runner accepts `BACKY_COMMERCE_CERTIFY_SITE_ID`, defaults to `site-demo`, records redacted `apiHandoffs`, checks product provider-sync and order analytics schemas, and asserts provider-certification evidence packets plus product storefront/design-readiness handoffs without exposing provider secrets, admin keys, payment references, webhook bodies, customer payloads, or raw private orders.
- 2026-05-23: Commerce provider certification now records `apiHandoffs.publicApis` for the selected site's public manifest, catalog, and order contract. The runner validates `GET /api/sites/:siteId/manifest`, `GET /api/sites/:siteId/commerce/catalog`, and `GET /api/sites/:siteId/commerce/orders` for `backy.commerce-provider-certification-handoff.v1` before the release doctor accepts a Commerce artifact, so custom storefront/API consumers are proved alongside the admin `/products` and `/orders` handoffs.
- 2026-05-22: Settings provider certification now produces a durable non-secret evidence artifact for the remaining `/settings` and Settings admin API Partial rows. `ci:settings-provider-certification` accepts `BACKY_SETTINGS_CERTIFICATION_OUTPUT`, writes `backy.settings-provider-certification-artifact.v1` JSON beside the existing stdout result, and the standalone Settings plus top-level release workflows upload `artifacts/backy-settings-provider-certification.json` as `backy-settings-provider-certification-evidence` after the Settings provider gate passes. The artifact preserves target mode, selected provider families, scenario/result summaries, and the no-secret boundary while excluding external target URLs, admin keys, provider credentials, webhook bodies, and customer/order payloads.
- 2026-05-23: Settings provider certification now validates the concrete Settings admin API provider handoffs before writing completion evidence. The runner records `apiHandoffs.settingsAdminApi` from `GET /api/admin/settings`, including `backy.settings-provider-certification-handoff.v1`, `backy.settings-provider-certification-evidence.v1`, `backy.settings-provider-certification-evidence-packet.v1`, and `backy.completion-status.v1`, and records `apiHandoffs.siteScopedSettingsApi` from `GET /api/admin/sites/:siteId/settings` for the selected site-scoped media-storage and frontend-database handoff. Settings artifacts therefore prove custom admin clients can discover both global and per-site certification evidence through APIs.
- 2026-05-22: The release certification doctor now verifies saved redacted Settings and Commerce provider evidence artifacts when `BACKY_SETTINGS_CERTIFICATION_ARTIFACT_PATH` / `BACKY_COMMERCE_CERTIFICATION_ARTIFACT_PATH` are provided. `BACKY_PROVIDER_CERTIFICATION_ARTIFACTS_REQUIRED=1` turns missing or schema-invalid artifact files into doctor failures, and the doctor reports `certificationArtifacts` readiness booleans for JSON parsing, `ok: true`, expected artifact schema versions, and the no-secret boundary before operators archive those files as closure evidence for the remaining Partial rows.
- 2026-05-23: The release certification doctor now rejects stale Settings evidence artifacts that do not include certified Settings admin API handoff evidence. `certificationArtifacts.settings` reports Settings schema readiness, `BACKY_SETTINGS_CERTIFY_SITE_ID`/nested `BACKY_COMMERCE_CERTIFY_SITE_ID` target readiness, `settingsApiHandoffReady`, `settingsScenarioEvidenceReady`, `settingsEvidencePacketReady`, `settingsCompletionStatusReady`, and `settingsApiHandoffSurface`, and artifact-required mode fails if the `GET /api/admin/settings?certificationSiteId={siteId}` provider-certification, scenario-evidence, evidence-packet, completion-status handoff, or selected-site target proof is missing or schema-invalid.
- 2026-05-23: The release certification doctor now also rejects saved Settings/Commerce provider artifacts with forbidden sensitive field names or credential-bearing URLs. `certificationArtifacts.*.noForbiddenArtifactFieldsReady` must be true before evidence can close a Partial row, and failures return `forbiddenArtifactFields` paths such as `target.externalBaseUrl` or `results.webhookBody`. Public manifest, OpenAPI, and Settings completion-status artifact verifiers advertise the new "no forbidden artifact field names or credential URLs" check so custom admin clients do not archive artifacts that accidentally include admin keys, external target URLs, database URLs, webhook bodies, provider credentials, payment references, customer payloads, or raw order payloads.
- 2026-05-23: `npm run ci:settings-provider-certification` now runs the same forbidden-field and credential-URL scan on the final `backy.settings-provider-certification-artifact.v1` payload before writing `BACKY_SETTINGS_CERTIFICATION_OUTPUT` or printing stdout. This prevents `/settings` and Settings admin API closure evidence from being emitted if future certification changes accidentally include admin keys, external target URLs, database URLs, webhook bodies, provider credentials, payment references, customer payloads, raw order payloads, or URL-embedded credentials.
- 2026-05-23: Provider artifact verification now actively scans saved Settings and Commerce certification artifacts for raw secret-like values instead of only trusting the written no-secret boundary text. `certificationArtifacts.*.noRawSecretValuesReady` must be true before artifact-required doctor mode accepts a file, and the doctor contract rejects a schema-valid Settings artifact that contains an obvious `sk_live_...` style value while still reporting the API handoff flags separately.
- 2026-05-23: The release certification doctor now rejects stale Commerce evidence artifacts that do not include certified public commerce plus `/products` and `/orders` API handoff evidence. `certificationArtifacts.commerce` reports `apiHandoffReady`, `publicCommerceApiHandoffReady`, product/order schema readiness, product/order `BACKY_COMMERCE_CERTIFY_SITE_ID` target readiness, `apiHandoffSiteId`, and the expected public manifest/catalog/order-contract plus product provider-sync/order analytics endpoints, and artifact-required mode fails if any handoff is missing, schema-invalid, or not tied to the selected commerce certification site.
- 2026-05-22: Page editor saves now preserve the full connected frontend design envelope when authors reopen and modify pages. `normalizeSavedCanvasContent` extracts existing custom CSS/JS, theme token refs, asset manifests, interactions/animation timelines, SEO, data bindings, editable maps, and content metadata; `serializeCanvasContent` writes those fields back into both the top-level canvas payload and canonical `backy.content.v1` document; and the page editor threads the preserved design state through every save instead of replacing it with only current elements/canvas size. The core content migration path now also merges prior document-level design metadata when a minimal editor save payload omits it, while still letting current canvas size and edited elements win. Core content migration smoke and editor smoke coverage guard this so Wix/Canva-style page edits do not flatten custom frontend animation/design provenance.
- 2026-05-22: Canonical page/blog content documents now persist the current Canva-style editor composition summary whenever canvas elements are serialized or migrated. `canvasElementsToBackyContentDocument` stamps `metadata.editorComposition` with `backy.editor-composition-summary.v1`, total/root/group/nested/container/responsive/hidden/locked layer counts, group ids, nested element ids, container ids, group/ungroup/select shortcuts, and invariants for same-parent grouping, locked-layer blocking, `props.editorGroup`, inline children persistence, first-class parent ids plus legacy parent-id metadata, and tablet/mobile responsive breakpoints. `serializeCanvasContent` mirrors the enriched document metadata back to the top-level saved canvas payload, and the core migration smoke now proves current minimal editor saves recompute composition metadata instead of preserving stale fallback group counts. This gives custom frontend editors and APIs a durable way to verify grouped/nested page state after Cmd/Ctrl+G edits without reverse-engineering the raw tree.
- 2026-05-22: The same `metadata.editorComposition` summary now reports connected design-state layers, not only layout structure. Core and SDK summaries include animated/action/data-bound/token-ref/asset-bound/interactive layer counts, booleans, and element id lists, so custom frontend editors can quickly detect motion, actions, dataset bindings, theme-token usage, media/file references, and interactive components before reopening or saving page/blog content. Core content migration smoke asserts the canonical counts and ids for an animated/data-bound/interactive legacy canvas, and the SDK CI smoke now patches animation token refs, actions, data bindings, responsive media refs, and editable-map overrides before verifying the refreshed composition metadata.
- 2026-05-22: The page editor composition handoff now renders the connected design-state metrics authors need when building reusable custom frontend sections. The inspector’s `backy.editor-composition-readiness.v1` panel exposes Motion, Data, Assets, and Interactive counts beside layer/group/nested/responsive counts, adds machine-readable `data-animated-layers`, `data-data-bound-layers`, `data-asset-bound-layers`, `data-interactive-layers`, and `data-design-state-layers` attributes, and includes the same metrics in the copyable `backy.editor-composition-action-plan.v1` JSON. The source-only editor smoke guards the rendered panel and aggregate, and admin typecheck plus editor smoke coverage pass for the updated editor page.
- 2026-05-22: The editor inspector composition handoff now includes a copyable `backy.editor-command-registry.v1` command registry for custom admin shells and external builders. The registry enumerates toolbar, inspector, viewport, workflow, and shell commands with stable ids, test ids, shortcuts/ARIA shortcut strings, target scopes, current ready/disabled/hidden state, and human-readable reasons for grouping, ungrouping, clipboard, sibling/child selection, layer state/order, alignment/distribution, grid/snap/pan/zoom, save, publish, settings, and reload actions. The editor source-only guard and smoke-coverage guard fail if the registry, copy action, or critical command ids drift.
- 2026-05-22: The public live-management contract now mirrors that editor command registry for custom frontend builders. `data.modules.liveManagement.editorComposition.commandRegistry`, OpenAPI `x-backy-live-management.editorComposition.commandRegistry`, the `LiveManagementDiscovery` schema, generated SDK manifest types, SDK smoke assertions, and frontend contract source guard expose `backy.editor-command-registry.v1` with stable command ids, test ids, shortcuts, target scopes, state rules, SDK/API helper mappings, editable target paths, and no-secret privacy flags without requiring clients to scrape the admin toolbar.
- 2026-05-22: Editor composition action-layer summaries now count prop-wired actions as first-class custom frontend wiring, not only explicit `actions[]` arrays. Canonical core migrations, the SDK live-management summary mirror, and the page editor metric collector all recognize `href`, `url`, `action`, `actionUrl`, `formId`, `successRedirectUrl`, and `redirectUrl` props, so buttons, links, forms, and redirecting blocks show up in `actionLayers`/`actionElementIds` even when the editor stores the action in the normal element prop bag. Core migration smoke now proves a prop-wired link layer increments action counts, SDK smoke patches a live-managed page title with `props.href` and checks the refreshed prop-action element id, and the editor source guard covers the same action-prop key list.
- 2026-05-22: SDK live-management element helpers now refresh `metadata.editorComposition` locally after add, duplicate, delete, transform, group, ungroup, and patch operations. The SDK mirrors the canonical `backy.editor-composition-summary.v1` shape for top-level and nested content documents before custom builders submit live-managed pages/blog posts back through Backy APIs, so external Wix/Webflow-style editors can trust current group ids, nested layers, responsive overrides, and locked/hidden layer counts immediately after local edits. SDK smoke coverage now asserts group, ungroup, and responsive patch metadata refreshes, and the frontend contract source guard fails if the SDK loses the composition refresh bridge.
- 2026-05-22: Blog/article editor saves now follow the same design-state preservation contract as pages. The post editor extracts stored custom CSS/JS, theme token refs, media assets, interactions/animation timelines, SEO, data bindings, editable maps, and metadata from the saved canvas payload and threads them through `serializeCanvasContent` on every article save while only replacing the edited canvas elements, canvas size, and post settings. The blog editor smoke source guard now fails if this envelope is dropped, and its seeded editor fixture carries a full custom frontend design envelope so future browser smokes exercise realistic article-template state.
- 2026-05-22: Page and blog editor handoff manifests now expose the stored custom frontend design envelope directly instead of only reporting canvas size/template identity. `/pages/:pageId/edit` and `/blog/:postId` build a current `backy.content.v1` document for the edited canvas, then include `backy.custom-frontend-design-envelope.v1` with custom CSS/JS presence and values, content-document identity, theme-token ref counts, asset counts, interaction groups, data-binding/editable-map coverage, metadata, and `metadata.editorComposition` in the copyable handoff JSON. The page editor source guard and blog editor source guard now fail if this custom-frontend handoff loses the design envelope or composition summary.
- 2026-05-22: Central media reference tracking now follows product/custom-CMS collection records in addition to pages and blog posts across both demo-store and Supabase/Postgres repository mode. Database-mode `collections.createRecord`, `updateRecord`, and `deleteRecord` are decorated at repository-runtime creation, so normal collection APIs, public visitor writes, bulk status/delete, CSV record import, full collection backup import, form-routed records, commerce/webhook mutations, and future direct repository callers sync media `metadata.bindings` with `scope: "collectionRecord"` from product values, frontend design assets, content-document assets, and JSON media refs. Public media references/OpenAPI expose `collectionRecordIds`, `collectionRecords`, and `collectionRecordCount`; the Media library source guard counts product/CMS references in placement handoffs, CSV exports, and cleanup warnings; and the focused media-reference smoke proves product design/download refs plus repository-backed product design/document refs are added and pruned.
- 2026-05-22: Supabase/Postgres repository runtime now decorates page and blog mutations with the same central media-reference invariant as collection records. Database-mode page/post create, update, publish, archive, rollback, and delete paths sync or remove media `metadata.pageIds`/`metadata.postIds` from `{ content, meta }` plus post `featuredImageId`, so custom frontend design assets, canonical content-document assets, and featured images stay discoverable in the central file area for both demo-store and durable database runs. The media-reference smoke now behavior-tests repository-backed page and post design/document/featured-image refs as well as stale cleanup.
- 2026-05-22: Central media usage indexing now includes custom frontend design-state asset manifests and canonical content-document asset refs for pages and blog posts, not only canvas element props. Page/post create, update, publish, archive, and rollback now sync media references from `{ content, meta }`, the collector recognizes `assets.media[]`/`assets.fonts[]` document refs by `id`, and `test:media-reference-collector` proves frontendDesignAssets, contentDocument assets, featured images, responsive element refs, and stale-reference cleanup all work. This keeps uploaded assets durable and discoverable when authors reopen or edit custom-designed pages/blogs through Wix-like builders or custom frontend APIs.
- 2026-05-22: Public media list/detail payloads now carry per-asset folder organization metadata for custom frontend media pickers. `toPublicMediaAsset()` adds `backy.media.organization.v1` with folder id/name/path/segments/depth, sort order, root status, and missing-folder status; public media list/detail routes load the folder catalog in demo and repository modes before rendering media assets; OpenAPI and generated SDK types expose the same `organization` contract. This lets custom page builders, font pickers, and storefront asset browsers show the same organized central file structure without making a second folder-tree call for every displayed asset.
- 2026-05-23: The same `backy.media.organization.v1` asset envelope now includes `folderAncestors` with stable folder ids, parent ids, names, and sort order. Public/admin media responses, OpenAPI, SDK types, the central Media placement handoff, and the editor media picker can now preserve enough hierarchy metadata for external Wix/Canva-style builders to rebuild a nested folder tree from selected assets, not only render a display path.
- 2026-05-22: Hosted/public rendering now keeps custom-frontend metadata animations renderable even when a captured template stores only `animation.tokenRefs.duration`/`animation.tokenRefs.easing` and no numeric duration. `PageRenderer` normalizes metadata/direct animation records with a safe default duration before token resolution, passes site `theme.motion` tokens into the shared theme-token compiler, and `test:page-renderer` now asserts a token-only metadata animation hydrates into `data-animation` with the resolved duration/easing. This keeps custom frontend designers from losing motion state when theme tokens are the canonical animation source.
- 2026-05-22: Custom frontend live-management contracts now explicitly guard full custom animation editability, not only motion-token bindings. Manifest/OpenAPI schema requirements, generated SDK contract fixtures, frontend contract source guards, and SDK smoke coverage now require `animation.type`, `animation.from`, and `animation.to` editable targets; the SDK smoke patches a custom GSAP-style `from`/`to` JSON payload plus duration and motion token refs through `patchBackyContentEditableFields()`, and layer descriptors must expose those target paths for external Wix/Canva-style editors. `npm run test:frontend-contract --workspace @backy/public`, `npm run test:generated-types --workspace @backy/sdk-js`, `npm run typecheck --workspace @backy/public`, and `npm run typecheck --workspace @backy/sdk-js` passed on 2026-05-22.
- 2026-05-22: Custom frontend page/blog creation is now discoverable from the same manifest contract as inline editing. `data.modules.liveManagement.lifecycle` exposes `backy.content-lifecycle-commands.v1` with authenticated page/blog lifecycle SDK helpers, required permissions, and the `frontendDesignTemplateId` clone field; `pagesRuntime.managementPolicy` and `blogRuntime.managementPolicy` expose the concrete admin list/create/detail/update/delete, readiness, publish/archive, preview-token, revision, rollback, and template-registry route templates with design-state preservation flags. This closes the gap where external builders could edit existing content through live management but had to already know the admin create routes to add pages/posts from captured frontend templates. The SDK smoke now behavior-tests the blog-post lifecycle too: `createAdminBlogPost()`, `updateAdminBlogPost()`, readiness, preview token creation, publish, archive, revisions, and delete all run against a temporary custom-designed post with custom CSS/JS, responsive media refs, token refs, and animation JSON before cleanup.
- 2026-05-22: `GET /api/admin/settings` now returns `data.settings.completionStatus` with the non-secret `backy.completion-status.v1` audit, remaining Partial surfaces, provider-certification gates, and per-surface runbooks previously available through manifest/OpenAPI. The site-scoped OpenAPI `AdminSettings` schema and generated SDK types now include that completion handoff, and SDK/admin contract smoke guards it so custom Settings/admin consoles can answer "what remains?" directly from the authenticated Settings API without scraping `/settings` or public discovery docs. `/settings` and Settings admin APIs remain **Partial** until credentialed Settings provider certification runs.
- 2026-05-22: Hosted/public page rendering now resolves `animation.tokenRefs.duration` and `animation.tokenRefs.easing` through the same theme token compiler used for style token refs before emitting `data-animation` JSON for GSAP hydration. Motion token changes therefore affect saved page/blog animations even when custom frontends or editor saves keep only the token reference as canonical design state.
- 2026-05-22: Interactive component property controls now normalize registry-declared toggle, color, number, and range values before persisting them into canvas element props and control metadata. This fixes the custom-control edge case where saved string values such as `"false"` rendered as enabled toggles, keeps color inputs on valid hex values, clamps numeric controls to registry bounds, and source-guards the behavior in the editor smoke so custom interactive blocks keep Canva-style controls reliable.
- 2026-05-22: Page and blog creation from connected frontend design templates now preserves the full editable design-state envelope, not only seeded elements and CSS. The shared frontend-design seeding path carries custom JS, content-document metadata, theme-token refs, assets, interactions/animation timelines, data bindings, editable maps, SEO, and template metadata into page/post content plus `meta.frontendDesign*` provenance. Page-create, blog-create, and frontend-contract source guards now cover this so custom frontend editors can reopen newly generated pages/posts without losing animation or editable-map state.
- 2026-05-22: Capturing existing content into the reusable frontend-design template registry now preserves array-based design-state payloads for asset manifests, animation timelines, and interactions instead of only record-shaped metadata. This keeps the `capture-content-template` flow aligned with the page/blog/form/collection/reusable-section/product seeding path, and `test:template-registry` now behavior-tests page, blog, form, collection, reusable-section, product, and collection-record template capture plus seeded new/existing content and public projection normalization so custom frontend templates can round-trip motion and interaction state through the registry before authors clone new pages, posts, forms, collections, reusable blocks, or storefront records from them.
- 2026-05-22: Page and blog revision APIs now attach `backy.content-revision-branch-metadata.v1` to every revision in repository and demo modes. New revision writes also persist first-class `parentRevisionId`, `operation`, `restoreTargetRevisionId`, and `backy.content-revision-metadata.v1` metadata through the core repository contract, database schema, Supabase migration, demo store, admin client, OpenAPI, and SDK types. The branch metadata still carries newest-first position, branch id/label/lane/role, chronological parent/child ids, rollback restore target ids, restore edge ids, source API, and persisted-field provenance, but it now prefers persisted lineage fields before using legacy note/order inference. Custom frontend design envelopes, animations, editable maps, and restore history can be reopened with durable backend lineage metadata instead of browser-only inference.
- 2026-05-22: The SDK/custom-frontend handoff now types that revision branch metadata instead of leaving revision arrays as loose records. `BackyAdminPageRevisionsResponse` and `BackyAdminBlogPostRevisionsResponse` expose `BackyAdminPageRevision[]`/`BackyAdminBlogPostRevision[]` with required `branchMetadata`, SDK runtime smoke asserts the branch metadata on lifecycle page/blog revisions, generated contract fixtures satisfy `BackyContentRevisionBranchMetadata`, and manifest/OpenAPI live-management discovery advertises `backy.admin-page-revisions.v1`, `backy.admin-blog-post-revisions.v1`, and `backy.content-revision-branch-metadata.v1`. External custom editors can now consume restore history through SDK/API contracts, not just the built-in admin app.
- 2026-05-22: Site OpenAPI now also exposes the concrete authenticated revision endpoints and schemas: `listBackyAdminPageRevisions`, `listBackyAdminBlogPostRevisions`, `AdminPageRevision`, `AdminPageRevisionsEnvelope`, `AdminBlogPostRevision`, `AdminBlogPostRevisionsEnvelope`, and shared `ContentRevisionBranchMetadata`. Generated SDK OpenAPI types include those components and the frontend contract smoke guards the operation ids, schema refs, and branch metadata field, so non-SDK custom builders can generate typed restore-history clients directly from `/api/sites/:siteId/openapi`.
- 2026-05-22: Site OpenAPI now completes the typed restore loop by documenting `rollbackBackyAdminPage` and `rollbackBackyAdminBlogPost` plus `AdminPageRollbackRequest` and `AdminBlogPostRollbackRequest`. The live-management lifecycle contract advertises `backy.admin-page-rollback-request.v1` and `backy.admin-blog-post-rollback-request.v1`, and the SDK rollback input types extend the generated OpenAPI request shapes. Custom builders can now generate both revision-read and revision-restore clients from OpenAPI.
- 2026-05-22: Page and blog publish/archive API responses now keep top-level `content.customJS` beside `customCSS` and the canonical `contentDocument`, matching the normal page/blog read/update responses. Custom frontend editors can publish or archive a page/post and immediately reopen the response without losing the easy custom-JS design-state handle while the deeper `backy.content.v1` document remains the source of truth.
- 2026-05-22: The shared page/blog metadata normalizer now carries `frontendDesignAnimations` in addition to assets, interactions, token refs, editable maps, custom JS, content documents, and metadata. Page-create and blog-create smoke fixtures now seed explicit frontend animation timelines and assert they round-trip into `meta.frontendDesignAnimations`, closing an animation provenance gap for custom frontend templates.
- 2026-05-22: Public `GET /api/sites/:siteId/pages` and `GET /api/sites/:siteId/blog` repository-mode payloads now project top-level `content.customJS` beside `customCSS` and `contentDocument`, matching the site OpenAPI `PageResource`/`BlogPostResource` contract and the admin editor responses. Custom frontends can fetch published pages/posts and keep custom script design state without digging through document metadata.
- 2026-05-22: Public page/blog `frontendDesign` summaries now expose the rich captured-template provenance already stored in metadata: custom JS, content-document snapshot, elements, canvas size, theme-token refs, assets, animations, interactions, data bindings, editable maps, SEO, metadata, and binding hints. The site OpenAPI/SDK `ReusableSectionFrontendDesign` shape now names those fields, so custom frontends can reopen published pages/posts with design provenance without scraping raw `meta.frontendDesign*` keys.
- 2026-05-22: Public reusable-section list/detail reads and manifest page/post/section summaries now use the same rich frontend-design provenance helper. Saved blocks created from custom frontend templates expose custom JS, content documents, elements, canvas size, token refs, assets, animations, interactions, data bindings, editable maps, SEO, and template metadata through normalized `frontendDesign`, matching the raw `metadata.frontendDesign*` storage so external editors can reopen reusable sections without flattening animation/design state.
- 2026-05-22: The shared Plate-based editor controls now avoid browser prompt dialogs for color, link, image, and custom font entry. The advanced toolbar, fixed toolbar, selected-text floating toolbar, and right-pane portal toolbar all use the reusable Backy `ColorPicker` popover for selected text color/highlight marks, including accessible dialog/trigger labels, stable popover/custom-hex/clear test ids, keyboard-open support, selection capture, and clear/set mark behavior. Rich-text toolbar icon/menu buttons expose accessible labels and menu expanded state where applicable. Advanced/fixed/floating/portal toolbars use inline URL forms that preserve the active Slate selection before URL-field focus, and `FontDropdown` adds custom Google fonts through an inline form with duplicate prevention and inline load errors. The package smoke `test:toolbar-controls` guards color set/clear mark behavior, prompt-free URL/font controls, preserved selection handoff, and stable test ids, keeping the rich editor closer to Canva-style controls for custom page/blog editing shells.
- 2026-05-22: The central `/media` storage command center now uses an accessible in-app confirmation dialog for non-dry-run Vercel storage secret promotion/revocation instead of a browser `window.confirm`. The dialog names the target mutation, explains the production/preview environment impact, keeps secret values redacted, exposes stable test ids, and is guarded by the media source smoke so credential rotation stays testable for backend storage operations.
- 2026-05-22: `/teams` destructive account/workspace actions now use accessible in-app confirmation dialogs instead of browser `window.confirm`. Team deletion and member removal name the target team/member, explain the persistence boundary, expose stable test ids for smoke automation, and are source-guarded so multi-site workspace/user management keeps the same polished control model as the editor and media library.
- 2026-05-22: `/users` CSV import rollback now uses an accessible in-app confirmation dialog instead of a browser `window.confirm`. The rollback dialog summarizes created/updated/skipped counts, explains that created import users are deleted while updated records are restored, exposes stable test ids, and is guarded by the Users smoke so account recovery workflows stay automatable and clear.
- 2026-05-22: `/users` no longer renders a static "Next controls" backlog panel. The right rail now exposes real access workflow actions for opening selected user detail, inviting users, jumping to delivery settings, managing teams, and reviewing permission matrices, and the Users source smoke guards those controls so the account-management page presents working backend workflows instead of visible roadmap copy.
- 2026-05-22: `/users` membership registration handoff now routes authors to the existing `registration`, `member-login`, and `member-account` page templates instead of showing stale future-work copy. The panel explains the current Forms/Contacts/Collections capture path, seeded member-page shells, signed-download/order-status handoff options, and Supabase/Auth provider gate for credential enforcement, with source guards preventing the old "not complete yet" copy from returning.
- 2026-05-22: `/users` now exposes a copyable `backy.member-access-handoff.v1` custom frontend contract inside the broader users handoff manifest and visible Membership panel. The contract names the registration/member-login/member-account starter routes, public registration form definition/submit APIs, private review APIs, editable page regions, member/profile/preference data bindings, action bindings, Supabase/Auth provider gate, and privacy exclusions for private admin users, raw contacts, sessions, auth secrets, reset tokens, and invite tokens. The Users source smoke guards this so external member-area frontends get a stable handoff without treating private Backy admin users as public members.
- 2026-05-22: `/contacts` now mirrors the member-registration boundary with a copyable `backy.contact-member-capture-handoff.v1` contract inside the lead promotion panel and contact handoff manifest. The contract names registration/member starter routes, public form catalog/definition/submit APIs, private Contacts review/promote APIs, registration identity/consent/member-type bindings, user/profile promotion action bindings, Users/Auth provider gate, and privacy exclusions for raw contact values, private admin users, sessions, auth secrets, invite tokens, and reset tokens. Contacts source smoke guards the contract and removes stale "not wired yet" copy so custom member frontends can treat Contacts as the capture/review layer and Users/Auth as the credentialed-session gate.
- 2026-05-22: `/forms` now exposes a copyable `backy.form-account-registration-handoff.v1` contract inside the registration/account panel and forms handoff manifest. The contract names registration form APIs, registration/member-login/member-account starter templates, Forms/Contacts/Users review endpoints, registration identity/consent/member-type bindings, action bindings, Users/Auth provider gate, and privacy exclusions for raw submission/contact values, private admin users, sessions, auth secrets, invite tokens, and reset tokens. Forms source smoke guards the contract and prevents stale account-roadmap copy from returning, so custom member frontends can treat Forms as the public capture layer and Users/Auth as credential enforcement.
- 2026-05-22: `/settings` now exposes a copyable `backy.media-storage-handoff.v1` contract from the Media storage panel and embeds it into the broader Settings handoff manifest. The contract names storage provider metadata, upload limits, private-file and transform policy, public/admin media endpoint templates, signed private delivery, `backy.media.organization.v1`, `backy.media.references.v1`, `backy.media.editable-metadata.v1`, `MediaDeliveryPolicy`, media file categories, design-state media fields, custom frontend picker use cases, Settings provider certification gate, missing runtime aliases, and the no-secret boundary for service-role keys, S3 keys, signed URL tokens, and private file bytes. Settings source smoke now guards the visible handoff so custom builders can connect central organized media storage without reverse-engineering storage/runtime settings.
- 2026-05-22: `GET /api/admin/settings` now returns `data.settings.mediaStorageHandoff` with the same non-secret `backy.media-storage-handoff.v1` storage/design contract used by the `/settings` UI. Site OpenAPI, generated SDK types, generated SDK fixtures, and admin client types expose the handoff, while source guards cover the Settings route, OpenAPI component, SDK fixture, and admin client bridge. Custom admin shells and frontend builders can now read media/storage policy, design-state asset fields, public/admin media endpoint templates, and privacy boundaries from the authenticated Settings API without scraping the Settings page. `/settings` remains **Partial** only for credentialed live provider certification.
- 2026-05-22: SDK runtime smoke now verifies `adminSettings().data.settings.mediaStorageHandoff` in addition to the generated type fixture. The smoke requires `backy.media-storage-handoff.v1`, the admin signed-url endpoint template, `backy.media.organization.v1`, the `element.props.mediaOrganization` design-state field, and `privacy.includesSecretValues === false`, and `test:settings-admin-contract` source-guards those assertions. Custom admin clients therefore have runtime coverage that the central media/storage design handoff is reachable through the Settings SDK bridge, not only present in OpenAPI.
- 2026-05-22: Site-scoped Settings now mirrors the media/storage handoff at `GET /api/admin/sites/:siteId/settings data.settings.mediaStorageHandoff` with `source: admin-site-settings-api`, the selected site id, template endpoints, resolved site media/font/signed-url endpoints, media organization/reference/editable-metadata contracts, design-state asset fields, and the same no-secret boundary. Site OpenAPI, generated SDK fixtures, admin client types, SDK runtime smoke, and `test:settings-admin-contract` now guard the handoff so multi-site custom admin shells can discover per-site media picker, font manifest, private signed delivery, and responsive transform endpoints without reading global Settings UI state. `/settings` and Settings admin APIs remain **Partial** only for live provider certification.
- 2026-05-22: Editor button and link download actions now use the media picker as a real uploaded-file binding instead of a URL-only field. Selecting or uploading a downloadable file writes `/api/sites/:siteId/media/:mediaId/file?disposition=attachment`, `download: true`, stable `fileMediaId`/file metadata/folder/scope/visibility props, and synchronized `assetIds`; manual URL/page changes clear stale file bindings. Canvas preview, public rendering, core migrations, SDK asset collectors, and repository media-reference sync all recognize `fileMediaId`/`downloadMediaId`, so custom frontend editors can round-trip downloadable files like other media-backed design state.
- 2026-05-22: `/products` digital delivery now uses the media picker as a stable product-file binding instead of URL-only storage. Selecting a central file writes an attachment URL plus `downloadMediaId`, name/type/folder/scope/visibility, and media organization metadata into product values; manual URL edits clear stale media metadata, CSV import/export carries the fields, template-seeded products preserve them, and commerce catalog readiness treats either delivery URL or media id as configured while public storefront handoffs still exclude the private download URL.
- 2026-05-22: `/orders` status-handoff is now represented as a structured site OpenAPI component, `CommerceOrderStatusHandoff`, in addition to the existing `/orders` UI copy action, authenticated status-handoff API, SDK helper, and commerce-management discovery entry. Generated SDK contract types now include the masked order/customer/tracking/refund/action-plan projection and constant privacy flags for raw contact, provider execution ids, payment references, addresses, and internal notes, so custom order-confirmation, tracking, refund, and support widgets can type the customer-safe handoff without importing admin internals. `/orders` remains **Partial** until credentialed live commerce provider certification is run.
- 2026-05-22: Public checkout order creation now returns `data.statusHandoff` using the same `backy.order-status-handoff.v1` customer-safe projection as the authenticated admin status API, with `source: "public-commerce-order-intake-api"` and post-checkout frontend binding metadata. Custom storefront order-confirmation, tracking, refund, and support views can bind to masked order/customer/tracking/refund/digital-delivery/action-plan fields immediately after `POST /api/sites/:siteId/commerce/orders`, while raw customer contact, internal customer ids, checkout sessions, payment references, addresses, internal notes, provider ids/references/payloads, shipping-label ids/URLs, and download URLs/media ids remain excluded. OpenAPI now requires `CommerceOrderEnvelope.data.statusHandoff`, generated SDK types include the public source/auth variants, and `createCommerceOrder()` returns `BackyCommerceOrderStatusHandoff`. `/orders` remains **Partial** until credentialed live commerce provider certification is run.
- 2026-05-22: Public checkout order creation now also returns `data.statusAccess` (`backy.order-status-access.v1`) so custom storefronts can refresh customer order status after the initial checkout response. Backy returns the raw `statusToken` once, persists only `statusaccesstokenhash` plus issued/expiry timestamps on the private order, and verifies `GET /api/sites/:siteId/commerce/orders?orderId=:orderId&statusToken=:statusToken` before returning the same masked `statusHandoff`. Manifest/OpenAPI/SDK contracts now expose the public status endpoint template, `CommerceOrderStatusAccess`, `CommerceOrderStatusHandoffEnvelope`, `post-checkout-status-token` binding mode, and SDK `commerceOrderPublicStatusHandoff(orderId, statusToken)` helper while excluding token hashes, raw contacts, checkout sessions, payment references, addresses, internal notes, provider payloads, shipping-label data, and download URLs/media ids from the customer projection. `/orders` remains **Partial** until credentialed live commerce provider certification is run.
- 2026-05-22: `/orders` status-handoff now includes the authenticated admin order-detail endpoint in the API payload and OpenAPI/SDK contract, matching the rendered `/orders` copy handoff. Custom support consoles can jump from the bounded customer-safe status projection to the private collection-record detail/update route without scraping the admin UI endpoint map, while the privacy boundary still excludes raw order records and provider/customer secrets.
- 2026-05-22: `/orders` customer-status handoff now carries frontend binding metadata for custom confirmation, tracking, refund, and support views. The rendered `/orders` handoff and authenticated status-handoff API include `backy.order-status-frontend-bindings.v1` with safe binding paths, masked customer fields, editable view regions, and action bindings for refresh-status, refresh-tracking, provider-refund, and private order detail workflows. The admin handoff now points quote, shipping-label, fulfillment, tracking, and provider-refund URLs at the real `/api/admin/sites/:siteId/commerce/orders/:orderId/...` operation endpoints instead of private collection-record paths, and OpenAPI/SDK generated-type fixtures guard the binding contract while the privacy flags still exclude raw contact, payment references, provider execution ids, addresses, and internal notes. `/orders` remains **Partial** until credentialed live commerce provider certification is run.
- 2026-05-22: `/orders` status-handoff now includes a customer-safe `backy.order-digital-delivery-handoff.v1` block for digital or downloadable line items. The rendered handoff, authenticated status-handoff API, OpenAPI schema, SDK smoke, and Orders source guard expose digital item counts, configured counts, pending counts, status, customer action copy, and `digitalDelivery.*` frontend binding paths while privacy flags and excluded fields explicitly keep raw `downloadUrl`, `downloadMediaId`, media organization, and delivery payload data out of customer-facing order confirmation/tracking/support views. `/orders` remains **Partial** until credentialed live commerce provider certification is run.
- 2026-05-22: `/products` provider-sync GET/POST responses now include a bounded `backy.product-storefront-handoff.v1` projection for authenticated custom admin clients and SDK consumers. The handoff exposes selected-product launch readiness, storefront endpoint pointers, pricing, inventory, media, merchandising, delivery, subscription, checkout mode, provider-sync booleans, and explicit privacy flags while excluding provider secrets, raw provider responses, private orders, customer payloads, raw checkout sessions, and digital delivery URLs. Public manifest `data.modules.commerceRuntime.managementPolicy` and site OpenAPI `x-backy-commerce-management` now also advertise `responseContracts.productStorefrontHandoff = "backy.product-storefront-handoff.v1"` plus `productStorefrontHandoffExcludesPrivateData`, OpenAPI exports `CommerceProductStorefrontHandoff` for generated clients, and `commerceProductProviderSync(productId)` is typed and smoke-guarded for the new `data.storefrontHandoff` bridge, so external product-page builders no longer need to scrape the `/products` UI copy action. `/products` remains **Partial** until credentialed live commerce provider certification is run.
- 2026-05-22: Product storefront handoff now carries the editable custom frontend design envelope directly. The rendered `/products` copyable `backy.product-storefront-handoff.v1` manifest includes normalized `design` aliases plus raw `frontendDesign*` keys for template id/name, source, binding hints, route pattern, tokens/chrome, custom CSS/JS, content-document snapshot, elements, canvas size, theme-token refs, assets, animations, interactions, data bindings, editable map, SEO, and metadata. The authenticated provider-sync API now returns `design: product.design || null` in the same bounded handoff, and site OpenAPI marks `CommerceProductStorefrontHandoff.design` as `CommerceProductDesign | null`, so custom product-page builders can connect to the selected product’s editable design state without scraping collection values or making a separate catalog call. Source-only commerce smoke guards the UI handoff, provider-sync API handoff, and OpenAPI schema while preserving privacy exclusions for provider secrets, raw provider responses, private orders, customer payloads, checkout sessions, and digital delivery URLs.
- 2026-05-22: The SDK product provider-sync bridge now types `BackyCommerceProductStorefrontHandoff.design` as `BackyCommerceProductDesign | null` and the SDK smoke creates a custom-designed product, reads `commerceProductProviderSync(productId)`, and verifies the returned storefront handoff preserves custom JS, content-document media refs, animation timelines, and editable-map paths. The source-only commerce guard now covers the SDK type and smoke assertions so external product-page builders can trust the provider-sync handoff as an editable product-template API, not just a storefront-safe launch checklist. `/products` remains **Partial** only for credentialed live provider certification.
- 2026-05-22: `/settings` notification controls now persist the previously disabled email and in-app preferences for new-user registration, page-published, system-update, team-mention, and daily/weekly digest settings through both the rendered Settings UI and direct `PATCH /api/admin/settings` contract. The Settings smoke source guard checks that those channels are active controls, and the full browser/API smoke verifies daily digest persistence from the UI plus weekly digest, `newUser`, `pagePublished`, `systemUpdates`, and `mentions` persistence through the Settings API. The row remains **Partial** until the external Settings provider certification workflow proves live notification, storage, Vercel, Supabase, and nested commerce provider accounts.
- 2026-05-21: `/products` now includes a copyable `backy.commerce-provider-certification-evidence-packet.v1` operator attachment manifest for selected live-provider families, including required input aliases, target/admin-key inputs, capture sources, scenario attachments, and a redaction policy that excludes webhook bodies. The surface remains **Partial** until the external live-provider TaxJar/Avalara/EasyPost/Shippo/Stripe promotion-code and deeper provider-managed lifecycle certifications are actually run.

## Product-level gates

Backy should not be called complete until these gates are satisfied:

- **Durable backend**: sites, pages, posts, users, settings, media, forms, comments, events, and publish state are stored in the same durable backend, not split between seed data, localStorage, and local JSON files.
- **Admin APIs**: every admin page has authenticated CRUD endpoints with validation, pagination, RBAC, audit trails, and consistent `{ success, requestId, data/error }` envelopes.
- **Public render contract**: custom frontends can fetch exact site/page/post data, theme tokens, assets, actions, bindings, SEO, and editable maps without relying on admin implementation details.
- **Editor parity**: page/post editing covers Canva/Wix expectations: drag/resize, selection, layers, snapping/alignment, grouping, undo/redo, responsive breakpoints, media/font insertion, template sections, preview, publish, and revision history.
- **Interactive component platform**: pages/posts/blog articles can author trusted interactive blocks, custom figures, code-driven animations, simulations, and fully custom code components through a registry or sandboxed iframe runtime with CSP, permission scopes, versioning, allowed data bindings, fallback render states, and public API contracts. Free-form script boxes are not acceptable for production.
- **WordPress parity**: pages/posts/media/users/settings support drafts, publish/schedule, slugs/permalinks, SEO, revisions, categories/tags where relevant, comments/moderation, forms, roles, and export/import hooks.
- **Operational readiness**: auth, permissions, rate limits, storage quotas, telemetry, backups, webhook retries, tests, and deployment configuration are documented and verified.

| ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `/login`                    | Ready   | `apps/public/src/app/api/admin/auth/login/route.ts`, `/session`, `/logout`, `/password-recovery`, `/accept-invite`, and `/reset-password` now provide backend-backed admin authentication. Login first attempts configured Supabase Auth password verification from `BACKY_SUPABASE_URL`/`BACKY_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` or aliases, maps the verified email to an existing active Backy admin user, and issues a Backy `authMode: "supabase"` admin session without enabling local demo auth in production; local-demo credentials remain available only when the production local-auth policy allows them. When Settings enables `auth.requireTwoFactor`, successful primary auth now requires a configured `BACKY_ADMIN_MFA_CODE`/`BACKY_ADMIN_2FA_CODE` static verifier or `BACKY_ADMIN_MFA_TOTP_SECRET`/`BACKY_ADMIN_2FA_TOTP_SECRET` TOTP secret before issuing the Backy session, and `/login` reveals a one-time-code field after the API returns `MFA_REQUIRED`; rejected MFA attempts revoke the tentative server session before returning. Successful login, invite acceptance, password reset acceptance, and session refresh now set a `backy_admin_session` httpOnly/SameSite=Lax cookie; session validation, admin access checks, session listing current-session detection, current-session token rotation, and logout accept the cookie while preserving bearer-token compatibility, and logout clears the cookie. Login success, MFA-required, MFA-invalid, MFA-provider-missing, logout, admin-session-rotate, and admin-session-revoke actions now record queryable `auth.*` admin audit events against the Settings security surface, and the Settings audit trail renders readable auth event titles/descriptions plus an auth/API-key/settings filter, summary counts, and detail payload drawer. The Settings security tab can rotate the current browser session, revoke the previous token, refresh the cookie-backed client session, and show the rotation in the auth audit trail; the user detail page lists active admin sessions with current-session protection, last-seen/issued/expires timestamps, and revocation controls. User detail now also exposes a permission-gated per-user MFA panel backed by `GET/PATCH /api/admin/users/:userId/mfa`, persists enrollment and hashed one-time recovery codes in Settings auth metadata, records `user.mfa.update` and `user.mfa.recovery_codes.rotate` audit events, and lets `/api/admin/auth/login` satisfy a user-specific MFA challenge by consuming exactly one recovery code. `apps/admin/src/stores/authStore.ts` signs in through the admin auth API, accepts invite tokens, resets local-demo passwords, persists only user plus session metadata without a readable token, validates and rotates sessions through the httpOnly cookie, scrubs old persisted tokens on rehydrate, and revokes the session on logout. `apps/admin/src/lib/adminAuthApi.ts`, `apps/admin/src/lib/adminContentApi.ts`, and `apps/admin/src/lib/mediaApi.ts` now issue credentialed cookie-first admin requests without reading the session token from localStorage, while `apps/public/src/proxy.ts` allows credentialed CORS for the admin origins. Non-auth admin mutation routes are guarded by server-side `requireAdminAccess` checks, and `npm run test:admin-rbac-coverage --workspace @backy/public` statically verifies every non-auth mutating admin API route calls the access gate, currently covering 94 mutating route files and 118 mutating handlers while excluding only auth lifecycle endpoints that authenticate through invite/reset/session/login tokens; it also source-checks the workspace Settings permission split and the site-scoped Settings `sites.view`/`sites.configure` contract. Login, password recovery, and password reset now share hashed auth rate-limit buckets persisted under Settings auth metadata with separate principal/client limits and retry-after responses; successful login clears prior failed-login counters for that email/client, and password recovery returns a neutral public delivery envelope for known and unknown accounts. The login page now requests password recovery through the backend auth API instead of checking local hard-coded users in the browser, queues password-reset email through Backy's shared transactional provider stack with provider/status metadata while keeping account lookup responses neutral, `/accept-invite?token=...` is a public route that activates pending invited users and starts a session, and `/reset-password?token=...` lets admins set a new local-demo password from a generated reset token. `npm run test:login --workspace @backy-cms/admin` covers invalid credentials, API-backed local recovery with queued reset delivery metadata, successful sign-in, httpOnly cookie issuance, cookie-only session validation, cookie logout/revocation, auth audit readback for login/logout, tokenless persisted session metadata, and dashboard redirect. `npm run test:admin-auth --workspace @backy/public` covers admin-key acceptance/rejection, owner-only key denial, repeated invalid-login rate limiting, neutral known/unknown password-recovery responses, recovery rate limiting, reset password policy, email-domain policy, and invite-only policy. `npm run test:admin-auth-supabase --workspace @backy/public` covers Supabase Auth credential exchange configuration, invalid credential rejection, and provider-unavailable errors; `npm run test:admin-auth-supabase-route --workspace @backy/public` starts a mocked Supabase Auth service plus a temporary public Next server and verifies `/api/admin/auth/login` maps Supabase-verified `admin@backy.io` credentials into an httpOnly-cookie Backy `authMode: "supabase"` session only after missing and invalid MFA codes are rejected without leaving active sessions. `npm run test:settings --workspace @backy-cms/admin` covers the now-enabled Settings two-factor toggle, persisted `auth.requireTwoFactor`, current-session rotation, previous-token rejection, auth audit filtering, and auth audit detail payload rendering. `npm run test:users --workspace @backy-cms/admin` now covers generated invite-link acceptance, user activation, returned session, generated reset-link acceptance, new-password login, active local-demo session history, protected current-session rendering, per-user MFA enablement, one-time recovery-code generation/copy, MFA-required login rejection, recovery-code login success, recovery-code reuse rejection, consumed-code count readback, and `user.invite.accept`/`user.password_reset.accept`/`user.mfa.update`/`user.mfa.recovery_codes.rotate` audit logging. Root admin site list, detail, create, update, and delete routes now apply team-scoped authorization on top of role/permission checks: non-owner sessions only see team-owned sites when they are members, and create/update/delete require owner/admin membership in the target team while admin API keys remain service-level bypasses. `requireAdminAccess` now also resolves `/api/admin/sites/:siteId/...` requests and enforces the same team-owned site gate centrally for nested pages, blog, media, collections, commerce, forms, comments, SEO, navigation, redirects, reusable sections, and frontend-design routes before route handlers run. `npm run test:admin-site-scope --workspace @backy/public` creates a temporary team-owned site plus non-member admin session and verifies list filtering, detail/update `FORBIDDEN_SITE_SCOPE` denial, nested pages list/create denial, and representative nested media, collections, forms, navigation, frontend-design, blog, SEO, redirects, reusable-sections, interactive-components, readiness, duplicate, settings, and commerce denials before cleanup. `npm run test:admin-site-scope:repository --workspace @backy/public` injects database-mode repositories into the real `requireAdminAccess` path and verifies non-member denial, viewer read-only allowance, viewer content-write denial, and editor content-write allowance for nested site routes by id and slug.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | Current login/auth scope is complete; broader product completion remains tracked under the remaining Partial page and API rows. | `/` dashboard               | Ready   | `apps/admin/src/routes/index.tsx` now loads sites, pages, blog posts, users, media, runtime storage/database/Supabase/Vercel settings, site readiness, recent admin audit logs, and commerce product/order record counts from backend APIs, with local fallback state if the admin API fails and user-directory access degrades independently if that protected endpoint is unavailable. It shows refresh/loading/fallback states, backend health, publishing blockers/warnings, quick creation paths, launch-ready workflow links, module map, API handoff, custom frontend setup links, request-id-backed activity, a launch onboarding checklist with setup progress and next-action links, an operations signal board for commerce catalog/orders, comment/form moderation, and workflow failure/readiness alerts, aggregate analytics for publishing mix, recent audit velocity, lead/comment pressure, and commerce totals, a dashboard-run infrastructure diagnostic panel that reuses the Settings validator for database, media storage, Supabase, and Vercel readiness, and a deployment preflight execution/history panel that records session runs with target URL, request id, and ready/warning/blocked counts. Dashboard data loading and rendered actions are filtered by the signed-in admin permission matrix or role defaults, including privileged users/settings/activity/admin-endpoint surfaces and settings-backed infrastructure/deployment checks. The dashboard design now follows `DESIGN.md` with semantic status tokens and a denser control-room layout. `npm run test:dashboard --workspace @backy-cms/admin` now covers API-backed temporary site creation using a real local-demo admin session, rendered command center/site selector/stats/onboarding/RBAC-scope/deployment-history/operations-signal/aggregate-analytics/readiness/infrastructure diagnostics/workflows/attention/activity/API/module regions, active API site switching with scoped endpoint text, critical builder/navigation links, deployment preflight execution/history rendering, infrastructure check execution, refresh behavior, desktop and mobile rendered visual regression checks for region geometry/control sizing/overflow/runtime overlays, and a temporary viewer-session RBAC pass that verifies users/settings/admin endpoints, privileged creation actions, privileged module cards, and settings-backed checks are hidden or disabled. | `/sites`                    | Ready   | `apps/admin/src/routes/sites.tsx` now loads and deletes through `GET/DELETE /api/admin/sites`, with local fallback state. The multi-site workspace includes command/readiness/RBAC/API/domain-verification/Vercel-deployment/billing-quota/feature-contract/audit/library regions, search plus status/domain/page-coverage filters, CSV/frontend-contract export, per-site publish status editing, selected-site DNS TXT/CNAME verification state, selected-site Vercel preview/production/rollback deployment handoff state, selected-site billing plan and quota usage state, duplicate-as-draft, quick archive, preview/manage links, and typed delete confirmation. The page loads the signed-in user's permission matrix and gates site creation, status/configuration, domain verification, deployment workflow actions, billing/quota changes, duplicate, archive/delete, CSV export, and audit activity visibility by `sites.*`, `settings.billing`, and `activity.export` capabilities. Site create/update/delete APIs now emit queryable `site.created`, `site.updated`, and `site.deleted` admin audit events, and the Sites page renders a request-id-backed Site audit trail panel. Site creation and site duplication now enforce the Settings Commerce billing site limit when overage mode is `block`, returning `BILLING_SITE_LIMIT` before persistence once the workspace reaches the configured site count. `npm run test:sites --workspace @backy-cms/admin` creates a temporary owner and viewer account, temporarily sets Settings billing to block at the current site count and verifies `POST /api/admin/sites` and `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/duplicate` reject over-limit creation without persisting a site, creates a temporary site after restoring billing settings, verifies the rendered multi-site command/RBAC/API/domain-verification/Vercel-deployment/billing-quota/feature-contract/audit/library surfaces, persists pending and verified domain verification state through the admin site API, persists Vercel preview queued, preview ready, and production ready deployment state plus run history through the admin site API, persists billing plan changes and quota usage snapshots through the admin site API, publishes it through the status control, exercises search/domain/page/status filters, duplicates it as a draft without carrying the custom domain, archives the original, verifies each mutation and site audit events through the admin API, verifies the viewer session cannot create/configure/verify domains/run deploy workflow actions/change billing quotas/duplicate/archive/delete or view audit activity from the rendered Sites UI, confirms typed deletion as owner, checks layout overflow, captures a screenshot, and cleans up. `npm run test:repositories --workspace @backy/db` now verifies repository-mode site settings JSON persists and reads back domain verification, Vercel deployment history, and billing quota state through the DB site repository and backing DB row. A fresh `/sites` readiness audit reran both suites successfully after the domain, deploy, billing, RBAC, audit, and DB persistence slices landed. | `/sites/$siteId`            | Ready   | `apps/admin/src/routes/sites.$siteId.tsx` now saves/deletes site settings through `PATCH/DELETE /api/admin/sites/:siteId`; forms/comments moderation sections are backend-connected. Form submissions that generated collection draft records now show a content shortcut into the Collections admin surface. The page now loads site pages and stored navigation through admin APIs, then lets admins add page/internal/external primary/footer menu items, nest children, reorder root and nested sibling links with move controls or drag handles/drop zones, toggle visibility, and save through `PATCH /api/admin/sites/:siteId/navigation`. It also loads/saves exact redirect and 410 gone rules through `GET/PATCH /api/admin/sites/:siteId/redirects`, can preview unsaved redirect route warnings through `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/redirects`, and displays source-shadowing or missing-target diagnostics before/after save. Site SEO defaults, JSON-LD defaults, sitemap settings, robots settings, and route-level SEO overrides persist through `GET/PATCH /api/admin/sites/:siteId/seo`; public SEO discovery now applies route overrides for canonical, title, description, Open Graph image, keywords, robots, priority, and change frequency before sitemap/robots output. The site workspace now includes a domain verification panel for saved custom domains, exposes TXT host/value, CNAME target, token, requested/checked/verified timestamps, and persists pending/verified DNS state through the admin site API. The site workspace also includes a theme/publish panel for brand colors, typography, spacing, custom CSS, public token preview, and status delivery state, persisting `theme` plus `settings.siteStatus` through the admin site API. Site-level webhook configuration is now typed under `settings.webhooks`, rendered in the site workspace with enablement, endpoint URL/name, event allowlist, secret reference, and headers controls, persists through the admin site API, and emits `site.webhooks.updated` audit metadata. The workspace now includes a compact standalone form builder that creates site forms, edits identity/delivery/moderation/contact-share/field contracts, reorders fields, persists through the admin forms APIs, and emits form audit rows. Site navigation, redirect, SEO, domain-verification, theme/publish, webhooks, form create/update/delete, comment-policy, frontend-design, create/update/delete mutations now emit site-scoped admin audit logs, and the site workspace renders a `Site activity` audit panel backed by `/api/admin/audit-logs?siteId=...` with request IDs and mutation metadata. `npm run test:site-detail --workspace @backy-cms/admin` now creates a temporary site and SEO target page, opens its workspace in authenticated headless Chrome, verifies command/readiness/domain/theme/webhooks/form-builder/navigation/redirect/SEO/settings/activity/automation/handoff regions, prepares and verifies the rendered domain DNS state, saves rendered theme/publish controls, saves rendered webhook configuration, creates and saves a rendered standalone site form, adds primary/footer navigation plus child routes through the rendered editors, verifies | `/pages`                    | Ready   | `apps/admin/src/routes/pages.tsx` now loads and deletes through `GET/DELETE /api/admin/sites/:siteId/pages`, with local fallback state. The list now has an active-site selector, status filters for all/published/draft/scheduled/archived pages, readiness health pulled from site readiness, first readiness issue text for route/SEO/layout diagnostics, a blocked-health metric filter, refresh control, preview action, published-page link action, search-aware row selection, and bulk publish/archive/delete actions through backend APIs. Empty states distinguish an empty site from filtered results, every create entry point routes through link-backed `/pages/new` navigation with the active `siteId`, and the empty-site action now uses the same generic `New Page` flow instead of a separate homepage starter. The table, CSV export, and handoff JSON now expose route diagnostics, delivery diagnostics, page hierarchy, and navigation placement context: page rows show route availability/conflict state, duplicate/reserved/invalid routes and collection list/item route shadows block publish and bulk publish, delivery rows show published/preview-only/scheduled/archived/blocked state with public/render/resolve/preview endpoint context plus public delivery health probes for published pages, top-level pages show child counts, child pages show their parent title/route, and page rows include navigation placement/label metadata for custom frontend handoff. Single-page and bulk publish actions now open review dialogs before mutating backend state, showing route, readiness, delivery, public/render/resolve, and preview endpoint context while still rechecking readiness before publish. Page rows also load revision summaries from `/api/admin/sites/:siteId/pages/:pageId/revisions`, showing revision count, latest note/date/status, and a direct history link into the editor; CSV and handoff JSON include route, delivery, delivery-health, delivery-health history, and revision summary fields. Published-page delivery health can be refreshed from the command center or directly from each published row, and each row now shows a recent-probes trail with timestamp, status, and public/render/resolve HTTP results. Browser QA covers Settings Commerce block-mode page quota enforcement, empty-site controls, blocked filter activation, clear-filter behavior, empty-state/new-page navigation, a temporary published row with health plus preview/edit/delete/published actions, and temporary search-filtered rows through bulk select-visible, publish, archive, and delete. `npm run test:pages-list --workspace command-center registration shortcut, temporary parent/child hierarchy rendering, a temporary backend revision surfaced in the list, row route availability diagnostics, row delivery diagnostics, published-page delivery health display, refresh control plus recent-probe history, the single-page publish review modal, the bulk-publish review modal, the confirmed bulk-publish mutation path by publishing a temporary draft page with `expectedUpdatedAt`, verifies the success notice/table row state, clears selected bulk UI state, reads the published status back from the admin page detail API, | `/pages/new`                | Ready   | `apps/admin/src/routes/pages.new.tsx` now creates through `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/pages`, with local fallback state, draft/published/scheduled status controls including `scheduledAt`, real starter canvas templates for landing/storefront/blog-index/about/contact/registration pages, editable header/navigation/footer chrome for non-blank templates, route conflict checks against the selected site's page library, a creation handoff manifest, and immediate transition into the visual editor after persistence. When opened with `collectionId` and `datasetMode`, it loads the selected collection, renders a dataset import panel, includes collection field mappings in the handoff manifest, and seeds collection-backed list pages with a repeater or item pages with bound title/summary elements. The create route includes site-aware navigation placement controls for no menu, primary menu, or footer menu, persists those choices in route search/meta preview, and patches site navigation after the page record is created. It also supports parent page placement: selected parents are saved as `parentId` plus `meta.parentPageId`/`meta.parentPageTitle`, homepage routes stay top-level, and primary/footer navigation nests the created page under the chosen parent. It has local autosave/recovery for unsaved title, slug, target site, template, status/schedule, homepage flag, parent page, SEO description, search title, canonical path, Open Graph image URL, robots flags, and navigation placement/label, with restore/discard actions and autosave status in the command center; successful create clears the local draft. The page now includes SEO/social controls and persists `meta.title`, `meta.description`, `meta.canonical`, `meta.keywords`, `meta.jsonLd`, `meta.ogImage`, `meta.noIndex`, and `meta.noFollow` so public render payloads and custom frontends receive route metadata immediately after creation. Keywords and JSON-LD can be supplied directly or generated from the selected template defaults. Template selection now includes reusable visual wireframe previews for every starter type plus a selected-template canvas summary for chrome/section expectations before create. The editor route retries freshly-created backend page loads before falling back to local state, so a successful create opens the backend-backed editor without a misleading local-copy warning. `npm run
