Text styling controls can now open the media picker to upload/select font files for the selected element. Backend preview tokens allow draft hosted page, render, and page API access, and page preview-token creation now records a redacted `previewToken.create` audit event with target, slug, TTL, expiry, and tokenless hosted/render/API path metadata. Editor layers can now select, root-reorder, move up/down in sibling order, move nested layers out to the parent while preserving visual position, move selected layers into child-accepting layers, hide/show, lock/unlock, duplicate, and delete canvas elements through persisted element fields. Canvas drag/resize now uses ref-backed transient transforms with one undo history commit on release, selected elements support arrow-key nudging, multi-layer drag, proportional multi-layer resize/scale from corner handles, toolbar align-left/center/right/top/middle/bottom commands, and multi-layer horizontal/vertical spacing distribution, the design surface has scale-aware zoom/fit controls, dragged elements avoid accidental text selection, and smart guide lines snap dragged elements to canvas/element edges and centers. Canvas elements also support shift/command/control-click multi-select from the design surface for grouping and layer operations. The component library now supports composed nested presets and includes hero, feature-grid, lead-capture form, HTML, and table blocks that remain normal editable canvas trees/elements after drop. The editor library also loads active API-backed reusable sections for the active site, can save the selected element tree as a reusable section, can rename/delete saved sections, can insert saved sections as synced canvas instances with source metadata, can refresh a selected synced instance from the saved source, can detach it into an independent editable copy, and still stores concrete canvas trees for public rendering. The shared canvas editor now supports desktop/tablet/mobile breakpoint editing by merging active breakpoint layout/content/style and layer visibility/lock changes into `element.responsive` overrides while keeping desktop base state intact; the inspector exposes an active breakpoint override card with reset-all plus group-level layout/layer/content/style reset controls that show which groups inherit desktop, and the public renderer applies mobile/tablet/desktop overrides from the rendered container width. `npm run test:editor-drag --workspace @backy-cms/admin` now creates a temporary backend page and smoke-tests authenticated editor load, heading/image/container/nested-child/form dragging, image/form resizing, Shift+Arrow keyboard nudging, Ctrl+Z/Ctrl+Shift+Z drag undo/redo plus non-drag undo/redo for inspector layout edits, keyboard nudges, and layer visibility toggles, selected text inspector font-media picker presence, `Ctrl/Cmd+A` sibling selection, three-layer spacing distribution, multi-layer resize/scale with persisted reload hydration, `Ctrl/Cmd+G` grouping, `Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+G` ungrouping with position-preserving roundtrip, layer-tree nest/outdent controls with position-preserving coordinate conversion, synced reusable-section insertion/source refresh/detach, invalid numeric input warning guards, backend save persistence, mobile and tablet breakpoint layout plus layer visibility/lock override persistence, group-level reset/inheritance controls, desktop/mobile/tablet switch hydration, and fresh editor reload rehydration in headless Chrome. | `/blog/new`                 | Ready   | `apps/admin/src/routes/blog.new.tsx` now creates through `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/blog`, with local fallback state, backend-loaded author/category/tag assignment controls, scheduled publishing input, shared `Panel`/`Notice`/`Button` controls, and the reusable `EditorWorkspaceFrame` around the same page editor canvas used for pages. The creation workspace has been reorganized into an editor-first layout with a wider canvas, editorial draft panel, sticky publishing rail, site-aware taxonomy/author controls, segmented draft/published/scheduled actions, readiness checks for title/slug/summary/SEO/featured-media/design/schedule, and taxonomy chips instead of cramped checkbox stacks. It now supports post-scoped featured image selection through the shared media library, persists `featuredImageId`, includes selected media in the create payload and frontend handoff manifest, and uses the selected image as the Open Graph fallback. It also includes route-level SEO creation controls for search title, canonical path, search description, Open Graph image URL, noindex, and nofollow, and persists those values into post meta. The create surface now has a real `Save draft and preview` workflow: it persists a draft, requests the existing backend preview-token endpoint, opens the hosted/API preview URL, and transitions into the blog edit page for revision/publish work. It also has local autosave/recovery for unsaved title, slug, excerpt, publishing status, SEO fields, featured media, taxonomy, author, canvas elements, and canvas size, with restore/discard actions and autosave status in the command center. The route now supports a canvas focus mode that hides draft/SEO/taxonomy/publish panels, preserves `focus=canvas` in search state, expands the editor frame for page-design work, and keeps compact save-draft-preview, publish, and show-panels controls available above the canvas. A writing-structure panel now adds editable article sections and pull quotes into the canvas while showing word-count and reading-time planning metrics, and those inserted long-form blocks persist through autosave and created post content. Browser QA covers rendering the workspace, the scheduled date control, expanded canvas height, readiness enabling save, and creating then cleaning up a draft post through the UI/API. `npm run test:blog-create --workspace @backy-cms/admin` covers the new create workspace render, desktop and focus-mode visual-state guards for visible command/workspace/canvas/editor side panels, no framework overlay, no horizontal overflow, breakpoint/grouping controls, frontend template handoff, focus-mode panel hiding, and screenshot artifacts, mobile breakpoint authoring for the frontend-template heading with desktop-layout preservation plus autosave/final-post mobile override persistence, writing-structure controls with section/quote insertion plus autosave/final-post persistence, canvas focus/restore behavior, featured media picker, SEO controls, autosave localStorage persistence, recovery restore, and save-draft-and-preview flow against the live admin/public dev servers. The embedded editor toolbar is now grouped into stable control clusters, scrolls instead of clipping in constrained widths, and supports `Cmd/Ctrl+G` plus `Shift+Cmd/Ctrl+G` for grouping/ungrouping selected layers. `npm run typecheck --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `git diff --check`, and `npm run build --workspace @backy-cms/admin` passed for the featured media, SEO, create-preview, autosave-recovery, browser-smoke, and canvas-focus slices. Backend preview tokens allow draft hosted post and post API access. The edit workspace verifies slug availability before save/publish, persists route-level SEO metadata including canonical path, Open Graph image, robots flags, and search title/description, supports featured image selection through the shared post-scoped media library, includes the image and SEO contract in frontend handoff JSON, and surfaces post-specific comment moderation state with pending/approved/flagged counts plus quick approve/reject actions against the comments API. `npm run typecheck --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `git diff --check`, and `npm run build --workspace @backy-cms/admin` passed for the route, SEO, featured media, and comment moderation slices. | `/collections`              | Ready   | `apps/admin/src/routes/collections.tsx` now provides a backend-backed collection builder for the active site. It lists collections, creates/updates/deletes collection schemas, edits field keys/labels/types/required/unique flags, edits select/tag options and reference targets, toggles public-read and visitor-create/update/delete permissions, configures the collection-scoped public write token required by public update/delete routes, saves visitor create/update selected-field allowlists in collection metadata, saves metadata-backed generated list/item template presets with title/summary/image/detail field roles for fallback dynamic routes, can attach a captured frontend collection template to an existing schema for custom list/item route rendering, creates/updates/deletes records, filters/searches/sorts paginated record lists through the admin API, renders backend validation details, runs selected-record bulk status/delete actions, imports CSV rows through backend schema validation/upsert, exports the filtered record set as backend-generated CSV, exports portable JSON collection backups through `GET /api/admin/sites/:siteId/collections/export`, imports/restores those schema-plus-record backups through `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/collections/import?upsert=true`, and links published records to generated `/:collectionSlug/:recordSlug` hosted routes. Collection schema mutations, individual record mutations, selected-record bulk status/delete mutations, and backup imports now emit request-id-backed audit logs readable from `/api/admin/audit-logs`. Collection schema creation now enforces the selected site's Settings Commerce billing collection limit when overage mode is `block`, returning `BILLING_COLLECTION_LIMIT` before persistence once the site reaches its configured schema count. The Collections surface also loads the current admin permission matrix, renders an allowed/blocked permission contract for `collections.view`, `collections.edit`, `collections.export`, and `collections.delete`, gates schema/record/import/export/delete controls by those permissions, shows recent request-id-backed collection schema and record mutation audit events from `/api/admin/audit-logs`, includes a visual relationship browser for outgoing reference fields, incoming reverse references, linked schema counts, unmapped relationship warnings, and route hints, and exposes dataset authoring shortcuts for the active collection that copy a repeater preset, field-binding preset, list-page brief, item-page brief, and list/item page-builder handoff routes into `/pages/new` with `collectionId` and `datasetMode`. The shared editor property panel now lets selected elements bind to collection fields with reusable title/summary/image/link binding presets, site-scoped shared saved preset management backed by `/api/admin/sites/:siteId/editor/collection-binding-presets` with local fallback, reference-field join controls, a selected-record value preview with image thumbnails plus resolved joined values, recent-record picker, manual record id/slug override, target-path selection, search/filter/sort/pagination controls, and persisted binding manifests; repeater elements expose collection, dataset id, join-aware field mapping, joined query filter/sort controls, current-record reference filter controls for reverse relationships, query-backed matching-record previews with joined title/summary/image resolution, and layout controls. `npm run test:collections --workspace @backy-cms/admin` now temporarily sets Settings Commerce to block mode at the active site's current collection count, verifies `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/collections` returns `BILLING_COLLECTION_LIMIT` without persisting a schema, creates temporary public-readable dynamic collections with outgoing and incoming references, loads `/collections` in authenticated headless Chrome, verifies the command center, templates, API contract, frontend contract, binding contract, dataset authoring shortcuts with list/item builder buttons, permission contract, relationship browser, captured frontend template selector, schema-and-record audit activity panel, schema builder, records table, field controls, route links, record selection controls, visitor update/delete policy controls, public write-token persistence, selected update-field persistence, authored-template compare controls and diff summaries, and New collection reset behavior, exercises the rendered repeater-preset copy action, attaches a captured frontend collection template to the selected schema and verifies persisted frontend template metadata, publishes the selected record through the rendered bulk action, confirms the bulk record audit event appears in the rendered activity panel, confirms admin and public record APIs expose the published record, captures a screenshot, and cleans up the temporary schemas. `BACKY_EDITOR_COMPONENT_SMOKE=data-binding npm run test:editor-drag --workspace @backy-cms/admin` verifies the canvas data panel loads collection field/query options, exposes title/summary/image binding presets, configures a joined reference binding such as `author.company.name`, can save/apply/delete a site-shared binding preset through the backend preset API, shows selected record preview values plus thumbnail image, exposes created records in the picker, applies a selected record to the binding, saves it, and confirms the persisted canvas binding includes dataset id, target path, source collection/field/path/record, query filter/sort, and pagination. `BACKY_EDITOR_COMPONENT_SMOKE=repeater npm run test:editor-drag --workspace @backy-cms/admin` verifies repeater dataset controls expose reference join field paths such as `author.company.name`, can set a `$currentRecord.id` reference filter for reverse relationship repeaters, render current-record preview guidance, show a query-backed matching-record preview with joined title value, summary, total count, and image, persist the joined title mapping, and reload cleanly. `npm run test:admin-contract --workspace @backy/public` verifies collection schema audit readback, individual and bulk collection record mutation audit readback, collection backup export/import with restored-record readback and import audit metadata, editor-session `collections.view` access, editor denial for `collections.export` and `collections.delete`, explicit `collections.edit` deny override enforcement, render payload/hosted page resolution for collection reference joins, joined repeater record hydration through `record.values["author.company.name"]`, dynamic item reverse-relationship repeater hydration by resolving `$currentRecord.id` to the active record id, and public create/update/delete policy enforcement with collection-scoped write tokens and selected update-field allowlists. Database-mode collection record reads now push status, search, field filter, sort, count, limit, and offset into repository queries with composite indexes and repository smoke coverage that verifies full pagination totals while materializing only the requested page. Drizzle schema and Supabase migrations now add JSONB containment, trigram search, and published-feed indexes for field-heavy custom record values. Public generated collection list/item renderers now consume saved dynamic-template metadata when no captured frontend-design template is attached, preserving the full canvas-template path as the higher-priority route model. The Collections surface now also captures Backy-authored page canvases as list/item dynamic templates, and the collection smoke verifies those authored roots and collection/record bindings render through public dynamic routes. It also exposes a visitor-create field policy that stores selected public-writable fields in collection metadata, and the public create API drops disallowed submitted fields before validation/storage. The dynamic template panel now includes a preview-record selector with hosted and render API URLs for list/item routes, bounded authored-template capture history with restore controls, and active-versus-history compare panels that summarize root element, source page, canvas size, and custom CSS changes before restore/save decisions. | `/products`                 | Partial | `apps/admin/src/routes/products.tsx` now exposes a backend-backed commerce catalog workspace for the active site: product schema setup/sync, public-read catalog permissions, readiness checks, public storefront/API handoff manifest, CSV export/import/template controls, filters, product CRUD, variants, option-matrix generation, pricing, inventory policy, delivery URL, checkout URL, subscription enabled/interval/trial metadata, gallery/media fields, category/tags/vendor merchandising, SEO title, publish status, and product editor state. Collection record CSV import now preserves typed number, boolean, tags/multi-reference, and JSON field values so imported catalog data is usable by storefront APIs in demo and database-backed modes, including subscription metadata. Public catalog responses now include a non-secret commerce storefront contract derived from Settings so frontends can read catalog/order-intake availability, default currency, payment-provider handoff mode, provider test/live mode, checkout redirect paths, tax/shipping/discount flags, configurable tax/shipping/discount quote rules, inventory reservation settings, webhook endpoint readiness, webhook event allowlist, and reconciliation mode/window without using admin APIs or leaking webhook secret references; product payloads now also expose a normalized `subscription` object for storefront recurring-plan UI. Products renders a four-variant storefront page-template library that opens `/pages/new` with the products collection id and `datasetMode=list-or-item`for catalog grid, featured collection, product detail, and product launch pages; each template brief includes route model, section expectations, dataset bindings, public commerce APIs, and dynamic-route promotion metadata. Public order intake now validates whole-number item quantities before pricing/reservation, calculates a checkout quote from the product tax class, shipping profile/weight, product discount code, and Settings tax/shipping/discount toggles/rules, calls configured HTTP tax/shipping/discount quote providers during public checkout, applies successful provider adjustments to persisted subtotal/discount/shipping/tax/total fields while surfacing provider adjustment metadata, returns a manual/provider-ready checkout-session handoff when no server Stripe credential is configured, executes Stripe Checkout Session creation when Settings selects Stripe and`BACKY_STRIPE_SECRET_KEY`/`STRIPE_SECRET_KEY`is configured, switches all-subscription carts to Stripe subscription mode with recurring interval and trial-day price data, then persists subtotal, discount, shipping, tax, total, checkout session, payment provider/reference, and customer linkage on the private order record while upserting a private customer profile. Product records also expose a provider catalog sync action:`POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/commerce/products/:productId/provider-sync`validates`commerce.edit`, stores safe `providersync`metadata, records a`commerce.product.provider_sync`audit event, falls back to handoff metadata when execution config is absent, creates Stripe Product/Price records through the configured Stripe API when`BACKY_STRIPE_SECRET_KEY`/`STRIPE_SECRET_KEY`is available, creates PayPal catalog products plus PayPal subscription billing plans when`BACKY_PAYPAL_ACCESS_TOKEN`/`PAYPAL_ACCESS_TOKEN`or PayPal client credentials are available, creates Paddle Product/Price records through the configured Paddle API when`BACKY_PADDLE_API_KEY`/`PADDLE_API_KEY`is available, upserts a Square Catalog Item/Variation through the configured Square API when`BACKY_SQUARE_ACCESS_TOKEN`/`SQUARE_ACCESS_TOKEN`is available, creates Shopify Admin API products/variants with Backy metafields when`BACKY_SHOPIFY_ADMIN_ACCESS_TOKEN`/`SHOPIFY_ADMIN_ACCESS_TOKEN`plus store domain or Admin API base URL are available, creates BigCommerce Catalog API products/variants with Backy custom fields when`BACKY_BIGCOMMERCE_ACCESS_TOKEN`/`BIGCOMMERCE_ACCESS_TOKEN`plus store hash or API base URL are available, creates WooCommerce REST API products and variations when`BACKY_WOOCOMMERCE_CONSUMER_KEY`/`WOOCOMMERCE_CONSUMER_KEY`, `BACKY_WOOCOMMERCE_CONSUMER_SECRET`/`WOOCOMMERCE_CONSUMER_SECRET`, and store URL or API base URL are available, creates Etsy draft listings when`BACKY_ETSY_ACCESS_TOKEN`/`ETSY_ACCESS_TOKEN`, `BACKY_ETSY_API_KEY`/`ETSY_API_KEY`, and `BACKY_ETSY_SHOP_ID`/`ETSY_SHOP_ID` are available, and executes a configured HTTP catalog sync provider from Settings or `BACKY_COMMERCE_PRODUCT_SYNC_URL`with a safe`backy.commerce-product-sync.v1`payload that includes product, variant, delivery, inventory, checkout, and subscription metadata; the Products editor renders the provider sync state and exposes Auto, Stripe, PayPal, Paddle, Square, Shopify, BigCommerce, WooCommerce, Etsy, Magento, and HTTP catalog-sync controls. Product operation endpoints now return Backy contract/cache headers for custom admin clients: provider sync emits`backy.commerce-product-sync.v1`, subscription lifecycle emits `backy.product-subscription-lifecycle.v1`, and lifecycle actions emit `backy.product-subscription-action.v1`, all with private/no-store cache scope. Products also renders a Product API response contracts panel and includes an `apiContracts`block in the exported product handoff manifest, mapping public catalog, order intake, provider sync, subscription lifecycle, subscription action, and product automation event endpoints to schema ids, methods, cache scope, and response headers. Products reads private customer profiles and paid order line items back into the commerce signal panel, ranks product performance by units/revenue/orders with current stock/low-stock context, includes that product-performance signal in the handoff manifest, and includes inline profile-management controls for name, email, phone, lifecycle status, and internal notes using the existing admin collection-record PATCH path. Checkout inventory reservation now emits product-specific low-stock automation through`commerce-product`delivery events when physical product stock crosses its configured threshold, supports Settings-gated low-stock email delivery plus workflow webhook handoff, exposes the stream through`/events?kind=commerce-product`, and renders the recent product automation panel in `/products`. The commerce provider webhook endpoint now accepts allowlisted provider settlement events, matches private orders by checkout session/order metadata/payment reference/subscription id, marks orders paid/refunded/failed, stores settlement notes, preserves Stripe subscription ids as payment references for subscription checkout completion, renewal invoice payment settlement, dunning failure settlement, pause, resume, trial-ending, and cancellation lifecycle settlement, and exposes commerce-webhook events through `/events`. Order analytics now reports subscription order, renewal, dunning, pause, resume, trial-ending, and cancellation counts, and `GET /api/admin/sites/:siteId/commerce/products/:productId/subscriptions`now exposes a product-scoped`backy.product-subscription-lifecycle.v1`lifecycle summary plus recent matching subscription orders for the Products lifecycle panel.`npm run test:commerce --workspace schema sync during the test window, creates a published product through the Product editor after generating four Size/Color variant combinations from the option-matrix UI, saves subscription metadata, imports a typed product CSV row through the admin import API, syncs the product provider catalog endpoint and verifies persisted Stripe, Paddle, Square, Shopify, BigCommerce, WooCommerce, Etsy, and Magento metadata plus mocked HTTP product catalog sync requests/payloads/provider ids when configured, verifies the public`/api/sites/:siteId/commerce/catalog`response includes subscription metadata, verifies fractional public checkout quantities are rejected instead of silently rounded while omitted quantities still default to one item, posts a public checkout order to`/commerce/orders`, verifies the returned and persisted quote totals use mocked HTTP tax/shipping/discount provider adjustments with custom pricing fallback plus inventory reservation/customer linkage/checkout-session handoff, executes one mocked Stripe subscription Checkout Session through the public order intake with Stripe Settings plus server Stripe env, verifies the Stripe form amount/metadata/auth/success URL, subscription mode, recurring interval, and trial period, persists the provider session id, URL, provider, and payment reference on the private order, posts simulated subscription checkout, renewal invoice, dunning, pause, resume, trial-ending, and cancellation webhooks, verifies the private order moves through paid, failed, paused/resumed lifecycle, trial-ending, and cancelled states while preserving the Stripe subscription id as its payment reference, verifies order analytics counts subscription orders, renewals, dunning, pauses, resumes, trial-ending notices, and cancellations, verifies the product subscription lifecycle endpoint/panel exposes the matching subscription reference and webhook/reconciliation contracts, verifies configured order notification delivery and product low-stock automation events, edits the checkout-created private customer profile from Products to `vip`with phone and note changes, posts a simulated provider webhook to`/commerce/webhook`, verifies order settlement and `/events?kind=commerce-webhook`readback, checks Products layout overflow/regions/import controls including the backend-fed commerce analytics, ranked product-performance, product automation, customer profile management, subscription metadata, subscription lifecycle, provider catalog sync, product API response contracts, provider execution/reconciliation readiness, and four captures a screenshot, and restores the original demo collection schemas/settings.`npm run test:settings --workspace @backy-cms/admin`now authenticates with the admin auth API, verifies Settings hydration, saves commerce provider/reconciliation metadata, pricing rules, catalog-sync provider settings, and EasyPost shipping-label execution defaults through the rendered UI, restores the original settings, and a focused manifest verification covers public commerce contract exposure. Product subscription action readiness now includes Adyen, Mollie, and Razorpay provider entries, and the action endpoint can execute Razorpay pause/resume/cancel actions with Basic auth, Adyen recurring disable cancellation, and Mollie subscription cancellation when the required credentials and provider references are available, while unsupported Adyen/Mollie pause/resume actions remain structured handoffs.`node --check apps/admin/scripts/commerce-smoke.mjs`, `npm run typecheck --workspace @backy/public`, and `npm run typecheck --workspace @backy-cms/admin` pass after the product API response contract and provider/subscription contract-header paths. | real-provider certification for TaxJar, Avalara, EasyPost, Shippo, Stripe promotion codes, and provider-managed tax/shipping/discount edge cases beyond mock coverage, and real-provider certification/deeper subscription lifecycle workflows beyond current mocked Stripe/PayPal/Paddle/Square/Adyen/Mollie/HTTP/manual action coverage plus the visible and settled checkout, renewal, dunning, pause, resume, trial-ending, and cancellation states. |

count. `npm run test:commerce --workspace @backy-cms/admin` temporarily blocks at the current product count, verifies the rejected product returns `BILLING_PRODUCT_LIMIT`, and confirms the product slug is not persisted before restoring site and workspace settings. | `/orders`                   | Partial | `apps/admin/src/routes/orders.tsx` now exposes a private commerce operations workspace for the active site: order schema setup/sync, private order permissions, public checkout-intake contract visibility, readiness checks, admin API handoff, CSV export/import/template controls, filters, order CRUD, line items, payment state, fulfillment state, tracking, refunds/returns, addresses, customer/support notes, workflow actions, shipment-label handoff metadata, tracking-refresh metadata, fulfillment-dispatch metadata, provider-refund handoff metadata, and an inline linked customer profile manager. Order cards now include rendered Refresh Quote, Prepare Label, Dispatch Fulfillment, Provider Refund, Refresh Provider Refund, and Refund/Return workflow actions; Refresh Quote calls `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/commerce/orders/:orderId/quote`, recalculates saved line items through current Settings tax/shipping/discount pricing rules, configured HTTP quote calculators, or Stripe Tax when Settings selects Stripe Tax and server Stripe env is configured, persists subtotal/discount/tax/shipping/total/currency fields plus provider-adjustment notes, and exposes the current `backy.order-quote.v1` payload with provider adjustment metadata through `GET`; Prepare Label calls `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/commerce/orders/:orderId/shipping-label`, persists label status/provider/id/url/service/cost/created-at fields, keeps draft handoff behavior when credentials or structured shipment data are absent, can purchase EasyPost labels when `BACKY_EASYPOST_API_KEY`/`EASYPOST_API_KEY` plus from/to/parcel payloads are supplied, can purchase Shippo labels when `BACKY_SHIPPO_API_KEY`/`SHIPPO_API_KEY` plus from/to/parcel payloads are supplied, exposes a rendered Void Label action backed by `DELETE` on the same endpoint for local voiding, EasyPost shipment refunds, or Shippo refund requests, and moves eligible orders into processing, Dispatch Fulfillment calls `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/commerce/orders/:orderId/fulfillment`, persists fulfillment dispatch status/provider/id/requested-at/completed-at/payload fields, exposes the current `backy.fulfillment-dispatch.v1` payload through `GET`, executes a Settings-backed HTTP warehouse/3PL dispatch adapter when configured, and keeps a manual warehouse/3PL handoff path when no provider endpoint is configured, Refresh Tracking calls `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/commerce/orders/:orderId/tracking`, persists tracking status/last-checked metadata, uses EasyPost trackers or Shippo tracking-status reads when the matching server credentials are configured, and keeps a manual refresh audit path when credentials are absent, Provider Refund calls `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/commerce/orders/:orderId/provider-refund`, persists refund status/provider/id/reference/amount/reason/requested-at/completed-at/payload fields, records a provider payload handoff when execution credentials are absent, executes Stripe refunds directly when a Stripe `pi_`/`ch_` payment reference plus server Stripe secret are configured, executes PayPal capture refunds directly when a PayPal capture reference plus server PayPal access token/API base are configured, executes Paddle refund adjustments directly when a Paddle transaction reference plus server Paddle API key/API base are configured, executes Square payment refunds directly when a Square payment reference plus server Square access token/API base are configured, executes Adyen payment refunds directly when an Adyen payment PSP reference plus server Adyen API key/merchant/API base are configured, executes Mollie payment refunds directly when a Mollie payment reference plus server Mollie API key/API base are configured, and executes Razorpay payment refunds directly when a Razorpay payment reference plus server Razorpay key id/secret/API base are configured; failed or requires-action provider refunds remain retryable from the order card, and Refresh Provider Refund calls `PATCH /api/admin/sites/:siteId/commerce/orders/:orderId/provider-refund` to refresh pending provider refunds through configured Stripe, PayPal, Paddle, Square, Mollie, or Razorpay status APIs, or to persist Adyen-specific webhook-pending reconciliation guidance because Adyen refund outcomes settle asynchronously through REFUND/REFUND_FAILED/REFUNDED_REVERSED notifications. Refund/Return persists refunded order/payment status, cancelled fulfillment status, refund amount/reason, and a timestamped private note through the admin records API. Public checkout intake now upserts a private customer profile by email, tracks order count/total spent/last order metadata with the real order record id, writes the customer record id into the private order, persists generated checkout-session/payment handoff fields and safe shipping-label/provider-refund defaults on the private order, executes configured commerce order email/workflow webhook notifications with queued/succeeded/failed `commerce-order` events, and persists checkout fraud-risk score/level/review reasons. The Orders editor loads private customer profiles, links the selected order by `customerid` or email, and lets admins edit profile name, email, phone, status, and notes without mutating the order record implicitly. Provider webhook settlement now updates matched private orders into paid/refunded/failed payment states, marks provider refund metadata succeeded for refund events, repairs failed provider-refund webhooks back to paid payment state with failed refund metadata, parses Adyen-style notification items for REFUND_FAILED and REFUNDED_REVERSED refund outcomes, persists safe refund webhook snapshots into provider-refund payloads, and records commerce webhook activity for connected dashboards, `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/commerce/reconcile` supports manual admin repair plus scheduled dry-run previews, `GET /api/admin/sites/:siteId/commerce/reconcile?limit=...` provides a bodyless service-key scheduled execution entrypoint for a single site, and `GET /api/admin/commerce/reconcile?siteId=...&limit=...` provides the platform cron-facing batch endpoint with per-site results/skips/errors. Root `vercel.json` schedules that platform endpoint daily, `GET /api/admin/commerce/reconcile/readiness` exposes non-secret cron/admin-key readiness booleans, and Orders renders those checks in the API panel; its provider-readiness handoff now includes runtime env diagnostics for executable Stripe, PayPal, Paddle, Square, Adyen, Mollie, and Razorpay refunds so operators know whether Provider Refund will execute or persist a handoff. `GET /api/admin/sites/:siteId/commerce/orders/analytics` now returns backend order analytics across demo and repository modes, including revenue totals, payment and fulfillment buckets, source/currency breakdowns, operations signals, 14-day trend points, and recent order summaries rendered in the Orders analytics panel and exposed through the handoff manifest. Orders also renders recent order notification delivery activity from `GET /api/sites/:siteId/events?kind=commerce-order`. `npm run test:commerce --workspace @backy-cms/admin` verifies the orders schema can be temporarily synced to the full 57-field order contract and that public checkout intake plus configured order notifications/provider webhook settlement writes a private order record/customer profile/checkout session, shipping-label/provider-refund defaults, and delivery events while keeping direct public order collection access disabled. `npm run test:orders --workspace @backy-cms/admin` snapshots/restores the order and customer schemas/settings, opens the real Orders UI, verifies command/API/metrics/analytics/notification-delivery/shipment-label/provider-refund/cron-readiness/risk-controls/queue/editor/profile-manager regions and layout/import controls, verifies the order analytics API reports pending manual orders and later paid revenue/fulfillment backlog after reconciliation, verifies scheduled reconciliation readiness diagnostics, imports a typed order CSV row through the admin import API including shipment-label/fulfillment-dispatch/provider-refund metadata, creates a manual order with line items, tax/shipping/discount, payment/customer/tracking/address/note data linked to a private customer profile, updates that profile to VIP through the Orders editor, exercises search/payment/fulfillment/source filters, refreshes the quote through the rendered backend action, then enables temporary HTTP tax/shipping/discount quote calculators, refreshes again, verifies provider-calculated totals plus provider-adjustment metadata through the quote `GET` endpoint, runs with a mocked Stripe Tax endpoint when Stripe Tax env is configured, verifies the Stripe Tax request auth/form payload and Stripe provider adjustment in the quote payload, marks the order paid, prepares a shipping label draft through the rendered backend action, verifies the label `GET` endpoint, enables a temporary HTTP warehouse fulfillment provider, dispatches fulfillment through the rendered backend action, verifies the provider request, persisted tracking handoff, provider execution metadata, and fulfillment `GET` endpoint, voids the label through the rendered action and verifies `GET` returns `voided`, prepares a replacement label, refreshes tracking through the rendered backend action and verifies the tracking `GET` endpoint, runs with a mocked EasyPost endpoint when EasyPost env is configured, verifies rendered EasyPost label purchase/void/tracking execution plus direct EasyPost shipment create/buy/refund/tracker requests, auth headers, selected rate, persisted purchased/voided label state, delivered tracking state, and provider execution payloads, runs with a mocked Shippo endpoint when Shippo env is configured, verifies direct Shippo shipment create, transaction purchase, refund request, tracking status read, token auth, selected rate, persisted purchased/voided label state, delivered tracking state, and provider execution payloads, fulfills it, runs the Refund/Return workflow, executes a mocked PayPal capture refund through the rendered backend action when PayPal refund env is configured, otherwise executes a mocked Paddle adjustment refund when Paddle refund env is configured, otherwise executes a mocked Square payment refund when Square refund env is configured, otherwise executes a mocked Adyen payment refund when Adyen refund env is configured, otherwise executes a mocked Mollie payment refund when Mollie refund env is configured, otherwise executes a mocked Stripe provider refund when Stripe refund env is configured, verifies provider refund request auth/body-or-form metadata, persisted returned refund id, execution payload, retry affordance for manual requires-action refunds, refund `GET` endpoint, rendered Refresh Provider Refund reconciliation for configured Stripe/PayPal/Paddle/Square/Mollie status mocks or a manual refresh handoff, and signed refund-failure webhook settlement into failed provider-refund metadata, and Adyen-style REFUNDED_REVERSED webhook settlement into requires-action refund metadata with persisted webhook payload evidence, otherwise verifies manual provider-refund handoff, edits refund and support notes, simulates a stale pending provider order, verifies a scheduled dry-run detects the eligible repair without mutating the order, verifies the platform scheduled reconciliation endpoint repairs the stale order without a browser click using bearer admin-key auth, runs the rendered Provider reconciliation action, captures a screenshot, deletes the order through the confirmation modal, and restores the original schemas/settings. `npm run typecheck --workspace @backy/public` passes after adding the Adyen webhook-pending refund refresh metadata path. | Add broader real provider API execution beyond current Stripe checkout/refunds/Tax, PayPal capture refunds, Paddle refund adjustments, Square payment refunds, Adyen payment refunds plus webhook-pending refresh guidance, Mollie payment refunds, Razorpay payment refunds, EasyPost label purchase/void/tracking, Shippo label purchase/refund/tracking, HTTP tax/shipping/discount quote calculators, and HTTP warehouse/3PL fulfillment dispatch.                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| `/forms`                    | Ready   | `apps/admin/src/routes/forms.tsx` now provides a backend-backed forms command center for the active site with form list/detail, frontend API snippets, template cards, submission inbox, approval/rejection/spam moderation, contact-share context, webhook delivery history, email notification delivery history, consent export summary/export, and rendered failed-delivery retry actions for both webhooks and email notifications. Template cards can create standalone backend forms directly instead of only copying schema or starting a page, and standalone forms now expose an inline form-builder panel for title/name/description, active/audience/moderation/success/spam/consent-retention/contact-share controls, field add/remove/reorder, field key/label/type/required/placeholder/help/options editing, per-field validation rule editing for min/max length, pattern, and numeric min/max, collection-write target selection, slug source selection, per-field collection mapping, dirty/reset state, and save through `PATCH /api/admin/sites/:siteId/forms/:formId`. Page/blog canvas-derived forms remain visible but are marked canvas-owned so admins edit them from the source canvas. Captcha-enabled public forms now verify external provider tokens before validation/persistence/delivery, accepting Turnstile, hCaptcha, reCAPTCHA, and generated frontend token aliases while keeping captcha tokens out of stored submission values. Public form notification email delivery now supports local-outbox, generic HTTP handoff, Resend, and SMTP provider modes with delivery events for queued/succeeded/failed outcomes. Selected forms can now be saved as backend reusable embed blocks through `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/forms/:formId/embed-block`, which creates a normal reusable section with a form canvas tree, public definition/submit URLs, and captured frontend design tokens/chrome/binding metadata so connected frontends can retain design context while dropping the form into future pages or blogs. Forms analytics are now loaded from `GET /api/admin/sites/:siteId/forms/analytics`, aggregating all form submissions across demo and repository modes into site totals, status counts, collection-route counts, approval/spam rates, 14-day trend data, and per-form breakdowns rendered on the Forms page. The same Forms analytics response now includes lead segmentation and saved-list analytics derived from form contacts and site contact list settings, including lifecycle/quality segment counts, matched saved-list contact IDs/form IDs, and per-form contact/qualified/ready-to-promote counts rendered on `/forms`. Form create/update/delete, submission review, consent retention, and form embed-block creation now emit request-id-backed admin audit logs, and `/forms` renders a Forms activity panel from `/api/admin/audit-logs`. Forms admin APIs now enforce first-class `forms.view`, `forms.create`, `forms.edit`, `forms.manage`, and `forms.delete` permissions instead of relying on broad collection permissions. Admin form creation also enforces the selected site billing quota `limits.forms` when Settings Commerce overage mode is `block`, returning `BILLING_FORM_LIMIT` before persistence once the site reaches its form count. The database repository now persists form spam/consent policy objects into the durable `settings` JSON; `packages/db/src/schema/index.ts` and `supabase/migrations/002_forms_contacts_persistence.sql` create durable `form_definitions`, `form_submissions`, and `form_contacts` persistence with RLS policies, contact-to-submission foreign key behavior, form active/page/post indexes, submission form/status/request indexes, and contact form/status/request/email indexes. `npm run test:forms --workspace @backy-cms/admin` verifies a denied `forms.create` permission override blocks form creation with `FORBIDDEN_PERMISSION`, verifies block-mode form quota enforcement returns `BILLING_FORM_LIMIT` without persisting a form, verifies captcha-protected public forms reject missing tokens and accept a valid provider token without storing it as field data, then opens the real Forms UI, creates a temporary public-create collection, creates a temporary Registration form from the template, edits it through the builder into a six-field public definition with an updated title, placeholder, Company field, Company minLength validation rule, collection field mapping, notification email, notification webhook, spam rules, and consent retention/export policy, saves the edited form as a reusable embed block and verifies the reusable-section content/metadata, verifies the analytics API and rendered analytics panel/trend/top-form regions plus lead-segment and saved-list analytics regions, verifies the public definition API exposes the rule and mapping, verifies an invalid public registration is rejected with the configured validation message, submits a valid public registration against the edited schema, verifies the submission creates a draft collection record with mapped values and source submission provenance, verifies configured email delivery events, retries a failed email delivery from the rendered delivery panel, verifies the consent export/retention policy panel, export action, and due-consent anonymization plus site-wide retention automation for consent fields, verifies failed webhook delivery events render in the Forms UI, retries the failed webhook from the rendered delivery panel, verifies retry queued/succeeded events, refreshes the inbox, approves the submission through the UI, verifies form/submission/embed audit-log request IDs and the rendered Forms activity panel, verifies the admin API readback, checks layout overflow/regions, captures a screenshot artifact, and deletes the temporary form/reusable block/collection. `npm run test:repositories --workspace @backy/db` verifies form definition/submission/contact repository persistence, including spam/consent settings merging. `npm run test:forms-postgres --workspace @backy/db` now provides the explicit configured Postgres/Supabase database-mode gate for migration preflight plus temporary site/page/form/submission/contact create, read, update, filter, merge, and cleanup coverage. `npm run typecheck --workspace @backy/db` passes after the schema/migration hardening.                                                                                     |
| `/contacts`                 | Ready   | `apps/admin/src/routes/contacts.tsx` now provides a backend-backed lead pipeline for contacts generated by form contact-share mappings. It loads source forms and contacts through admin APIs, aggregates all source forms or a selected form, exposes readiness checks, lifecycle metrics, permission contract/audit activity panels, private contacts/list/create/update/import/promote API snippets, handoff JSON, CSV export/import/template controls, manual contact creation with email upsert, source-value search, form/status/quality/duplicate filters, duplicate-email readiness, internal notes editing, individual lifecycle actions for new/contacted/qualified/archived, selected-contact state, select-visible controls, bulk lifecycle updates, and selected duplicate-email merge actions that consolidate identity/source data into one primary contact while archiving duplicates with trace notes. `GET /api/admin/sites/:siteId/forms/contact-segments` now provides backend contact segment analytics for all forms or a selected form, including lifecycle counts, missing identity segments, source-value retention, ready-to-promote leads, duplicate-email records, contact IDs, and source form IDs across demo and repository modes. `GET/POST/DELETE /api/admin/sites/:siteId/forms/contact-lists` persists saved contact filter lists under site settings in demo and repository modes, returns matched contact counts/IDs, and records create/update/delete audit events. `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/forms/:formId/contacts/:contactId/promote` promotes qualified contacts into invited or active Backy users, reuses existing users by email, generates invite links for invited accounts, writes promotion metadata back to the contact, and records contact/user audit events. `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/forms/:formId/contacts/:contactId/promote-customer` promotes qualified contacts into private customer collection records, creates or extends the `customers` collection when needed, upserts by email, writes customer promotion metadata back to the contact, and records contact/customer audit events. The contact handoff manifest includes create/import/segment/saved-list/promote-user/promote-customer/sync/consent-retention endpoints, backend segment summaries, saved list summaries, duplicate group metadata, forms/activity permission requirements, recent contact audit actions, and current selection/merge/retention metadata for custom admin frontends. `npm run test:contacts --workspace @backy-cms/admin` creates a temporary contact-share form, creates a contact through the direct admin contact API, imports a contact from CSV, creates duplicate-email leads, verifies the contact-segments API, creates and later deletes a saved qualified-leads list through the contact-lists API, submits a public lead, opens the real Contacts UI on that form, verifies create/import/bulk/merge/segment/saved-list/promote-user/promote-customer/sync/retention/access-audit regions render, promotes an imported contact to a Backy user and private customer record through the UI, syncs that selected contact to a webhook receiver, verifies contact-sync delivery events, exports contact consent evidence, applies due contact consent retention through the UI, verifies permission keys plus promotion/sync/retention audit activity in the Contacts access panel, merges the duplicate leads through the rendered UI, saves internal notes, marks the submitted contact qualified through the UI, selects the rendered contact, archives it through the bulk lifecycle panel, verifies the admin contact API readback, checks layout overflow/regions, captures a screenshot artifact, and deletes the temporary form/contact/customer data. Contact-to-user promotion now enforces the Settings Commerce `seatLimit` for new Backy users when `overageMode` is `block`, returning `BILLING_SEAT_LIMIT` before user, invite, audit-create, or contact promotion metadata is persisted; `npm run test:contacts --workspace @backy-cms/admin` covers the blocked promotion path. `npm run test:repositories --workspace @backy/db` now verifies the repository-backed contact workflow used by the Contacts surface, including form/status/request/search filters, exact contact ID read/update behavior, duplicate merge metadata, contact/user/customer promotion metadata, consent-retention anonymization, and contact audit-log filters. ci:forms-postgres` when `BACKY_DATABASE_URL` points at a disposable migrated database.                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| `/comments`                 | Ready   | `apps/admin/src/routes/comments.tsx` now provides a backend-backed moderation command center for page and blog comments. It loads site-wide comments, pages, posts, site comment policy, author blocklist entries, private comment analytics, and comment delivery/activity events through API helpers, shows readiness checks, moderation metrics, workflow links, private list/analytics/bulk/single update/blocklist/event API snippets, handoff JSON, CSV export, search/status/target/triage/thread/sort filters, visible selection, moderation reasons, author blocklist search/type filters, and per-comment approve/reject/spam/block/report-resolution actions. The site policy panel persists default public comment rules through `PATCH /api/admin/sites/:siteId` with `comments.configure` authorization and `commentPolicy.update` audit logging, including open/closed state, manual vs auto-approve, guest/name/email/reply/report toggles, captcha provider enforcement, blocked terms, closed message, and default sort. The admin surface now derives a thread map from `commentThreadId`/`parentId`, exposes thread summary/reply/report/pending counts, lets reviewers filter the queue to a single discussion, and displays parent/reply context on comment cards. Comment cards now include an official reply composer that posts admin-authored replies through the page/blog comment API, attaches them to the selected parent/thread, and lets reviewers publish immediately or send the reply to the manual queue. Reply cards now include a same-target parent selector so reviewers can move a reply under another top-level parent/thread through the single-comment update API. A rendered analytics panel consumes `GET /api/sites/:siteId/comments/analytics`, exposing 30-day totals, report reasons, target hot spots, and threaded queue signals for custom admin dashboards. A rendered delivery activity panel consumes `GET /api/sites/:siteId/events?kind=all`, filters comment events, and shows activity, webhook, and email-channel submission, report, moderation, and thread-change handoffs plus retry controls for failed webhook/email handoffs. Settings now exposes comment email notification enablement, recipient, and workflow webhook URL used by comment delivery. Reported comments can now be resolved from the queue or selected bulk controls, clearing `reportCount`/`reportReasons` while preserving review metadata through the moderation API. Blocking a comment now refreshes the author blocklist panel, and stored email/IP blocklist entries can be reviewed and removed from the Comments UI. Repository mode now stores comment author email/IP blocklist entries in the database, the site comment blocklist API reads/deletes those durable entries, and page/post repository deletes clean attached comments before removing the target. `npm run test:comments --workspace @backy-cms/admin` asserts denied `comments.manage` and denied `comments.configure` overrides return `FORBIDDEN_PERMISSION`, creates a temporary published page, configures a temporary comment webhook plus local-outbox comment email recipient, verifies required comment captcha rejects missing tokens and accepts a mock provider token, submits public pending page comments plus a parent/reply thread and second top-level move target, reports one comment through the public report API, verifies the analytics API reports seeded pending/reported/threaded/reason counts, verifies comment-submitted/comment-reported activity, webhook, and email delivery event readback including failed webhook retry from the rendered panel, opens the real Comments UI, verifies the rendered analytics panel, delivery activity panel, thread map, and parent/reply card context, creates an official admin reply through the rendered composer and verifies API readback with parent/thread linkage plus approved status, moves an existing reply to another rendered parent and verifies parent/thread API readback, saves a stricter comment policy through rendered controls, verifies policy API readback, verifies a public blocked-term comment is rejected, approves one comment, rejects another, resolves the reported comment through rendered controls, blocks one comment with a persisted reason, verifies the author blocklist entry is created, removes it through the rendered blocklist panel, verifies API readback including reviewer/rejection reason/cleared report flags/blocklist cleanup, checks layout overflow/regions, captures a screenshot artifact, deletes the temporary page, restores notification settings and default policy, and verifies attached fallback comments are cleaned up. `npm run test:repositories --workspace @backy/db` covers durable comment moderation update persistence, comment blocklist create/list/delete behavior, and page/post comment cleanup in repository mode. | `/media`                    | Ready   | `apps/admin/src/routes/media.tsx` now loads/uploads/updates/deletes through backend media endpoints, with metadata editing, search/type/visibility filters, folder create/delete/filter/assignment UI, selectable media cards, bulk visibility/folder/delete actions, usage links to referenced pages/posts, explicit page/post bind-unbind controls, and font registration fields for uploaded font family/weight/style/fallback/display. The page now groups uploaded font assets by family, variants, fallback stack, display mode, and delivery visibility. The page exposes usage analytics for the loaded library: storage/type mix, referenced vs unused counts, public/private delivery counts, replacement/version counts, largest assets, and actionable All/Unused/Referenced/Replaced grid focus controls. The media storage panel now shows the active runtime provider, quota, Supabase project/bucket readiness, missing environment fields, a direct Infrastructure settings link, Media-page "Run check" plus "Provision probe" actions that reuse the Settings infrastructure validator for storage/Supabase diagnostics, and a dedicated "Rotation probe" action that validates provider-specific replacement credential environment variables before operators promote them. The provisioning path can create or verify the configured local uploads directory/Supabase Storage bucket/S3-compatible bucket when credentials allow it, and verifies the active bucket/path can upload, read, stat, list, and clean up a probe object. The same storage panel can edit and save media.configure-gated non-secret provider metadata from Media itself: provider, bucket, public base URL, path prefix, private file delivery, image transforms, Supabase project URL/ref, and Supabase storage enablement, while keeping secrets in environment variables. It now also renders a provider-specific environment contract for local, Supabase, and S3-compatible storage, including required env vars, optional vars, secret indicators, and current missing/detected status so operators have the runtime credential checklist beside the Media setup form; provisioning and credential-rotation probes return provider-specific credential fields plus no-secrets promotion runbooks. The media storage surface now also renders upload-scanner runtime status plus a scanner env contract for `BACKY_MEDIA_SCAN_*`, including provider, HTTP endpoint, ClamAV clamd host/port, optional bearer key, timeout, and fail-open semantics, while admin settings exposes the same non-secret `runtimeMediaScanner` summary for custom admin frontends. The media detail dialog exposes delivery controls for custom frontends: public file endpoint URLs, time-limited signed URL generation with expiry/disposition controls, public image transform URL generation for optimized frontend image delivery, prepared responsive image `srcset` manifests with generated WebP byte/provider metadata, Backy-served delivery analytics, static upload safety scan status, stable-ID file replacement, replacement history, and a per-asset activity trail from admin audit logs. Page/post save, rollback, and delete flows sync media usage references, and `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/media/:mediaId/bind` can explicitly bind/unbind media to page/post contexts. Public media catalog hides private items, while public font media is exposed through `/render` `assets.fonts` with `weights`, `styles`, `fallbackStack`, `display`, and `cssFamily`; hosted pages/posts inject matching `@font-face` rules. Admin integrations can now mint temporary signed media file URLs through `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/media/:mediaId/signed-url`, and `/api/sites/:siteId/media/:mediaId/file` blocks unsigned private files while serving valid signed private bytes from the storage adapter and recording Backy-served request/byte analytics. Public image assets expose a `responsive` manifest in list/detail responses, `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/media/:mediaId/transforms` now generates stored WebP variants through the active storage adapter while accounting generated bytes against quota, and `/api/sites/:siteId/media/:mediaId/transform?width=...&quality=...` remains a validated on-demand optimizer fallback that records transform request analytics, with manifest/OpenAPI/SDK exposure and admin smoke coverage. Uploads and replacements enforce a per-site media quota from `BACKY_SITE_MEDIA_QUOTA_BYTES` with a 500 MB default, run static safety checks before storage, can optionally forward bytes to a fail-closed HTTP scan provider or built-in ClamAV `clamd` INSTREAM adapter configured through `BACKY_MEDIA_SCAN_*` env vars, reject active-content SVG or provider-rejected payloads, return quota usage on upload/list/replace responses, and count retained replacement versions plus generated transform bytes against storage usage. The Vercel environment secret-manager path now executes provider-native storage credential promotion/revocation without storing raw secrets in Backy: promotion upserts replacement `BACKY_*_NEXT_*` values into active Vercel project env vars using `upsert=true`, stores secret storage credentials as Vercel sensitive variables, rejects development-target secret promotion, and replacement revocation deletes the NEXT env vars after promotion. `npm run test:media --workspace @backy-cms/admin` now covers API-backed folder creation, public image and private file upload, rendered media command center/upload/API/storage/env-contract/scanner-contract/folder/bulk/library regions, Media-page media.configure-gated storage metadata editing with API readback, denied media.create/media.edit/media.configure/media.delete permission coverage and restoration, Media-page Supabase env contract rendering, Media-page scanner env contract rendering, Media-page storage/Supabase infrastructure check rendering, Media-page provisioning probe rendering with bucket automation, dedicated credential rotation probe rendering, detail-dialog stable-ID file replacement with retained replacement history, responsive variant preparation with generated transform metadata, metadata editing with visibility changes and API readback, temporary signed URL generation from the details dialog, delete confirmation, layout checks, screenshot capture, and cleanup. `npm run test:media-secret-manager --workspace @backy/public` starts a temporary public app with S3 replacement env vars and a mocked Vercel REST API, verifies authenticated promote/revoke requests hit the configured Vercel API base with bearer auth/team id, asserts active variables are upserted with sensitive secret typing and production/preview targets, asserts raw replacement secrets are not returned, verifies development-target secret promotion is blocked before any provider call, and verifies replacement env revocation deletes every mapped NEXT variable. `npm run test:media-safety --workspace @backy/public` verifies optional scanner hooks across default static scanning, clean HTTP provider verdict metadata, HTTP provider rejection, missing HTTP endpoint fail-closed/fail-open behavior, ClamAV clean verdict metadata, ClamAV infected rejection, ClamAV availability fail-open behavior, and unsupported-provider errors. `npm run test:media-quota --workspace @backy/public` verifies low-quota rejection against a configured server. `@backy/storage` provides local/S3/R2/Supabase adapter contracts for upload/read/delete/sign/list/stat, with local path traversal coverage in `npm run test:smoke --workspace @backy/storage`; public runtime media storage now selects local, S3/R2-compatible, or Supabase storage from environment variables and exposes a non-secret runtime summary through admin settings. Upload/replacement metadata stores the provider storage path and safety scan result so private file reads and deletes do not depend on reverse-parsing remote public URLs. Media create/update/delete, replace, transform preparation, provider analytics ingest, storage provisioning, credential-rotation probe, Vercel secret-manager execution, and explicit bind/unbind now emit queryable admin audit logs with request-id correlation, existing-media mutation routes require media.edit while upload/folder-create require media.create and destructive routes require media.delete, provider/CDN metrics persist request/byte/conversion/value attribution fields, the library analytics panel renders provider ROI totals for attributed value/conversions/CVR/value per request, retained-version comparisons include media-aware preview fallbacks, and `npm run test:repositories --workspace @backy/db` covers DB-backed media version create/list/delete cleanup. | `/users`                    | Ready   | `apps/admin/src/routes/users.tsx` provides a backend-backed users command center. It loads users through authenticated `GET /api/admin/users`, exposes access-readiness checks, role/status metrics, private users API snippets, handoff JSON, CSV export/import/template controls with explicit duplicate-handling policy and dry-run preview, search/role/status/access-review filters, table pagination/sorting, inline role/status updates through authenticated `PATCH /api/admin/users/:userId`, selected-row bulk status/delete actions through `POST /api/admin/users/bulk`, delete confirmation through authenticated `DELETE /api/admin/users/:userId`, role permission summaries, signed-in account self-protection locks for role/status/removal/bulk selection, membership-registration handoff links into Pages, Forms, Contacts, and Settings, a right-rail access workflow panel with working actions for user detail drill-down, invites, delivery settings, Teams, and permission review, and a user access activity panel backed by admin audit logs. Users APIs enforce the saved permission matrix for `users.view`, `users.create`, `users.manage`, and `users.delete`, including explicit per-user deny overrides. Direct user creation enforces the Settings Commerce `seatLimit` when overage mode is `block`, returning `BILLING_SEAT_LIMIT` before persistence once the workspace reaches the configured user count. Invited user creation queues transactional invite delivery through the shared Backy email provider stack while keeping copy-link backup controls. CSV import records rollback snapshots for created/upserted users and exposes a rollback action that opens an in-app confirmation dialog, deletes users created by the selected import batch, restores pre-import fields for updated users, blocks repeated rollback, and records `user.import.rollback` audit metadata. Repository mode uses `BackyUserRepository`, `packages/db/src/repositories/users.ts`, and DB-level email uniqueness plus role/status indexes in `packages/db/src/schema/index.ts`. `npm run test:users --workspace @backy-cms/admin` verifies the users API rejects missing auth, denied `users.create` override blocks user creation with `FORBIDDEN_PERMISSION`, Settings billing seat-limit rejection, authenticated collaborator creation, rendered invite/directory surfaces, CSV dry-run/import/upsert/rollback, import audit metadata, signed-in admin self-protection locks, role/status updates, rendered bulk status update, bulk API delete and audit readback, API readback, rendered Access activity panel, layout/regions, screenshot artifact, in-app confirmation dialogs, and cleanup. User billing note: direct user creation and CSV import creation both enforce the Settings Commerce `seatLimit` when `overageMode` is `block`, returning `BILLING_SEAT_LIMIT` before persistence. `npm run test:users --workspace @backy-cms/admin` covers both blocked paths and verifies no user is persisted.
| `/teams` | Ready | `apps/admin/src/routes/teams.tsx` now loads teams through authenticated `GET /api/admin/teams`, gates the workspace with `users.view`/`users.manage`, renders loading/empty/error/permission states, and uses the shared admin session token for backend writes. The Teams UI can create teams, switch the current team, edit team name/slug, delete teams through an accessible in-app confirmation dialog, invite members with role selection, queue transactional invite email delivery through Backy's shared provider stack with provider/status metadata and a manual copy-link backup, update member roles, block self/final-owner membership mutations, and remove invited members through an accessible in-app confirmation dialog. Newly created teams are inserted into local state before the post-mutation refresh, so a successful create stays visible even if the follow-up list refresh fails. Backend team APIs support authenticated list/create/update/delete plus member invite/list/update/remove through `/api/admin/teams` and `/api/admin/teams/:teamId/members`, reject direct self membership changes, final-owner demotion/removal, and non-owner owner-role assignment in demo fallback or repository mode, and emit team/member audit metadata including invite delivery provider/status/error fields. The page now renders an `activity.export`-gated Team activity panel for the selected workspace, loading request-id-backed team/member audit events from `GET /api/admin/audit-logs?teamId=...` with refresh, loading, empty, and error states. Team list/detail APIs now include a workspace ownership summary for owned sites, the rendered Teams UI shows a Workspace Sites panel with site counts and owned-site rows, and team deletion is blocked in demo and repository modes while owned sites remain attached. Settings Commerce now persists a workspace `teamLimit`, and `POST /api/admin/teams` enforces it in block overage mode with `BILLING_TEAM_LIMIT` before persistence. Repository mode has durable team and team-member tables, unique team slugs, unique `(teamId, userId)` membership rows, and owner/member lookup indexes for workspace authorization. `npm run test:teams --workspace @backy-cms/admin` creates and updates a temporary team through the API, verifies Settings block-mode team creation returns `BILLING_TEAM_LIMIT` without persisting a team, verifies direct API rejection for self membership changes, non-owner owner-role assignment, final-owner demotion/removal, and over-seat brand-new member invites, verifies API deletion, opens the real `/teams` UI in authenticated headless Chrome, creates a team through the rendered modal, verifies the Team activity panel shows the create audit event, creates a temporary site owned by that team, verifies `DELETE /api/admin/teams/:teamId` returns `TEAM_HAS_SITES`, verifies the rendered Workspace Sites panel shows the owned site and disables deletion until the site is removed, edits name and slug, verifies the update audit event, invites a temporary viewer, verifies the queued invite delivery panel plus invite audit event, changes the invited member to editor, verifies the role-update audit event, opens and confirms the member-removal dialog, verifies the member-removal audit event, opens and confirms the team-delete dialog, then creates and accepts a temporary admin invite to verify the rendered Teams UI allows create/edit/invite/delete and audit refresh for a non-owner admin role, creates/accepts a temporary editor invite to verify default editor role access is blocked by `users.view` and disables create/refresh, creates/accepts a temporary default viewer invite to verify the viewer role is blocked by `users.view` and disables create/refresh, and creates/accepts a separate temporary viewer invite with explicit `users.view` allow plus `users.manage`/`activity.export` deny overrides to verify the rendered Teams UI is read-only with audit refresh disabled by that real session. The smoke captures a screenshot and cleans up temporary team/user records. `npm run typecheck --workspace @backy/db` passes after the repository index hardening. | Current Teams surface scope is complete; broader workspace/site authorization across every content resource and broader production role policy rollout remain tracked globally. |

Team billing note: creating teams through `POST /api/admin/teams` now enforces Settings Commerce `teamLimit`, and inviting a brand-new team member through `POST /api/admin/teams/:teamId/members` enforces `seatLimit` when `overageMode` is `block`. Existing-user team membership changes do not consume seats, and `npm run test:teams --workspace @backy-cms/admin` covers both blocked paths.
| `/settings` | Partial | `apps/admin/src/routes/settings.tsx` now loads/saves delivery mode and regenerates API keys through `GET/PATCH/POST /api/admin/settings`, with local fallback state. Settings persist through the file-backed admin content store in demo mode and through the DB-backed `platform_settings` repository in repository mode; admin reads include active runtime media storage, database, Supabase, media scanner, Vercel, notification email, commerce webhook-secret diagnostics, catalog provider credential readiness for Shopify, BigCommerce, WooCommerce, and Etsy, and interactive-component runtime readiness. `POST /api/admin/settings` with `action: "validate-infrastructure"` returns database/storage/Supabase/media-scanner/Vercel/notification/commerce readiness diagnostics against draft metadata and runtime env, and full Settings editors can record Vercel deployment/domain check history with request IDs, status counts, project/domain metadata, and audit entries. The Delivery tab surfaces API contracts and storage runtime state, the Infrastructure tab persists non-secret Supabase/Vercel connection metadata, shows detected environment readiness, exposes runtime cards and an env validation matrix for storage, Supabase, media scanning, Vercel, notifications, commerce webhook secrets, Stripe API execution, Shopify/BigCommerce/WooCommerce/Etsy catalog-sync env readiness, EasyPost/Shippo label-execution env readiness, and interactive-component registry/sandbox runtime readiness, exposes a rendered infrastructure check result panel plus recent deployment history, and persists global media policy for max upload size, workspace storage limit, warning threshold, and allowed file types; the Commerce tab now persists storefront mode, currency, payment-provider handoff metadata, checkout redirect paths, guest checkout, taxes, shipping, discounts, pricing rule defaults, inventory reservation, commerce webhook intent, catalog-sync provider endpoint settings, and global platform billing plan/limits/overage/contact policy including site, team, and seat limits. The configured Settings Commerce `siteLimit`/`teamLimit`/`seatLimit` and `overageMode` are now enforced by direct site, team, and user creation APIs when overage mode is `block`, returning `BILLING_SITE_LIMIT`, `BILLING_TEAM_LIMIT`, or `BILLING_SEAT_LIMIT` before persistence once the workspace reaches the configured count. This keeps the content/settings/API control plane Backy-owned while treating Supabase, Vercel, notification providers, media scanners, and payment/catalog providers as configurable external connectors with secrets kept outside settings. Commerce webhook signing secret fields now accept environment-variable references only; default site name, appearance theme tokens, SEO OG image URL, storage policy bounds, storage public base URL, Supabase project URL/ref, Vercel production domain, HTTP quote/fulfillment/catalog-sync provider endpoints, commerce redirects/contact email, notification recipient/webhook URLs, and auth policy bounds are validated before persistence; raw secret-like provider values are rejected by UI validation and the Settings PATCH API with `SECRET_REFERENCE_REQUIRED`, invalid URLs are rejected with `VALIDATION_ERROR`, and the Settings smoke verifies rejection without mutating persisted settings. The page has moved further onto shared Backy-owned UI primitives (`Button`, `Panel`, `Notice`, `SegmentedTabs`) and includes a settings command center, frontend API capability map,
