without also importing `deliverSiteWebhooks`, and separately guards the named site create/update/delete, settings, SEO, navigation, redirects, frontend design, page/blog, reusable section, collection, media, form, commerce, and interactive-component webhook dispatch contracts. The Security smoke creates a temporary owner session, drives the real Security tab public-key regeneration confirmation, verifies the public key changes while the admin key is preserved, issues a named admin service key, confirms the raw key is shown only at issue time while persisted/read API responses expose only redacted metadata, verifies the service key authenticates admin reads before revoke, persists a last-used timestamp with non-owner-admin scope, and is rejected after revoke, verifies the API-key audit row renders, verifies the non-secret rotation-history and revocation-history entries persist and render changed/unchanged/revoked/manual fingerprints, and restores the original API keys. Settings changes, infrastructure deployment-check recordings, notification webhook test/retry attempts, API key regeneration, service-key issuance, service-key usage timestamp updates, and service-key revocation emit queryable admin audit logs with redacted API-key snapshots, and the Security tab shows recent settings audit events plus API-key rotation and revocation history with refresh/loading/empty/error states. `npm run test:repositories --workspace @backy/db` now explicitly verifies Settings repository default creation, delivery mode updates, API-key rotation, storage/auth/Supabase/Vercel metadata persistence, and readback from the DB-backed `platform_settings` row; `npm run typecheck --workspace @backy/db` passes after the persistence smoke hardening. | Actual Supabase/Vercel/payment-provider connection execution, live billing/provider certification, and external provider-managed webhook certification. |

Settings billing note: site creation, site duplication, custom-domain assignment, team creation, direct user creation, CSV user import creation, brand-new team-member invites, contact-to-user promotion, page creation, collection creation, form creation, product creation, media upload/replacement/responsive transforms, and public checkout order intake now enforce the configured blocking billing limits. `npm run test:settings-admin-contract` source-guards these route-level enforcement hooks and the matching admin smoke coverage so the billing rollout cannot silently regress while live provider billing remains a platform integration backlog item.

URL lookup, rate-limit header exposure, and the public site list pagination envelope. | `GET /api/sites/:siteId/pages`                         | Ready  | Public page retrieval exists from `backyStore`; draft access requires a matching preview token instead of raw `includeUnpublished`, scheduled pages are hidden until their `scheduledAt` time passes, and page list/detail responses now return `{ success, requestId, data }` envelopes while preserving legacy top-level page keys. Page list/detail resources now include normalized SEO metadata (`title`, `description`, `canonical`, `canonicalUrl`, robots, Open Graph, keywords, and JSON-LD) derived from page meta for custom frontends, and that SEO shape is documented in OpenAPI and the JS SDK. Published page reads now emit public contract/cache headers, ETags, conditional 304 responses, and database-mode `content` cache revisions, while preview-token reads are no-store. Page preview-token creation records redacted `previewToken.create` audit logs with tokenless path metadata. `GET /api/sites/:siteId/navigation` now exposes a backend-owned primary/footer navigation tree with page, internal route, external URL, and nested child items while falling back to publishable pages when no custom primary menu exists, and public navigation reads emit contract/cache headers, ETags, conditional 304 responses, and database-mode site-level cache revisions. `GET/PATCH /api/admin/sites/:siteId/navigation` now reads/writes the stored menu and validates page references before saving, and `/sites/$siteId` now provides a connected navigation-management UI. `GET/POST/PATCH /api/admin/sites/:siteId/redirects` now reads/writes stored redirect/410 rules, validates invalid rules, and previews source-route conflicts or missing internal targets without requiring a save; `/sites/$siteId` exposes connected redirect management and conflict diagnostics. `GET /api/sites/:siteId/resolve?path=/...` resolves page/blog/dynamic collection item paths plus site-level redirect and gone rules for external frontends with public contract/cache headers, ETags, conditional 304 responses, and database-mode site-level cache revisions for published reads. `npm run test:admin-contract --workspace @backy/public` covers future-hidden/past-visible scheduled pages, `slug=` and `path=` page lookup, page list `limit`/`offset` pagination, success envelopes and legacy top-level parity, normalized public page SEO on detail/list responses, page preview-token audit readback/redaction, page/post content feed cache headers/304 behavior, configured nested navigation and URL links plus navigation cache headers/304 behavior, invalid navigation references, invalid redirect rules, redirect conflict preview diagnostics, page/post route resolution cache headers/304 behavior, redirect/410 route resolution, and collection dynamic item route resolution. | `GET /api/sites/:siteId/render`                        | Ready  | `apps/public/src/app/api/sites/[siteId]/render/route.ts` returns the AI/custom-frontend render payload for pages, blog posts, and generated collection dynamic list/item pages, supports token-gated draft preview, and negotiates the current `backy.content-payload.v1` schema through `schemaVersion`, `renderSchemaVersion`, or `x-backy-accept-schema-version`; unsupported schema requests return `406 UNSUPPORTED_RENDER_SCHEMA_VERSION` with supported-version details. Render payloads include a `navigation.primary` manifest derived from publishable pages, public media, uploaded public font assets, normalized collection dataset manifests, resolved public collection fields/records, server-resolved element prop values, collection-record editable map entries when canvas elements declare collection `dataBindings`, hydrated `repeater` element datasets/records, and generated dynamic list/item payloads. Published render responses include short public cache headers, ETag/304 revalidation, Backy contract/schema/supported-schema/request/site metadata headers, and database-mode cache revisions sourced from the latest site invalidation event; preview/error responses are no-store. Page preview-token creation is audited without storing raw tokens. `npm run test:admin-contract --workspace @backy/public` validates page, blog, collection-bound page, repeater hydration, custom collection route-pattern list/item renders, and dynamic item render responses against `specs/ai-frontend-contract/content-payload.schema.json`, checks uploaded font asset manifests, checks bound collection values resolve into element props, covers public post render payloads, checks scheduled post render guards, verifies page preview-token audit readback/redaction, verifies render cache/contract/schema/supported-schema headers plus conditional 304 responses, and covers supported/unsupported render schema negotiation. `npm run test:preview-contract --workspace @backy/public` covers draft blocking and tokenized page render/page/hosted preview. `@backy/sdk-js` exposes `render()` and `renderCached()` schema-version request options, `renderCached()` surfaces negotiated Site SEO defaults now persist via `GET/PATCH /api/admin/sites/:siteId/seo`, are editable from `/sites/$siteId`, and are applied to route titles, fallback descriptions, Open Graph images, site-level JSON-LD defaults, discovery defaults, sitemap enablement/default priority/default frequency/dynamic-route inclusion, and robots index/follow/custom directives. Admin SEO responses and the site UI now show supported title variables plus sample dynamic collection list/item route previews using real collection and record values. Page settings can edit route-specific JSON-LD arrays, and page/post render payload SEO merges site-level and route-level JSON-LD for custom/default frontend head rendering. `format=sitemap` emits XML from indexable routes according to stored sitemap settings and `format=robots` emits robots text from stored robots settings; custom-domain sites expose `https://custom-domain/...` canonical URLs and custom-domain sitemap/robots public URLs while keeping path canonicals for API compatibility. Cacheable public contract JSON responses emit `x-backy-cache-revision`, SEO sitemap/robots responses carry the same SEO revision, hosted sitemap/robots responses now emit Backy cache/contract/site headers, and SEO JSON, API sitemap, API robots, hosted sitemap, hosted robots, API RSS, and hosted RSS all support ETag/304 revalidation. Hosted and API RSS feeds emit RSS schema/site/cache-revision headers. `@backy/db` includes a durable `cache_invalidation_events` table and repository/factory contract for scoped invalidation revisions, and database-mode admin SEO, navigation, redirect, page, blog post, collection, collection record, media, and media folder mutations record scoped invalidation events. Hosted Backy sites expose crawlable `/sites/:siteSlug/sitemap.xml` and `/sites/:siteSlug/robots.txt` from the same route index/settings. Site readiness flags duplicate page/post canonical paths as publish-blocking SEO errors. Site settings persist redirect/410 route rules consumed by public route resolution; admin redirect read/save/preview responses include route-shadowing and missing-target warnings for safer SEO routing. `test:repositories` verifies the invalidation repository contract.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | Current SEO discovery and public feed cache scope is complete; metadata, page/post reference browsing, unsigned private blocking, signed private delivery, generated image variants, image transform redirects, delivery analytics, and unsupported transform errors, while SDK smoke validates media/reference typings and media cache revalidation. list filters support type, visibility, search, tag, folder, and quarantine state; upload and folder creation require `media.create`, existing-media metadata updates, replacements, retained-version restore, transform preparation, folder updates, bind/unbind, and provider analytics ingestion require `media.edit`, storage metadata changes require `media.configure`, and destructive media/folder/version operations require `media.delete`; `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/media/:mediaId` replaces an asset file while keeping the media id stable and retaining prior file metadata in `metadata.replacementVersions`; uploads/replacements store SHA-256 binary fingerprints, and retained-version comparison renders name, size, MIME, provider, storage path, checksum, and timeline deltas before restore/delete decisions; `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/media/provider-analytics` ingests provider/CDN counters plus conversion/value attribution fields in batch, matches rows by media id, storage path, or URL, supports replace/increment modes, returns matched/unmatched rows, persists provider delivery metadata, records audit activity, and invalidates media caches in repository mode; `GET /api/admin/sites/:siteId/media/:mediaId/versions`, `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/media/:mediaId/versions/:versionId`, and `DELETE /api/admin/sites/:siteId/media/:mediaId/versions/:versionId` expose DB-backed retained version review, restore, and deletion with stored-file cleanup when a storage path is recorded; | Collection APIs                                        | Ready  | `GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE /api/admin/sites/:siteId/collections` and nested `/records` endpoints support demo persistence and repository persistence through `BackyCollectionRepository`, `packages/db/src/repositories/collections.ts`, and `packages/db/src/schema/index.ts`. Public endpoints under `GET /api/sites/:siteId/collections` expose only published/public-readable collections and publishable records, include select/tag option metadata plus reference targets in field schemas, return `{ success, requestId, data }` envelopes while preserving legacy top-level collection/record keys, emit public contract/cache headers with ETags, support conditional 304 responses, and attach database-mode `content` cache revisions from invalidation events. Public/admin record lists support search, field-value filtering, sorting, limit, offset, and pagination metadata. Public `POST /api/sites/:siteId/collections/:collectionId/records` respects `permissions.publicCreate`, applies collection metadata visitor-create field allowlists before validation/storage, validates submitted values, stores visitor-created records as draft, and returns private/no-store contract responses with ignored-field metadata. Public `PATCH/DELETE /api/sites/:siteId/collections/:collectionId/records/:recordId` respect `permissions.publicUpdate`/`permissions.publicDelete`, require a collection-scoped public write token from `visitorWritePolicy`, enforce selected public-update field allowlists, and return private/no-store envelopes for successful mutations. The admin record list endpoint also serves the same filtered result set as CSV via `format=csv`, `/records/bulk` handles selected-record status updates and deletes, and `/records/import` accepts CSV for schema-validated create/upsert, including select/tag option validation. DB schema now enforces site-scoped collection slug uniqueness and collection-scoped record slug uniqueness for generated `/:collectionSlug/:recordSlug` routes, with status/route/value indexes for public feeds and dynamic route rendering. Collection schema and admin record create/update/delete mutations emit queryable admin audit events with request IDs plus compact before/after or deletion metadata. The admin `/collections` page exposes schema CRUD, public read/create/update/delete controls, collection-scoped public write-token configuration, selected visitor create/update field allowlists, field option/reference editors, record CRUD, record search/filter/sort/pagination/bulk controls, CSV import/export, JSON backup import/export, and permission-aware mutation/export/delete controls against these endpoints, and the editor property panel can attach collection field bindings to selected canvas elements. `npm run test:admin-contract --workspace @backy/public` covers collection CRUD, collection schema and record create/update/delete audit readback, required/unique/option field validation, public collection feed cache headers/304 behavior, public visibility guards, public record reads/search/filter/sort, public create disabled/allowed behavior, draft moderation for visitor-created records, public update/delete disabled guards, write-token enforcement, selected update-field filtering, admin CSV export/import, admin record pagination metadata, bulk record status/delete actions, partial record updates, collection backup export/import, collection RBAC denies, collection-bound render payload hydration, repeater dataset hydration, dynamic list/item route/render payloads, hosted collection route rendering, OpenAPI public collection update/delete operations, and cleanup. `npm run test:collections --workspace @backy-cms/admin` verifies Backy-authored list/item page canvases can be captured into collection metadata and rendered by public dynamic collection routes with collection and record bindings applied, verifies public update/delete policy controls persist the write token and selected update field allowlist through the admin UI, and verifies authored-template compare controls render active-versus-history diff summaries. | Current Collection API scope is complete. | Users admin APIs                                       | Ready  | `GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE /api/admin/users` persists users in demo mode and repository mode through `BackyUserRepository`, `packages/db/src/repositories/users.ts`, and `profiles`/`admin_user_credentials` schema tables with email uniqueness plus role/status indexes. `POST /api/admin/users/import` imports CSV rows with full-name/email/role/status validation, duplicate-email skip or explicit upsert mode, dry-run preview counts with no writes/audit mutation, `users.create` plus `users.manage` permission enforcement for upserts, current-user protection, last-active owner/admin safeguards, and queryable create/upsert import audit events. `POST /api/admin/users/bulk` supports selected-user status updates and deletes with current-user protection, last active `owner`/`admin` safeguards, `users.manage`/`users.delete` permission enforcement, and queryable bulk audit events. User role/status normalization preserves `owner` and `suspended`, the detail endpoint prevents deleting, demoting, or deactivating the last active `owner`/`admin`, and create/update/delete mutations emit queryable admin audit logs with before/after snapshots, request IDs, role/status/email metadata, and changed-field hints. Admin sessions can be listed and non-current sessions can be revoked through `GET/DELETE /api/admin/auth/sessions` with bearer/cookie session authorization and current-session protection. Invite tokens can be generated through `POST /api/admin/users/:userId/invite-link` with validated expiry minutes, accepted through public `POST /api/admin/auth/accept-invite`, consumed on success, and audited with selected expiry metadata. Password reset tokens can be generated through `POST /api/admin/users/:userId/password-reset` with validated expiry minutes and accepted through public `POST /api/admin/auth/reset-password`, which consumes the token, updates a hashed local/database credential, starts a session, and records `user.password_reset.accept`. `npm run test:admin-contract --workspace @backy/public` covers list/create/duplicate-email/update/delete plus the last-admin safeguard. `npm run test:users --workspace @backy-cms/admin` covers API user creation, API search/role/status filters, API `sortBy`/`sortDirection` ordering before pagination, API limit/offset pagination metadata, CSV import dry-run preview, duplicate skips, update-existing mode, permission-denial enforcement, invite generation and acceptance, reset generation and acceptance, selected invite/reset expiry audit metadata, new-password login, UI self-protection locks, active session rendering, UI directory role/status updates, bulk status updates, bulk API deletion, user update/import/bulk audit-log API readback, rendered activity panel, detail lifecycle status updates, delete confirmation, API readback, layout checks, and cleanup.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | Current Users admin API scope is complete. Full cross-resource session auth/RBAC and workspace scoping remain global platform hardening.                                                                                                                      |

Users API billing note: `POST /api/admin/users` and `POST /api/admin/users/import` enforce Settings Commerce `seatLimit` for new seats before persistence; updates, dry-run previews, and duplicate skips do not consume seats.

| Settings admin APIs | Partial | `GET/PATCH/POST /api/admin/settings` now persists delivery mode, API keys, API-key rotation history metadata, API-key revocation history metadata for rotated-out and manually revoked key fingerprints, hash-only named admin service-key grants with permission scope and last-used timestamps, auth policy, global design/SEO/notification settings, commerce behavior and billing-limit settings, global storage policy limits, and non-secret Supabase/Vercel/storage integration metadata including Vercel deployment/domain check history to `data/backy/admin-content.json`; it returns non-secret `runtimeStorage`, `runtimeDatabase`, `runtimeSupabase`, `runtimeMediaScanner`, `runtimeVercel`, `runtimeNotifications`, and `runtimeCommerce` diagnostics including Stripe API, Shopify/BigCommerce/WooCommerce/Etsy catalog sync, and EasyPost/Shippo API key/base-url readiness for the active runtime without exposing stored secrets. `GET/PATCH/POST /api/admin/settings` responses now advertise the stable `backy.admin-settings.v1` schema through admin no-store contract headers and success payloads include schema/scope/endpoint metadata that points site-owned settings to `/api/admin/sites/:siteId/settings`. Settings update, Settings infrastructure deployment-check recording, Settings notification webhook test/retry execution, API-key regeneration, admin service-key issuance, and admin service-key revocation emit redacted admin audit logs covered by `test:admin-contract` and `test:settings`. Commerce webhook signing secret and storage credential reference updates are restricted to environment-variable references, with raw secret-like values rejected before persistence by the Settings PATCH API; `/settings` now exposes the Supabase/S3 storage secret-reference fields, validates them client-side, persists them through the rendered Infrastructure tab, and renders storage provisioning secret-reference readiness, storage write/read/cleanup probe results, storage credential-rotation probe results, and Vercel environment secret-manager plan/promote/revoke operation results. `POST /api/admin/settings` can issue a named admin service key once, stores only its hash/fingerprint/prefix/scope metadata, excludes hashes from settings responses and audit snapshots, accepts active service keys for non-owner admin API access, updates last-used metadata after successful service-key auth, rejects revoked service keys, records deployment-check history for full Settings infrastructure checks, and executes Settings notification webhook test/retry requests with request-id/status/error metadata. Media upload, replacement, and responsive-transform APIs now resolve the Settings storage policy for quota limits, allowed MIME/extension checks, and max upload size enforcement, while env quota remains an override for deployment-level caps. `npm run test:settings-admin-contract` fast-checks the workspace `backy.admin-settings.v1` route/proxy/service-key/docs contract plus the site-scoped `backy.site-settings-scope.v1` route contract before the broader public smoke. That same contract gate now source-guards Settings billing-limit normalization and every current plan-controlled allocation route, executable provider-operation actions for storage provisioning, storage credential rotation, Vercel env secret management, and notification webhooks, site-owned webhook dispatch coverage for every cache-invalidating site admin route, the site webhook OpenAPI/SDK/docs contract, and Settings audit evidence so the previous broad contract-test gap is reduced to live provider certification that cannot be completed without external services. `npm run including EasyPost shipping-label execution defaults, Settings broader environment validation, catalog provider env readiness, storage secret-reference UI persistence and raw-secret API rejection, | Forms/Contacts APIs | Ready | Form definitions, submissions, contacts, spam checks, CSV-like admin exports, and audit events exist in `apps/public/src/lib/backyStore.ts`. Public form list/detail, definition, submission list/detail/create/review, and contact list/status routes now return `{ success, requestId, data }` envelopes while preserving legacy top-level fields where applicable. Public form list reads and `GET /api/sites/:siteId/forms/:formId/definition` emit contract/cache headers with ETags and conditional 304 responses; form detail, submission list/detail/create/review, and contact list/update routes use contract headers but remain private/no-store because they currently include submission/contact visitor data. Admin forms now support direct `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/forms` creation plus `GET/PATCH/DELETE /api/admin/sites/:siteId/forms/:formId` detail/update/deletion in both repository-backed and demo fallback modes, with fallback form definitions persisted into `admin-content.json`; deletion also removes related fallback submissions and contacts. Required checkbox consent fields and authored validation rules validate correctly for public submissions. Canvas-derived forms and standalone admin-created forms can define collection write targets and field maps; accepted public form submissions can create draft collection records after public-create permission and collection schema validation, including normalized source-submission provenance fields when present, and contact-share submissions create/update private contacts. Admin contact list/create/update/import endpoints are exercised from `/contacts`, including direct JSON create, CSV import with email upsert, full contact field updates for merge operations, and rendered duplicate merge UI contracts. `GET /api/admin/sites/:siteId/forms/contact-segments` now returns backend contact segment analytics for all site forms or a selected form across demo and repository modes. `GET/POST/DELETE /api/admin/sites/:siteId/forms/contact-lists` now persists saved contact filter lists under site settings, returns matched contact counts and IDs, and records audit events in demo and repository modes. `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/forms/:formId/contacts/:contactId/promote` now promotes qualified contacts into Backy users with optional invite-link generation and persisted contact promotion metadata. `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/forms/:formId/contacts/:contactId/promote-customer` now promotes qualified contacts into private customer collection records with persisted contact metadata. `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/forms/:formId/contacts/sync` now sends selected contacts to an admin-provided webhook and records `contact-sync` delivery events. `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/forms/:formId/contacts/consent-retention` now exports selected contact consent evidence and applies due consent/source-IP anonymization. `test:admin-contract` covers the temporary page/form/collection/contact flow plus form list and form-definition cache headers/304 behavior and private submission/contact cache headers. The frontend manifest/OpenAPI and SDK now expose the cacheable form definition URL and `formDefinitionCached()` helper. Form delivery events can be queried through `GET /api/sites/:siteId/events?kind=form-submission&formId=:formId`; notification email sends support local-outbox, generic HTTP handoff, Resend, and SMTP provider modes with queued/succeeded/failed events. Failed submission webhooks can be replayed through `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/forms/:formId/submissions/:submissionId/webhook-retry`, and `/forms` now renders delivery history plus retry controls. `/forms` also detects consent-like checkbox fields and exports a dedicated consent CSV with status, request, timestamp, source, policy label, retention/delete due dates, privacy request email, and optional IP hash/user-agent provenance. `/forms` now persists per-form spam timing, rate-limit, duplicate-window, blocked-term controls, and consent retention/export policy controls; admins can apply due-consent anonymization for consent values plus IP/user-agent provenance, and admin automation can run the same policy site-wide; public submissions enforce spam rules before persistence or delivery. Captcha-enabled public forms verify Turnstile, hCaptcha, reCAPTCHA, and generated frontend token aliases before persistence or delivery. Form admin mutations and review/retention workflows now emit request-id audit events rendered on `/forms`, and form admin routes enforce dedicated `forms.*` permission keys with override coverage. `supabase/migrations/002_forms_contacts_persistence.sql` adds durable Supabase/Postgres tables, policies, and indexes for forms/submissions/contacts, while `npm run test:repositories --workspace @backy/db` covers repository create/update/list/detail/delete behavior for form definitions, submissions, contacts, and spam/consent settings JSON persistence. `npm run test:forms --workspace @backy-cms/admin` covers denied `forms.create` override enforcement, admin template creation, form-builder update persistence, authored validation rule persistence, standalone form collection mapping persistence, per-form spam and consent settings persistence, consent retention anonymization including the site-wide automation endpoint, public blocked-term spam rejection, public definition readback, invalid public registration rejection, valid public registration submit, collection record creation, configured email delivery events, consent export UI, webhook failure/retry event history through the rendered Forms UI, UI moderation, rendered audit activity, API readback, layout checks, and cleanup. `npm run test:contacts --workspace @backy-cms/admin` covers contact direct creation, CSV import, contact-share creation, backend contact segment analytics, saved contact-list API create/render/delete, contact-to-user promotion, contact-to-customer promotion, selected contact webhook sync, contact consent-retention export/anonymization, permission/audit panel rendering, duplicate-email merge, private contact listing, notes/status update through the Contacts UI, API readback, layout checks, and cleanup. | Current Forms/Contacts API scope is complete. The configured Supabase/Postgres integration harness is `npm run ci:forms-postgres` / `.github/workflows/forms-postgres-contract.yml`, backed by `packages/db/scripts/forms-postgres-smoke.mjs`; it requires `BACKY_DATABASE_URL` or `DATABASE_URL` because this local environment has no configured disposable Postgres/Supabase service. |
| Comments APIs | Ready | Page/blog comments, reports, moderation filters, site policy, analytics, and report reasons exist. Public page/blog comment submit/list/detail/update endpoints, site-wide comment list/detail/update/report routes, comment analytics, event listing, and report-reason discovery return `{ success, requestId, data }` envelopes while preserving legacy top-level comment/reason/event keys. Report-reason discovery emits public contract/cache headers, ETags, conditional 304 responses, and cache revisions; comment list/detail/moderation/report submission, analytics, and interaction-event routes emit contract headers with `private`/`no-store` cache scope because they can include visitor identity, actor, and moderation state. Page/blog comment submission respects persisted site comment policy for open/closed state, default moderation, guest/name/email/reply rules, blocked terms, and captcha provider enforcement; report submission respects the policy report toggle. The frontend manifest exposes normalized `site.commentPolicy` for custom frontend builders, including captcha requirement/provider/site-key hints for rendering public comment forms. Comment-policy-only admin site patches require `comments.configure`, moderation/update/retry/blocklist routes require `comments.manage`, private comment reads require `comments.view`, comment policy/moderation/thread/retry/blocklist actions record admin audit logs, `/comments` renders the filtered comment audit trail, and the comments smoke asserts denied `comments.manage` and `comments.configure` overrides plus rendered audit activity. The site-wide moderation PATCH route supports `action: "clearReports"` / `clearReports: true` in demo and repository modes so report triage can resolve flags without changing the comment status, and the admin API helper forwards `commentThreadId` filters for thread-specific moderation handoffs. Admin-authored official replies are submitted through the page/blog comment API with parent/thread IDs, admin request IDs, and reviewer-selected moderation mode. Single-comment PATCH also supports moving replies by updating `parentId`/`commentThreadId` with same-target parent validation in demo and repository modes. `GET /api/sites/:siteId/comments/analytics` provides authenticated 30-day/all-time totals, status counts, report reasons, thread metrics, top targets, and daily submitted/reviewed/reported trends in demo and repository modes. Comment submission, report, moderation, and thread-update paths execute configured Settings workflow webhooks and comment notification emails, recording queued/succeeded/failed channel events through `GET /api/sites/:siteId/events?kind=all`; `/comments` consumes those events so admins can see activity, webhook, and email handoffs beside the moderation queue. Failed comment webhook/email events can be replayed through `POST /api/sites/:siteId/comments/:commentId/delivery-retry`, and `/comments` renders retry controls for failed webhook/email handoffs. Repository mode includes `BackyCommentRepository`, `packages/db/src/repositories/comments.ts`, durable `comments` and `comment_blocklist` tables, DB-backed blocklist deletion, page/post target delete cleanup for attached comments, moderation/list indexes, and a site/type/value unique blocklist index. `test:repositories` covers moderation persistence, durable blocklist create/list/delete, and target cleanup. `test:admin-contract` submits an approved page comment and verifies target comments, site-wide comments, detail/update/report routes, report reasons, report-reason cache headers/304 behavior, private comment/event route cache headers, and audit events through the public envelopes. `npm run test:comments --workspace @backy-cms/admin` covers permission-denied overrides, rendered comment audit activity, policy UI persistence, required comment captcha rejection/acceptance, public blocked-term rejection, public pending page comment submission, parent/reply thread creation and UI context, analytics API counts, site-wide comment listing, public report submission, configured webhook and local-outbox email delivery events, rendered comment delivery activity from the events API, rendered comment audit activity, a failed report webhook replayed from the rendered delivery panel, UI-created official admin reply readback, UI reply reparenting readback, UI-driven approve/reject/report-resolution actions, API readback, layout checks, and fallback cleanup after deleting the temporary page. | Current Comments API scope is complete. The broader configured Supabase/Postgres integration smoke is tracked under platform DB rollout. |
| Blog APIs | Ready | Public blog routes exist; draft detail access requires a matching preview token instead of raw draft status access, scheduled posts are hidden until their `scheduledAt` time passes, and blog post list/detail plus taxonomy/author endpoints return `{ success, requestId, data }` envelopes while preserving legacy top-level keys. Public category/tag/author list endpoints and `categoryId/categorySlug/tagId/tagSlug/authorId/authorSlug`, `q`/`search`, and `year`/`month` blog filters exist. `GET /api/sites/:siteId/render?path=/blog/:slug` returns a post-shaped AI/custom-frontend render payload with post comment interactions. Public RSS is available at `GET /api/sites/:siteId/blog/rss` and hosted `/sites/:subdomain/blog/rss.xml`, uses the same visible published/past-scheduled post rules as the blog API, includes post links, descriptions, categories/tags, authors, and RSS contract headers, and is advertised through the frontend manifest, OpenAPI, and JS SDK. The frontend manifest exposes structured `modules.blog.feeds` discovery metadata for RSS, including endpoint, hosted path, content type, schema version, visibility rules, cache revision header, and `limit` bounds; OpenAPI carries the same feed metadata through an `x-backy-feed` operation extension plus `BlogFeedDiscovery` schema, and the JS SDK exposes `blogFeeds()` with smoke coverage. `npm run test:blog-api --workspace @backy/public` is the public blog/API contract entrypoint chaining preview, admin-contract, and public-security smokes. `npm run ci:blog-api-contract` runs `npm run test:blog-types --workspace @backy/sdk-js`, starts the public app, waits for manifest discovery, runs that blog/API smoke, shuts the server down, and `.github/workflows/blog-api-contract.yml` wires the gate into pull requests and pushes to `main`. Blog preview-token creation records a `previewToken.create` post audit event with target, slug, TTL, expiry, and tokenless preview path metadata, while keeping the raw token out of audit storage. `packages/sdk-js/scripts/blog-contract-types.ts` verifies generated-client/type contract coverage for typed blog list filters, `blog`/`blogCached` response types, taxonomy response types, manifest feed discovery typing, and rejected unsupported generated-client filters; `npm run test:blog-types --workspace @backy/sdk-js` passes for this contract. `npm run test:preview-contract --workspace @backy/public` covers draft post blocking and tokenized post API preview; `npm run test:admin-contract --workspace @backy/public` covers public taxonomy/author list/filter smoke, preview-token audit readback/redaction, RSS future-scheduled hiding/past-scheduled inclusion, hosted RSS, manifest/OpenAPI RSS discovery, structured feed discovery, and post render payloads; `npm run test:smoke --workspace @backy/sdk-js` covers the SDK `blogRss`/`blogRssUrl`/`blogFeeds` helpers; `test:blog-list` covers public search/archive feeds. | Current Blog API scope is complete. Broader generated SDK-from-OpenAPI rollout remains tracked under Frontend manifest/OpenAPI/SDK APIs. |
| Public hosted pages | Ready | `apps/public/src/app/sites/[subdomain]/[[...path]]/page.tsx` renders page content, supports token-gated draft page previews, resolves collection-bound page element values before rendering, renders generated collection item pages for published `/:collectionSlug/:recordSlug` records, and applies site-level redirect/gone settings before normal page resolution. Demo hosted page, redirect/gone, and dynamic collection route selection reuses `resolveSiteRoute` from `apps/public/src/lib/routeResolver.ts` before fetching the resolved page or collection content for HTML rendering, reducing drift with `GET /api/sites/:siteId/resolve`. Repository-backed hosted page, blog, redirect/gone, and dynamic collection route selection reuses `resolveRepositorySiteRoute` from `apps/public/src/lib/repositoryRouteResolver.ts` in both hosted HTML rendering and `GET /api/sites/:siteId/resolve`; `npm run test:public-security --workspace @backy/public` passes and includes static regression coverage that blocks duplicated database collection route matching from returning to either surface. `apps/public/src/app/sites/[subdomain]/blog/[slug]/page.tsx` renders published posts, resolves collection-bound post element values, and supports token-gated draft post previews with preview metadata marked noindex. `apps/public/src/app/sites/[subdomain]/blog/page.tsx` renders a theme-aware hosted blog archive with published-post cards, featured-image delivery, category/tag/author filters, search, empty states, and pagination links scoped to `/sites/:subdomain/blog`; when the site frontend-design contract includes a `/blog` page template or `blog-archive` template, the archive renders that authored canvas through `PageRenderer`, hydrates archive title/description/filter tokens, and injects published posts into bound repeaters with post href/image/author/category/tag fields. Hosted sitemap/robots route handlers publish crawlable SEO files for each published Backy site. `GET /api/sites/:siteId/resolve` exposes page/blog/dynamic item/redirect/gone publish guards for external frontends. Hosted HTML receives explicit `Cache-Control: no-store`, `x-backy-cache-scope`, `x-backy-hosted-cache-policy`, and `x-backy-render-surface` headers from `apps/public/src/proxy.ts`, keeping draft preview and mutable public HTML out of shared caches while API/render endpoints retain their cache/ETag contracts. `PageRenderer` applies inherited site theme tokens to hosted output, including root/body/heading fonts, text/background colors, unit-normalized spacing CSS variables, site custom CSS, theme custom font URLs, uploaded font assets, hidden-element suppression, responsive breakpoint markers, scale markers, and mobile/tablet/desktop responsive overrides for layout, style, props, visibility, and nested children; `npm run test:page-renderer --workspace @backy/public` covers that inherited theme/font contract plus responsive breakpoint thresholds/overrides, custom blog archive template hydration, collection-authored dynamic item template precedence, record binding, canvas sizing, and captured custom CSS preservation. `npm run test:preview-contract --workspace @backy/public` covers hosted page/post preview smoke, resolver-backed dynamic collection list/item HTML, hosted HTML cache headers, and blog archive/filter/empty-state rendering. `npm run test:hosted-responsive --workspace @backy/public` creates a temporary published demo page, opens hosted HTML in headless Chrome at desktop/tablet/mobile sizes, asserts live breakpoint and scale markers, checks responsive text/layout/visibility overrides from real DOM geometry, rejects horizontal overflow/error overlays, captures non-empty screenshots, and cleans up the temporary page. Public rendering skips elements marked `visible: false` by the editor layer controls. | Current hosted-page scope is complete for page/blog/dynamic collection rendering, resolver reuse, preview guards, sitemap/robots, theme/responsive rendering, and static hosted-route regression coverage. Broader live DB service and cross-browser CI rollout remain global platform hardening. |
| Frontend manifest/OpenAPI/SDK APIs | Ready | `GET /api/sites?identifier=:identifier` and `GET /api/sites` provide contract/cache-aware public site discovery with ETags, conditional 304 responses, and site metadata headers for identifier lookups. `GET /api/sites/:siteId/manifest` exposes a schema-validated site discovery document for external frontends, including site identity, theme tokens, schema references, capability flags, auth-aware admin capability flags, route patterns, localized route-pattern variants for path-prefix/domain locale strategies, redirect/gone route metadata, configured navigation, public endpoint URLs, admin endpoint hints, SEO/sitemap/robots URLs, canonical/custom/managed delivery domain discovery, site-settings-backed locale discovery, pages, blog taxonomy/author summaries/feed discovery, collection schemas/routes, reusable section summaries, form submit/detail/submission/contact URLs, page/blog comment URL templates, comment report URLs, interaction event URLs, collection targets, media/font counts, and a Settings-derived commerce storefront contract for catalog/order-intake/checkout UI decisions. Anonymous manifest requests now expose `data.admin.auth.mode=anonymous` with every known admin permission false, while valid admin session requests expose the session role/status plus allowed `sites.*`, content, media, commerce, comments, users, settings, and activity flags; admin API keys are treated as non-owner admin capability contexts so owner-only permissions such as key management are not advertised. Site-scoped settings now accept `localization` through `GET/PATCH /api/admin/sites/:siteId/settings`; public manifest/OpenAPI delivery discovery normalizes `defaultLocale`, `localeStrategy`, locale path prefixes, locale domains, and per-locale route variants for generated/custom frontends. Site frontend design contracts can be read/saved/captured through `GET/PATCH/POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/frontend-design` with `sites.view` for reads and `sites.configure` for writes; create-page/create-blog/forms/collections/products/reusable-section workflows can preserve frontend design template provenance, and frontend-design contract saves/captures now emit queryable `frontendDesign.update`/`frontendDesign.capture` audit logs with database-mode settings cache invalidations. `GET /api/sites/:siteId/openapi` exports a site-scoped OpenAPI 3.1 document for discovery, resolve, render, SEO discovery, navigation, media, collection, reusable-section, form detail/submission/contact, page/blog/site comment, comment report, report reason, interaction event operations, and interactive-component registry/sandbox/telemetry operations, including typed page/post/dynamic route resource schemas, typed redirect/gone route schemas, typed collection field/permission schemas, typed form field/validation schemas, typed reusable-section/content-document schemas, `ROUTE_GONE` 410 responses, RSS feed discovery extensions, `x-backy.delivery` domain/locale metadata, and `x-backy.localeRouting` for locale-aware frontend routing. Interactive registry OpenAPI components now include named schemas for `InteractiveComponentRegistry`, `InteractiveComponentManifestContract`, component entries, controls, fallback, security, integrity, runtime, pagination, registry envelopes, and runtime telemetry requests, preserving the postMessage-only security boundary and sandbox metadata in generated client types. Published discovery responses include short public cache headers, ETag/304 revalidation, and Backy contract/schema/request/site metadata headers, while hidden/error discovery responses are no-store. `packages/sdk-js` now provides a TypeScript client and exported public contract shapes for render payloads, typed route results including redirect/gone, content documents/elements, navigation items, SEO routes, media/font assets, collection schemas/records, reusable sections, forms/submissions/contacts, comments, events, commerce storefront settings, manifest admin discovery, manifest delivery discovery, response metadata, and conditional 304 results. The SDK also exposes `manifestCached`, `openapiCached`, and `renderCached` helpers so generated/custom frontends can use Backy's ETags without raw `fetch`, and `resolve()` returns non-throwing gone envelopes for 410 routes while ordinary errors still throw. `packages/sdk-js/scripts/generate-contract-types.mjs` now generates stricter exported `GeneratedBacky*` TypeScript contract types from the AI/frontend JSON schemas before SDK build/typecheck, including manifest, localized route-pattern metadata, render payload, theme token, content element, element action, data-binding, editable-map, and OpenAPI document shapes. The generator also evaluates the live public OpenAPI component schema object from `apps/public/src/app/api/sites/[siteId]/openapi/route.ts`, emitting `GeneratedBackyOpenApiOperationId`, `GeneratedBackyOpenApiComponentName`, per-component `GeneratedBackyOpenApi*` schema shapes, dedicated generated route resource types, generated navigation/SEO/page/blog/font/media/frontend-design schemas, generated media asset/reference/editable-metadata types, generated collection field/permission/record types, generated form list/detail/definition/submission/validation/contact types, types, and dedicated generated interactive registry/runtime request types. `npm run test:frontend-contract-types` compiles representative schema-shaped payloads and negative contract cases through the
