Payloads include post id/title/slug/status/schedule/author/category/tag metadata, before/after snapshots where relevant, changed-field metadata for updates, request id, actor, `content` changed-key metadata, and route-specific source markers so custom frontend build, search indexing, cache, RSS, and integration workers can react to Backy-owned blog CRUD changes beyond explicit publish. `test:settings-admin-contract` source-guards the blog CRUD dispatch contracts.
- **Blog taxonomy webhook update**: `POST/PATCH/DELETE /api/admin/sites/:siteId/blog/categories` and `POST/PATCH/DELETE /api/admin/sites/:siteId/blog/tags` now dispatch `site-updated` webhooks after successful category and tag create, update, and delete mutations in both demo and repository modes. Payloads include compact taxonomy id/name/slug/description/color/sort metadata, before/after snapshots where relevant, changed-field metadata for edits, request id, actor, `content` changed-key metadata, and route-specific source markers so custom frontend build, search indexing, RSS grouping, and blog filter integrations can react when Backy-owned blog taxonomy changes. `test:settings-admin-contract` source-guards the blog taxonomy dispatch contracts.
- **SDK blog taxonomy admin update**: the convenience SDK now exposes authenticated category/tag/author admin helpers for custom CMS dashboards: `adminBlogCategories()`, `createAdminBlogCategory()`, `adminBlogCategory()`, `updateAdminBlogCategory()`, `deleteAdminBlogCategory()`, `adminBlogTags()`, `createAdminBlogTag()`, `adminBlogTag()`, `updateAdminBlogTag()`, `deleteAdminBlogTag()`, and `adminBlogAuthors()`. Generated type fixtures and SDK smoke cover the new methods so external WordPress-style blog editors can manage taxonomy without importing the admin app.
- **Reusable section webhook update**: `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/reusable-sections`, `PATCH /api/admin/sites/:siteId/reusable-sections/:sectionId`, and `DELETE /api/admin/sites/:siteId/reusable-sections/:sectionId` now dispatch `site-updated` webhooks after successful shared-section create, update, and delete mutations in both demo and repository modes. Payloads include section id/name/slug/status/category/tag metadata, before/after snapshots where relevant, changed-field metadata for updates, request id, actor, `content` changed-key metadata, and route-specific source markers so custom frontend build, cache, and page-composition integrations can react when shared builder blocks change. `test:settings-admin-contract` source-guards the reusable-section CRUD dispatch contracts.
- **Interactive component webhook update**: `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/interactive-components`, `PATCH/DELETE /api/admin/sites/:siteId/interactive-components/:componentKey/:version`, and `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/interactive-components/:componentKey/:version/review` now dispatch `site-updated` webhooks after registry create, update, delete, submit, approve, and reject mutations in both demo and repository modes. Payloads include compact component id/key/version/display name/type/status/review/render/source snapshots, actor, request id, `interactiveComponents` changed-key metadata, and route-specific source markers so custom frontend build workers can react when Backy's safe interactive block registry changes. `test:settings-admin-contract` source-guards these registry mutation dispatch contracts.
- **Reusable section workflow webhook update**: `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/reusable-sections/import`, `PATCH /api/admin/sites/:siteId/reusable-sections/:sectionId/metadata`, `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/reusable-sections/:sectionId/versions/:version/restore`, and non-dry-run `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/reusable-sections/:sectionId/instances` now dispatch `site-updated` webhooks after successful reusable-section import/upsert, structured metadata update, version restore, and synced-instance refresh workflows in both demo and repository modes. Payloads include compact section id/name/slug/status/category/tag/version metadata, before/after snapshots or refreshed target summaries where relevant, changed-field/restored-version/target metadata, request id, actor, `content` changed-key metadata, and route-specific source markers so custom frontend build, cache, and page-composition integrations can react to shared-block workflow changes beyond direct CRUD. `test:settings-admin-contract` source-guards the reusable-section workflow dispatch contracts.
- **Collection schema webhook update**: `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/collections`, `PATCH /api/admin/sites/:siteId/collections/:collectionId`, and `DELETE /api/admin/sites/:siteId/collections/:collectionId` now dispatch `site-updated` webhooks after successful CMS collection schema create, update, and delete mutations in both demo and repository modes. Payloads include collection id/name/slug/status/route-pattern/field-count/public-permission metadata, before/after snapshots where relevant, changed-field metadata for updates, request id, actor, `content`/`collections` changed-key metadata, and route-specific source markers so custom frontend build, schema-codegen, cache, and integration workers can react when Backy-owned headless CMS schemas change. `test:settings-admin-contract` source-guards the collection schema dispatch contracts.
- **Collection import webhook update**: `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/collections/import` now dispatches one `site-updated` webhook after successful CMS backup/schema imports in both demo and repository modes. Payloads include imported collection summaries, a bounded imported-record preview, created/updated collection and record counts, upsert state, request id, actor, `content`/`collections` changed-key metadata, and the `admin-collections-import-api` source marker so custom frontend build, schema-codegen, search indexing, cache, and data-sync workers react to imported CMS shape/data changes without scraping admin audit logs.
- **Collection record webhook update**: `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/collections/:collectionId/records`, `PATCH /api/admin/sites/:siteId/collections/:collectionId/records/:recordId`, and `DELETE /api/admin/sites/:siteId/collections/:collectionId/records/:recordId` now dispatch `site-updated` webhooks after successful CMS record create, update, and delete mutations in both demo and repository modes. Payloads include collection id/name/slug context, record id/slug/status/schedule/value-key metadata, before/after snapshots where relevant, changed-field metadata for updates, request id, actor, `content`/`collections` changed-key metadata, and route-specific source markers so custom frontend build, data-binding, search indexing, cache, and integration workers can react when Backy-owned headless CMS entries change. `test:settings-admin-contract` source-guards the collection record dispatch contracts.
- **Collection record batch webhook update**: `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/collections/:collectionId/records/bulk` and `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/collections/:collectionId/records/import` now dispatch one batch `site-updated` webhook after successful bulk status updates, bulk deletes, and CSV imports in both demo and repository modes. Payloads include collection id/name/slug context, requested/imported record summaries, created/updated/deleted/skipped/error counts, upsert state, changed-field metadata, request id, actor, `content`/`collections` changed-key metadata, and route-specific source markers so custom frontend build, search indexing, cache, and data-sync workers react to batch CMS changes without scraping audit logs or receiving noisy per-record events. `test:settings-admin-contract` source-guards the collection record batch dispatch contracts.
- **Form embed-block webhook update**: `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/forms/:formId/embed-block` now dispatches `site-updated` webhooks after generating reusable frontend form embed sections in both demo and repository modes. Payloads include the source form id/name/title/active state/field count, generated reusable-section id/name/slug/category/tags, public definition/submission URLs, request id, actor, `content`/`forms` changed-key metadata, and the `admin-form-embed-block-api` source marker so custom frontend page builders, section browsers, cache workers, and form integration listeners can react when Backy creates a form-backed reusable design block.
- **Media library webhook update**: `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/media`, `PATCH|POST|DELETE /api/admin/sites/:siteId/media/:mediaId`, `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/media/folders`, and `PATCH|DELETE /api/admin/sites/:siteId/media/folders/:folderId` now dispatch `site-updated` webhooks after successful media asset upload, metadata update, replacement, delete, folder create, folder update, and folder delete mutations in both demo and repository modes. Payloads include media id/name/type/MIME/visibility/folder/size metadata or folder id/name/parent/sort metadata, before/after snapshots where relevant, changed-field metadata for edits, request id, actor, `media` changed-key metadata, and route-specific source markers so custom frontend build, CDN sync, cache, font discovery, and media-picker integrations can react when Backy's central media library changes. `test:settings-admin-contract` source-guards the media asset and folder dispatch contracts.
- **Media workflow webhook update**: `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/media/:mediaId/bind`, `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/media/:mediaId/transforms`, and `POST|DELETE /api/admin/sites/:siteId/media/:mediaId/versions/:versionId` now dispatch `site-updated` webhooks after successful media binding/unbinding, responsive transform preparation, retained-version restore, and retained-version delete workflows in both demo and repository modes. Payloads include compact media before/after snapshots, binding target metadata, generated transform metadata, retained-version metadata, request id, actor, `media` changed-key metadata, and route-specific source markers so custom frontend build, CDN sync, page usage, font/media discovery, and cache workers can react to central media workflow changes beyond basic upload/update/delete.
- **Media provider analytics webhook update**: `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/media/provider-analytics` now dispatches one batched `site-updated` webhook after successful provider/CDN analytics ingestion in both demo and repository modes. Payloads include compact before/after media snapshots with provider-delivery metadata, matched/unmatched counts, matched metrics, reporting window, merge mode, request id, actor, `media` changed-key metadata, and the `admin-media-provider-analytics-api` source marker so custom frontend cache, CDN sync, attribution dashboards, and media optimization workers can react to provider usage changes without polling media records.
- **Media operations handoff/action plan**: `/media` now exports a named `backy.media-handoff.v1` manifest with `backy.media-readiness.v1`, `backy.media-operation-action-plan.v1`, and `backy.media-attribution-handoff.v1` blocks. The command center renders the same action plan for storage configuration, provisioning probes, scanner readiness, quota, attribution ingest, asset organization, and font publishing, and it now exposes a copyable attribution handoff that packages provider coverage, source/window channels, conversion/value metrics, missing evidence, and ingestion next steps for custom frontend media ROI dashboards.
- **Media storage-provider certification handoff**: `/media` now includes `backy.media-storage-provider-certification.v1` in the exported Media handoff manifest and renders a copyable Storage provider certification panel under Storage health. The handoff lists the selected local/Supabase/S3 provider, non-secret saved metadata, required env alias names, runtime and probe evidence, provider-certification commands, missing evidence, and the explicit secret boundary so central file uploads remain tied to auditable production storage readiness without leaking credentials.
- **Media smoke auth update**: `test:media` now supports seeded admin MFA through `BACKY_MEDIA_SMOKE_MFA_CODE`, `BACKY_ADMIN_MFA_CODE`, or `BACKY_ADMIN_2FA_CODE` before driving the central media library UI/API smoke, keeping the folder/upload/font/private-file verification runnable when workspace security requires two-factor admin login.
- **Site-owned webhook coverage guard**: `test:settings-admin-contract` now scans every `apps/public/src/app/api/admin/sites/:siteId/**/route.ts` file and fails when a route records `recordSiteCacheInvalidation` without also importing `deliverSiteWebhooks`. This makes site-owned custom-frontend sync coverage enforceable across pages, blog, settings, SEO, navigation, redirects, frontend design, reusable sections, collections, media, forms, and commerce workflow mutations instead of relying on a one-off route checklist.
- **Site webhook contract export update**: the site OpenAPI document now exposes a named `SiteWebhookPayload` schema for the delivered `backy.site-webhook.v1` body, including stable event-kind enums, site identity, request id, reason, actor, and route-specific data payloads. The generated SDK exports `GeneratedBackyOpenApiSiteWebhookPayload`, and `npm run test:generated-types --workspace @backy/sdk-js` covers both positive payloads and invalid schema-version/event-kind regressions so custom frontend and integration workers can type webhook consumers without scraping admin route internals.
- **Frontend contract current slice**: the public OpenAPI export and generated SDK now expose named route-resolution, redirect/gone route, navigation, SEO discovery/route, page/page-list, blog post/category/tag/author/feed, font manifest, frontend-design, collection record/list/detail/delete, media asset, media reference, editable media metadata, form list/detail/definition/submission/validation/contact, collection-record link/error, comment, moderation, report, blocklist, interaction-event/runtime-record envelope, commerce catalog, commerce product, checkout order, order-contract, webhook, generated render-payload media/font/form/comment/navigation/frontend-design subtypes, generated content action/data-binding/editable-map, reusable-section content/document, and interactive registry/runtime schemas. The convenience SDK now also exports `BackyLocaleStrategy`, narrows `BackyManifestDeliveryDiscovery.localeStrategy` to `none | path-prefix | domain`, and types manifest route patterns, route frontend-design metadata, localized route-pattern groups, redirect/gone rules, the routing module, manifest-specific page/blog resources, manifest collection schemas, manifest reusable-section summaries, manifest form definitions, and manifest media summaries for custom frontend locale routing, redirects, render links, captured route-template setup, dynamic collection routing, reusable-block discovery, public form setup, and media/font discovery. `npm run test:generated-types --workspace @backy/sdk-js` now exercises positive and negative generated contract cases for route-resolution, navigation, SEO, page/blog/taxonomy, font, frontend-design, render-payload subtypes, collection records, media, form envelopes/validation/contact, comment/moderation/report/blocklist, event/runtime-record, commerce, generated content subtypes, interactive payloads, SDK locale strategy narrowing, SDK localized route-pattern requirements, SDK route frontend-design metadata requirements, SDK manifest page/blog render-link requirements, SDK manifest collection record-url requirements, SDK reusable-section manifest requirements, SDK manifest form submission-url requirements, SDK manifest media list-url requirements, and SDK redirect/routing-module requirements, keeping custom frontend typing guarded after the broader Frontend manifest/OpenAPI/SDK row moved to Ready with DB-backed service data on 2026-05-21.
- **Frontend contract taxonomy/filter update**: the frontend manifest JSON schema now generates typed blog category/tag/author summary arrays instead of `Array<unknown>`, so generated contract consumers can rely on `id`, `name`, `slug`, and `postCount` for blog archive navigation. Reusable-section category/tag filters now generate as `string[]` instead of loose arrays. The convenience SDK also names `BackyManifestBlogModule`, `BackyManifestBlogCategory`, `BackyManifestBlogTag`, and `BackyManifestBlogAuthor`, with generated-type smoke fixtures covering both positive payloads and missing `postCount`/non-string filter regressions.
- **Frontend blog runtime discovery update**: the public manifest now exposes structured `backy.blog-discovery.v1` metadata for custom frontend blog index/detail UIs, including list/detail/live-manage/RSS/taxonomy/resolve/render endpoint templates, GET/PATCH live-management method markers, published/scheduled and taxonomy/feed counts, public/private cache boundaries, preview-token privacy rules, supported query filters, and stable blog error markers. The manifest schema, SDK type surface, generated-type fixtures, frontend-contract source guard, and SDK smoke now cover the `blogRuntime` module.
- **Frontend commerce runtime discovery update**: the public manifest now exposes structured `backy.commerce-discovery.v1` metadata for custom storefront product catalog/detail and checkout UIs, including catalog/detail/order/webhook endpoint templates, GET/POST method markers, product/private-order collection boundaries, product filters/facets, pricing/inventory/provider capabilities, public/private cache policy, customer-data and provider-secret privacy rules, and stable commerce error markers. The manifest schema, SDK type surface, generated-type fixtures, frontend-contract source guard, and SDK smoke now cover the `commerceRuntime` module.
- **Frontend contract field-schema update**: manifest collection and form modules now generate structured field arrays instead of `Array<unknown>`, including stable `key`/`type` requirements and common label/options/validation metadata. The generated-type smoke covers collection/form field regressions so custom frontends can build dynamic collection lists and public form UIs from the manifest without falling back to admin internals.
- **Frontend contract module-metadata update**: manifest collection modules now generate typed `status` and boolean `permissions` metadata, while manifest form modules now generate typed moderation target metadata, detail/definition/submission/contact endpoint URLs, collection-target handoff, and frontend-design provenance. This matches fields the public manifest already emits and gives external frontends enough contract data to decide read/create affordances and build form detail/submission flows without scraping route internals.
- **Frontend contract reusable/media update**: manifest reusable-section items now generate typed description/category/tags/canvas-size metadata, and manifest media `types` now generates as `string[]`. Generated-type smoke fixtures cover non-string reusable tags and media type regressions, keeping custom frontend section browsers and media filters on documented manifest data.
- **Frontend contract interactive-module update**: manifest interactive-component modules now generate typed registry, sandbox, render-contract, and security boundary metadata instead of loose records. The generated-type smoke covers registry readiness and parent-DOM security-boundary regressions so custom frontends can read the same Backy-owned component/sandbox contract used for interactive figures and code-component rendering.
- **Frontend contract commerce-module update**: the frontend manifest schema now describes the runtime-emitted `modules.commerce` storefront contract, including mode, provider readiness, checkout URLs, pricing rule defaults, inventory reservation policy, webhook state, and reconciliation metadata. Generated-type smoke fixtures cover unsupported commerce modes and non-boolean provider-checkout capability regressions so custom storefronts can read commerce affordances from the manifest.
- **Frontend contract navigation update**: the frontend manifest schema now types top-level navigation menus and layout metadata, including primary/footer menu arrays, recursive children, page/route/url item types, link targets, and header/footer layout variants. Generated-type smoke fixtures cover invalid link targets and unsupported header variants so external frontends can render Backy-managed navigation directly from the manifest.
- **Frontend contract capability/endpoint update**: manifest capability flags and endpoint handoff maps now generate typed keys for routing, render, SEO, navigation, frontend-design, interactive components, media/font/detail/transform URLs, blog taxonomy/RSS URLs, commerce catalog/order URLs, reusable-section detail URLs, form detail/submission/contact URLs, comments/report URLs, and events. Generated-type smoke fixtures cover non-boolean capability regressions and non-string endpoint regressions so custom frontends can rely on the manifest as the API bootstrap document.
- **Frontend contract site/contract update**: manifest site metadata now generates typed description, custom-domain, and comment-policy fields, and the AI frontend contract block now generates typed docs plus schema-reference keys for manifest, render payload, theme tokens, element actions, data bindings, and editable maps. Generated-type smoke fixtures cover unsupported comment moderation modes and non-string schema references so custom frontends can trust the manifest's site identity, comment controls, and contract-document bootstrap fields.
- **Frontend SDK CI contract update**: root `npm run test:frontend-contract-types` now exposes the generated OpenAPI/schema type contract gate directly, and `npm run ci:sdk-smoke` runs those positive and negative generated contract cases before SDK typecheck, build, public-app startup, and runtime smoke. This keeps the normal SDK CI path aligned with the stricter custom-frontend type contract checks after the Frontend manifest/OpenAPI/SDK APIs row moved to Ready with the configured Supabase/Postgres service-data smoke on 2026-05-21.
- **Frontend discovery contract update**: the public manifest now has source-level guard coverage for its bootstrap `data.endpoints.site` URL, the site-scoped OpenAPI document declares `/api/sites` as `operationId: discoverBackySite` with list/detail envelopes and rate-limit errors, and the generated SDK exports `discoverBackySite`, `GeneratedBackyOpenApiSiteSummary`, `GeneratedBackyOpenApiSiteEnvelope`, and `GeneratedBackyOpenApiSiteListEnvelope`. `packages/sdk-js/scripts/smoke.mjs` now verifies every non-fragment manifest endpoint path is represented in OpenAPI, while `npm run test:frontend-contract --workspace @backy/public` guards the same discovery contract from source plus `specs/backy-api-contracts.md` evidence before generated SDK type checks run.
- **Frontend SDK database certification handoff**: public manifest responses now expose non-secret `data.contract.databaseCertification` metadata for the configured SDK Postgres gate, and the site-scoped OpenAPI document mirrors it through `x-backy-database-certification`. The schema/generated SDK export `GeneratedBackyFrontendManifestDatabaseCertification` and convenience SDK export `BackyFrontendDatabaseCertification` at `BackyFrontendManifest.contract.databaseCertification` name `npm run ci:sdk-postgres-smoke`, `.github/workflows/sdk-postgres-smoke.yml`, `npm run test:sdk-postgres-preflight-contract`, `npm run test:frontend-contract-types`, `BACKY_DATABASE_URL`/`DATABASE_URL`, `BACKY_DATABASE_DISPOSABLE_CONFIRMED=true`, `BACKY_SDK_REQUIRE_DATABASE=1`, target-guard env vars, and the disposable migrated Supabase/Postgres requirement without exposing database URLs or service credentials. It also includes `operatorCommandTemplate` for the guarded copy command plus non-secret runtime evidence for configured database URL alias presence, runtime data mode/type, disposable confirmation state, target-guard presence, missing inputs, and certification readiness. `npm run test:sdk-postgres-preflight-contract` and `npm run test:frontend-contract --workspace @backy/public` source-guard the manifest/OpenAPI/SDK/docs handoff after the configured database smoke moved the row to Ready on 2026-05-21.
- **Frontend launch readiness contract**: public manifest responses now expose non-secret `data.contract.frontendLaunchReadiness` metadata for generated/custom frontend launch planning, and the site-scoped OpenAPI document mirrors it through `x-backy-frontend-launch-readiness`. The schema/generated SDK export `GeneratedBackyFrontendManifestLaunchReadiness` and convenience SDK export `BackyFrontendLaunchReadiness` use `backy.frontend-launch-readiness.v1` and `backy.frontend-launch-action-plan.v1` to report endpoint/route/module counts, a launch score/status, routing/render, CMS/design, media/font, visitor interaction, commerce, live-management, and database-certification checks, recommended gate commands such as `npm run ci:sdk-postgres-smoke`, and explicit privacy flags that keep private order/submission payloads and provider/database secrets out of public responses. `npm run test:sdk-postgres-preflight-contract`, `npm run test:frontend-contract --workspace @backy/public`, `npm run test:generated-types --workspace @backy/sdk-js`, and the SDK smoke source/response-guard the manifest/OpenAPI/SDK/docs handoff after the configured Supabase/Postgres database certification smoke moved the row to Ready on 2026-05-21.
- **Frontend SDK database preflight guard update**: `npm run test:sdk-postgres-preflight-contract` now also source-guards the SDK Postgres smoke's service-data RLS policy expectations for media folders, media versions, cache invalidation events, and interactive components, in addition to table/schema/index/constraint coverage. This keeps the database certification gate aligned with the public SDK surfaces that read uploaded media organization, media version history, cache events, and interactive-component registry data after the row moved to Ready with a migrated Supabase/Postgres database smoke on 2026-05-21.
- **Frontend SDK database activity/settings guard update**: `npm run ci:sdk-postgres-smoke` now requires RLS/policy/index readiness for `activity_logs` and RLS/policy/constraint readiness for `platform_settings`, not just column presence. `npm run test:sdk-postgres-preflight-contract` source-guards those expectations so SDK database certification fails before public app startup if audit/event evidence or settings-backed manifest data is not protected by the migrated Supabase/Postgres schema.
- **Frontend SDK local verification update**: `npm run test:sdk-postgres-preflight-contract`, `npm run test:frontend-contract-types`, and the full non-database `npm run ci:sdk-smoke` now pass locally. The CI smoke verifies SDK type generation, SDK typecheck/build, public app startup, manifest/OpenAPI/render/cache reads, blog taxonomy, navigation, SEO, media/font, collections, reusable sections, forms, comments, events, commerce order/catalog contracts, and temporary write fixtures for redirects, navigation, reusable sections, public CMS records, form submissions/contacts, comments, reports, and event reads. The row moved to Ready after the configured `BACKY_DATABASE_DISPOSABLE_CONFIRMED=true npm run ci:sdk-postgres-smoke` certification ran against a migrated Supabase/Postgres database on 2026-05-21, while these local checks keep the contract guarded without exposing database credentials.
- **Frontend SDK database handoff response guard**: the regular non-database SDK smoke now response-tests `manifest.data.contract.databaseCertification` and OpenAPI `x-backy-database-certification` for the SDK Postgres command, manual workflow, local preflight, `BACKY_DATABASE_URL`/`DATABASE_URL` aliases, expected-host/name target guards, database-mode requirement, disposable database confirmation, `operatorCommandTemplate`, media/forms/interactive-component coverage families, runtime readiness/missing-input evidence, and the non-secret database credential boundary. `npm run test:sdk-postgres-preflight-contract` source-guards those response assertions so generated/custom frontend consumers cannot lose the public database-certification handoff after the row moved to Ready with the configured Postgres smoke on 2026-05-21.
- **Frontend SDK smoke port isolation update**: `scripts/sdk-smoke-ci.mjs` now allocates a fresh local port for the public Next app, starts Next directly with that port, and gives the smoke server an isolated `BACKY_NEXT_DIST_DIR` so concurrent local `next dev` processes cannot hold the same `.next/dev/lock`. `BACKY_SDK_BASE_URL` still supports explicit external targets. This prevents the local SDK smoke from accidentally testing a stale process on port 3001, and `npm run test:sdk-postgres-preflight-contract` source-guards the free-port/distDir startup path.
- **Settings frontend database-certification export**: the `/settings` Delivery tab now exposes the SDK/Postgres certification blocker as a dedicated copyable/downloadable `backy.frontend-database-certification.v1` JSON artifact beside the runtime readiness panel. The artifact includes the `npm run ci:sdk-postgres-smoke` gate, manual workflow, local preflight, generated-type contract gate, accepted database URL aliases, disposable confirmation, target guards, operator command template, manifest/OpenAPI/SDK pointers, coverage families, non-secret database/Public API runtime evidence, and the server/CI secret boundary. `apps/admin/scripts/settings-smoke.mjs` and `npm run test:sdk-postgres-preflight-contract` source-guard the controls and artifact metadata after the Frontend manifest/OpenAPI/SDK APIs row moved to Ready when the configured Supabase/Postgres smoke ran on 2026-05-21.
- **Interactive component authoring current slice**: editor interactive blocks now select registry entries by a unique `componentKey + version` option value, then persist both the component key and pinned version into the page/blog canvas element. This prevents multiple reviewed versions of the same interactive animation or sandboxed code component from collapsing to key-only selection in the inspector. The inspector also renders runtime capability badges for status, render mode, source, signature state, sandbox route readiness, permissions, admin API access, dependency policy preset, compatibility targets, and animation-library metadata, then renders schema-driven registry controls for select, range/number, boolean/toggle, color, and text fields and persists edited values into both the element `controls` list and top-level props so trusted figures and sandbox payloads receive the configured animation state without bespoke per-component UI. Registry-declared data-binding presets now render as authoring buttons and persist the selected binding target into the canvas element, while public registry/OpenAPI/SDK contracts expose dependency policy, compatibility, and binding preset metadata for custom frontends. The inspector now also renders a compact registry-driven visual preview panel for rounds, stepper, chart, timeline, simulation, data explorer, and sandboxed canvas/custom component families, including selected title, render mode, family-specific caption, feature chips, and control-influenced miniature output. Editor publish readiness and inspector guidance now require the same Backy-owned `/api/sites/:siteId/interactive-components/:componentKey/:version/sandbox` route pattern as the server, including key/version matching, before code components can publish. Server-side registry validation rejects remote HTTPS, generic relative, wrong-key, and wrong-version custom-code sandbox URLs, requiring the Backy-owned route to match the component's resolved site, key, and version across create, update, review approval, and bundle-upload mutation paths. It also enforces dependency policy presets, blocked runtime built-ins, disabled lifecycle scripts, disabled remote runtime policies, bounded Backy runtime compatibility, render-target compatibility with the selected render mode, required static fallback compatibility, and binding preset schemas before custom-code components can advance through create/update/review/bundle mutation paths. Template breadth now includes timeline, parameter simulation, data explorer, and sandboxed canvas animation presets in the editor catalog and public registry, with admin-contract source guards for registry exposure, controls, data scopes, sandbox runtime metadata, dependency policy, compatibility metadata, binding preset pickers, and visual preview rendering. `apps/admin/scripts/editor-drag-smoke.mjs` now includes source guards for version-aware registry selection, runtime capability badges, control-value persistence, binding preset persistence, sandbox route/key/version publish gating, interactive visual preview rendering, and the broader interactive catalog, `npm run typecheck --workspace @backy-cms/admin` passes, `npm run typecheck --workspace @backy/public` passes, `npm exec --workspace @backy/public -- tsx -e "...validateInteractiveComponentPayload(...)"` accepts the owned sandbox route while rejecting remote/generic/mismatched routes and metadata render-target mismatches, and `npm run test:interactive-sandbox-security --workspace @backy/public` passes for the constrained iframe security baseline, hostile browser fixture, validator source guard, and representative animated canvas runtime fixture.
- **Interactive sandbox response hardening update**: the Backy-owned public sandbox bootstrap route now returns an explicit deny-by-default `Permissions-Policy` for camera, microphone, geolocation, payment, USB, serial, clipboard, and related browser capabilities while preserving the narrow fullscreen permission needed for selected interactive canvases. Its CSP now also denies objects, nested frames, workers, external fonts, manifests, base navigation, and form actions, with source guards in `apps/public/scripts/interactive-sandbox-security-smoke.mjs` so custom code animation support remains a governed iframe platform rather than a free-form script box.
- **Interactive runtime telemetry hardening update**: `POST /api/sites/:siteId/interactive-components/runtime-events` now normalizes runtime messages to the documented `ready`, `init`, `resize`, `error`, `fallback`, and `blocked` lifecycle set, rejects missing messages, rejects unbounded event types, and verifies the reported `componentKey`/`version` exists in the selected site's public interactive-component registry before recording an `interactive-runtime` event. The admin contract smoke includes negative coverage for invalid telemetry types and unknown component keys so public sandbox diagnostics cannot pollute the event stream with arbitrary component identities.
- **SDK interactive runtime event builder update**: `packages/sdk-js` now exports `buildBackyInteractiveRuntimeEventInput()` for generated/custom frontends that host registry-backed interactive figures or sandboxed code components. The helper normalizes sandbox `postMessage` lifecycle names, nested component/data context, and error objects into the bounded payload accepted by `recordInteractiveRuntimeEvent()`, and the runtime-event method now applies the same normalization before posting telemetry. SDK generated-type checks, SDK smoke assertions, API docs, and the SDK README guard the helper after the Frontend manifest/OpenAPI/SDK APIs row moved to Ready with the disposable Supabase/Postgres SDK smoke gate on 2026-05-21.
- **SDK interactive-component admin bridge update**: `packages/sdk-js` now exposes typed admin helpers for the reviewed interactive component registry: list/create/detail/update/delete, usage inventory, portable export, and submit/approve/reject review workflow. The helpers preserve the same admin-session/API-key/bearer auth options as live-management, keep sandbox validation server-side, and add generated-type fixtures plus read-only smoke assertions for registry/detail/usage/export so custom builder consoles can manage rich page/blog components without scraping Backy's admin implementation.
- **SDK page lifecycle admin bridge**: `packages/sdk-js` now exposes authenticated page lifecycle methods for external builders and custom admin frontends: page list/create/detail/update/delete, readiness, publish/archive, preview-token creation, revisions, and rollback. The methods preserve the existing admin auth options and optimistic `expectedUpdatedAt` mutation inputs, and pair with live-management editable-map helpers so custom page builders can create, manage, preview, publish, and then edit Backy pages through APIs instead of scraping the admin route implementation.
- **SDK page lifecycle runtime coverage update**: `packages/sdk-js/scripts/smoke.mjs` now executes the page lifecycle bridge against a temporary backend page, covering create, conflict-safe update with `expectedUpdatedAt`, readiness, preview-token generation, publish, archive, revision listing, and delete through the public SDK methods used by custom page builders.
- **SDK editor composition helper update**: `packages/sdk-js` now exposes `groupBackyContentElements()` and `ungroupBackyContentElements()` for external Canva-like builders. The helpers operate on nested Backy page/blog content without importing admin editor code, enforce same-sibling grouping by default, mark generated groups with `props.editorGroup`, rewrite child `parentId`s, preserve desktop plus tablet/mobile responsive geometry, and return changed content for conflict-safe `updateLiveManagedPage()` or `updateLiveManagedBlogPost()` calls. SDK generated-type fixtures cover the typed helper results, and the runtime SDK smoke groups nested form fields, persists the grouped tree through live page management, ungroups it, then applies the existing editable-path patch before the final live-management update.
- **Frontend editor composition discovery update**: Manifest `data.modules.liveManagement` and OpenAPI `x-backy-live-management` now expose a versioned `backy.editor-composition-commands.v1` handoff for custom builder shells. The handoff names `groupBackyContentElements()` and `ungroupBackyContentElements()`, Cmd/Ctrl+G and Shift+Cmd/Ctrl+G shortcuts, same-parent/unlocked selection constraints, the `props.editorGroup` marker, tablet/mobile responsive preservation, and the live-management content update target so external editors can wire Canva-like grouping without scraping Backy's admin editor.
- **Frontend media management discovery update**: Manifest `data.modules.media.managementPolicy` and OpenAPI `x-backy-media-file-categories.managementPolicy` now expose a typed `backy.media-management.v1` handoff for authenticated custom admin/frontends. It advertises admin media list/upload/detail/folder/version/signed-url/bind/transform/provider-analytics endpoint templates, methods, session/API-key headers, required `media.*` permissions, accepted multipart upload fields including fonts and folder metadata, SDK helper names, response contract markers, and non-secret private-delivery handling. The frontend manifest schema, OpenAPI schema, SDK `BackyManifestMediaManagementPolicy`, generated-type fixtures, frontend contract smoke, SDK smoke, API docs, and SDK README guard the shape after the Frontend manifest/OpenAPI/SDK APIs row moved to Ready with the 2026-05-21 SDK Postgres service-data certification gate.
- **SDK site lifecycle admin bridge**: `packages/sdk-js` now exposes authenticated multi-site lifecycle methods for external builders and custom admin frontends: site list/create/detail/update/delete, readiness, and duplicate. The methods preserve the existing admin auth options and pair with page lifecycle/readiness helpers so custom builder shells can create or clone websites, inspect launch blockers, then manage pages and settings through APIs instead of relying on the Backy admin UI implementation.
- **SDK blog-post lifecycle admin bridge**: `packages/sdk-js` now exposes authenticated blog-post lifecycle methods for external builders and custom admin frontends: post list/create/detail/update/delete, readiness, publish/archive, preview-token creation, revisions, and rollback. The methods preserve the existing admin auth options and optimistic `expectedUpdatedAt` mutation inputs, and pair with live-management editable-map helpers so custom blog editors can create, manage, preview, publish, and then edit Backy posts through APIs instead of scraping the admin blog route implementation.
- **SDK CMS collection admin bridge**: `packages/sdk-js` now exposes authenticated collection schema and record management methods for external CMS builders and custom admin frontends: schema list/create/detail/update/delete plus record list/create/detail/update/delete. The methods preserve the existing admin auth options and pair with public collection schema/read helpers plus `buildBackyCollectionRecordWriteInput()` so custom CMS UIs can manage Backy dynamic content through APIs instead of scraping the admin Collections page.
- **SDK reusable-section admin bridge**: `packages/sdk-js` now exposes authenticated reusable-section library methods for external builders and custom admin frontends: saved-section list/create/detail/update/delete, version listing, and version restore. The methods preserve the existing admin auth options and pair with public reusable-section reads so custom editors can manage saved blocks and restore prior block versions through APIs instead of scraping the admin reusable-section implementation.
- **Interactive sandbox contract update**: the interactive component manifest contract now publishes sandbox response-header guarantees through `sandbox.responseHeaders`, including the same deny-by-default CSP directives, deny-by-default browser permissions policy, `no-referrer`, and `nosniff` settings emitted by the public sandbox route. The site OpenAPI document also declares those headers on the sandbox `200`, disabled `403`, and missing `404` responses, and the SDK generated/handwritten types expose them as required fields for custom frontend builders. `packages/sdk-js/scripts/smoke.mjs` now asserts the same manifest and registry response-header contract, accepts the shared MFA smoke-code environment fallback when local admin auth requires two-factor verification, and `packages/sdk-js/scripts/generated-contract-types.ts` includes generated/OpenAPI plus SDK-convenience negative contract cases proving response headers stay required.
- **Commerce/orders current slice**: `/orders` now loads non-secret Settings commerce/runtime diagnostics into a provider execution readiness panel covering Stripe checkout/refunds, tax quotes, shipping quotes, discount quotes, EasyPost/Shippo labels and tracking, HTTP/manual fulfillment dispatch, webhook settlement, and scheduled reconciliation. The same provider-readiness envelope is included in the order handoff manifest so custom admin frontends can decide whether actions will execute against providers or fall back to manual handoff, and `npm run test:orders --workspace @backy-cms/admin` verifies the rendered readiness panel with the existing order analytics, notification delivery, label, refund, risk, cron, queue, and profile-manager regions. Order analytics now also exposes and renders provider execution analytics for payment-provider mix, provider-refund pipeline status, fulfillment-dispatch provider status, shipping-label provider status, and pending/failure attention counters so operators can see where provider execution is blocked without opening raw order records; the handoff manifest exposes that same provider analytics summary at top level for custom admin frontends, and the analytics API now returns Backy contract/cache headers with `backy.order-analytics.v1`, site id, private cache scope, and no-store semantics. The readiness panel now names and evaluates the full current quote-provider matrix: HTTP, Stripe Tax, TaxJar, Avalara AvaTax, EasyPost rates, Shippo rates, and Stripe promotion-code discounts, using the same non-secret runtime diagnostics that the execution routes require, the quote API now returns private/no-store Backy contract headers for `backy.order-quote.v1`, and the admin API client quote type now advertises those first-class provider adjustment values instead of only `http | stripe`. Repository/Supabase-mode quote refresh, tracking refresh, provider refund creation, and fulfillment dispatch now read the persisted Settings commerce contract before executing configured HTTP/Stripe Tax/TaxJar/Avalara pricing providers, EasyPost/Shippo shipping-rate providers, Stripe promotion-code discount providers, EasyPost/Shippo tracking providers, Settings-selected payment refund providers, or HTTP warehouse/3PL adapters, matching the shipping-label route instead of falling back to demo-store settings. Settings now exposes TaxJar and Avalara AvaTax as selectable tax providers, EasyPost and Shippo as selectable shipping quote providers, and Stripe promotion codes as a selectable discount provider, with server-env readiness for `BACKY_TAXJAR_API_KEY`/`TAXJAR_API_KEY`, `BACKY_AVALARA_ACCOUNT_ID`/`AVALARA_ACCOUNT_ID`, `BACKY_AVALARA_LICENSE_KEY`/`AVALARA_LICENSE_KEY`, `BACKY_AVALARA_COMPANY_CODE`/`AVALARA_COMPANY_CODE`, `BACKY_EASYPOST_API_KEY`/`EASYPOST_API_KEY`, `BACKY_SHIPPO_API_KEY`/`SHIPPO_API_KEY`, `BACKY_STRIPE_SECRET_KEY`/`STRIPE_SECRET_KEY`, and optional API-base/version overrides including `BACKY_STRIPE_DISCOUNT_API_BASE_URL` and `BACKY_STRIPE_API_VERSION` for Stripe quote calls. The Orders smoke now includes environment-gated TaxJar and Avalara AvaTax mock paths that verify persisted quote totals, provider adjustment metadata, provider auth, Settings origin use, order destination address mapping, uncommitted Avalara SalesOrder requests, and TaxJar API-version headers when the matching provider base URLs point at local mocks. It also includes environment-gated EasyPost and Shippo shipping-rate mock paths that verify persisted quote totals, provider adjustment metadata, carrier auth, Settings origin/default parcel use, and order destination address mapping before the label-purchase workflow reuses the same mock servers, plus an environment-gated Stripe promotion-code discount mock path that verifies persisted quote totals, provider adjustment metadata, bearer auth, discount-code query, and active promotion-code filtering when `BACKY_STRIPE_DISCOUNT_API_BASE_URL`/`BACKY_STRIPE_API_BASE_URL` points at the Stripe mock. `npm run test:public-security --workspace @backy/public` now includes source-level regression assertions that the repository branches read and pass persisted Settings into the order quote, tracking, provider-refund, and fulfillment execution paths, and that TaxJar/Avalara/EasyPost/Shippo/Stripe-discount remain first-class Settings-selected quote execution paths.
- **Commerce provider CI update**: `npm run ci:commerce-provider-smoke` now starts the public and admin apps, sets deterministic local mock-provider environment for the existing commerce and order smoke tests, and runs provider catalog sync including Etsy draft-listing coverage, public checkout/subscription actions, first-class public checkout TaxJar/Avalara tax quotes, EasyPost/Shippo shipping-rate quotes, Stripe promotion-code discounts, admin order TaxJar/Avalara/Stripe Tax/Stripe discount quotes, EasyPost/Shippo rates/labels/tracking, Stripe/PayPal/Paddle/Square/Adyen/Mollie/Razorpay refund paths, webhook settlement, and reconciliation coverage without live credentials. `.github/workflows/commerce-provider-smoke.yml` wires the same mock-provider gate into pull-request and main-branch CI, reducing the product/order gap from hidden local env knowledge to repeatable automation while real-provider certification remains Partial.
- **Commerce mock-provider API contract guard**: `specs/backy-api-contracts.md` now mirrors the full current `ci:commerce-provider-smoke` matrix, including Stripe/PayPal/Paddle/Square/Shopify/BigCommerce/WooCommerce/Etsy/Magento/HTTP catalog sync, public checkout/admin TaxJar and Avalara quotes, EasyPost and Shippo rates/labels/tracking, Stripe Tax and promotion-code discounts, and Stripe/PayPal/Paddle/Square/Adyen/Mollie/Razorpay refunds. `test:commerce-provider-certification-preflight-contract` source-guards that summary so the public API contract does not drift behind the actual repeatable mock-provider coverage while live provider certification remains external.
- **Commerce mock-provider setup runbook guard**: `SETUP.md` now names the same `ci:commerce-provider-smoke` provider matrix as the API contract, including catalog sync, public checkout/admin quote providers, EasyPost/Shippo rates/labels/tracking, Stripe Tax/promotion-code discounts, and Razorpay refund coverage. `test:commerce-provider-certification-preflight-contract` source-guards the setup runbook so operators can see the real repeatable mock coverage before deciding which live provider certification gates remain.
- **Razorpay order refund provider update**: `/orders` now has a first-class Razorpay provider-refund adapter beside Stripe, PayPal, Square, Adyen, and Mollie. `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/commerce/orders/:orderId/provider-refund` executes Razorpay refunds with server-only `BACKY_RAZORPAY_KEY_ID`/`BACKY_RAZORPAY_KEY_SECRET`, optional `BACKY_RAZORPAY_API_BASE_URL`, Basic auth, minor-unit amounts, Backy refund metadata, sanitized provider payload persistence, and `executionMode: "razorpay-api"`; `PATCH` refreshes refund status through `GET /v1/refunds/:id`. Settings runtime diagnostics, Settings env validation UI, Orders provider-readiness UI, Commerce provider certification selectors, release-doctor missing-credential checks, standalone/release workflows, and mock-provider CI env now include Razorpay so refund certification can target that provider without accepting unrelated payment credentials.
- **Magento catalog provider update**: `/products` now has a first-class Magento/Adobe Commerce catalog-sync adapter beside Stripe, PayPal, Paddle, Square, Shopify, BigCommerce, WooCommerce, Etsy, and HTTP. `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/commerce/products/:productId/provider-sync` accepts `provider=magento`, reads server-only `BACKY_MAGENTO_ACCESS_TOKEN` plus `BACKY_MAGENTO_STORE_URL` or `BACKY_MAGENTO_API_BASE_URL`, sends a Magento REST `V1/products` payload with SKU, status, stock, Backy product metadata, subscription metadata, and price, persists sanitized provider product/price metadata with `executionMode: "magento-api"`, and keeps handoff behavior when credentials are absent. Settings runtime diagnostics, Settings env validation UI, Products provider-sync controls, Commerce provider certification selectors, release-doctor missing-credential checks, standalone/release workflows, mock-provider CI env, and source guards now include Magento so catalog certification can target that provider without accepting unrelated Shopify/BigCommerce/WooCommerce/Etsy credentials.
- **Commerce provider certification workflow**: `npm run test:commerce-provider-certification-preflight-contract` now guards the manual Commerce provider certification workflow, root scripts, runtime Settings commerce diagnostics, products provider-sync controls, orders provider-readiness UI, and the existing mock-provider smoke coverage. `npm run ci:commerce-provider-certification` can run against a locally started public app or an external `BACKY_COMMERCE_CERTIFICATION_BASE_URL` and checks non-secret `runtimeCommerce` readiness for payment, tax, shipping, discount, catalog, subscription, and webhook provider groups using explicit `BACKY_COMMERCE_CERTIFY_*` flags. Required-mode failures are now scoped to the requested certification groups, so webhook-only certification does not fail on unrelated carrier credentials while shipping-only certification still fails without EasyPost/Shippo readiness. The harness now supports provider-specific certification selectors for payment, tax, shipping, discount, catalog, subscription, and provider-managed webhook groups, so CI can prove one intended provider family such as PayPal, Razorpay, TaxJar, EasyPost, Stripe promotion-code discounts, Magento, Paddle, Stripe webhooks, or Razorpay webhooks instead of accepting any unrelated credential. Local demo certification prepares only its disposable Settings commerce tax/shipping/discount provider selection, selected HTTP endpoint URLs, and webhook URL/secret reference from the explicit provider selectors or auto-detected credentials before checking readiness, while external-base certification remains read-only and verifies the provider already selected in deployed Settings where Settings selection applies so TaxJar/EasyPost/Stripe discount credentials cannot falsely certify an unrelated HTTP/manual provider and webhook certification cannot pass without an env-backed signing secret plus webhook endpoint metadata. The release doctor now also fails selected discount and webhook certification early when the env-backed endpoint/secret or selected provider credentials, including Razorpay key id/secret, are missing. The harness now also asserts that the Settings infrastructure diagnostics response includes a real commerce diagnostic group with provider checks before reporting certification readiness, preventing a false-positive provider gate if Settings stops returning commerce diagnostics, and emits non-secret `target.mode`/`externalBaseUrlConfigured` JSON evidence so release logs prove whether the run certified a local disposable instance or an external deployment. `.github/workflows/commerce-provider-certification.yml` exposes the same manual gate with real provider secrets/vars for Stripe, TaxJar, Avalara, EasyPost, Shippo, PayPal, Paddle, Square, Adyen, Mollie, Razorpay, Shopify, BigCommerce, WooCommerce, Etsy, and Magento plus HTTP discount endpoints. This creates an auditable live-provider readiness gate for products/orders while real-provider certification remains Partial until the workflow passes against configured provider accounts.
- **Products provider certification handoff**: `/products` now renders a visible live-provider certification matrix beside checkout/order-intake readiness and includes the same non-secret `providerCertification` block in the product handoff manifest. The matrix separates repeatable mock coverage through `ci:commerce-provider-smoke` from the required-mode `BACKY_COMMERCE_PROVIDER_CERTIFICATION_REQUIRED=1 npm run ci:commerce-provider-certification` operator gate, names payment/subscription, tax, shipping, discount, and catalog provider families including TaxJar, Avalara, EasyPost, Shippo, Stripe promotion codes, Razorpay, and Magento, visibly includes `backy.commerce-provider-certification-handoff.v1` plus `live-commerce-provider-launch`, and documents that secrets remain server-env/configuration only. The panel now also exposes preflight/doctor commands, provider-family selector envs, and expected evidence attachments so product operators can run credentialed certification without reading hidden release docs. `apps/admin/scripts/commerce-smoke.mjs` source-guards the rendered certification panel, manifest field, gate names, schema, live-launch requirement, operator command, runbook, and representative provider labels so the remaining `/products` Partial status stays tied to an explicit operator-visible live-certification blocker rather than hidden release knowledge.
- **Orders provider certification handoff**: `/orders` now renders a visible live-provider certification matrix inside the provider execution readiness panel and includes the same non-secret `providerCertification` block in the order handoff manifest. The matrix separates repeatable order-operation mock coverage through `ci:commerce-provider-smoke` from the required-mode `BACKY_COMMERCE_PROVIDER_CERTIFICATION_REQUIRED=1 npm run ci:commerce-provider-certification` operator gate, names checkout/payment settlement, quote recalculation, carrier label/tracking, fulfillment dispatch, and provider-refund families including Stripe webhooks, TaxJar, Avalara, EasyPost labels, Shippo tracking, PayPal refunds, Paddle refunds, Square refunds, Adyen refunds, Mollie refunds, and Razorpay refunds, visibly includes `backy.commerce-provider-certification-handoff.v1` plus `live-commerce-provider-launch`, and documents that order records expose only non-secret readiness evidence. The panel now also exposes preflight/doctor commands, provider-family selector envs, expected evidence attachments, runtime provider-family evidence with configured/missing family lists, missing runtime aliases, provider analytics, and the server-env-only secret boundary so custom admin clients and operators can inspect/run the live-provider blocker without reading hidden runbooks. `apps/admin/scripts/orders-smoke.mjs` source-guards the rendered certification panel, manifest field, gate names, schema, live-launch requirement, operator command, runbook, and representative provider labels so the remaining `/orders` Partial status stays tied to an explicit operator-visible live-certification blocker.
- **Commerce storefront provider certification handoff**: public commerce contracts now expose the same non-secret live-provider gate metadata that `/products` and `/orders` show in the admin UI. `data.modules.commerce.providerCertification` on the manifest and `data.commerce.providerCertification` on public catalog/order-intake contracts use `backy.commerce-provider-certification-handoff.v1`, name `ci:commerce-provider-smoke` versus `ci:commerce-provider-certification`, list payment, tax, shipping, discount, catalog, subscription, and mock-provider families, expose runtime provider-family readiness booleans plus configured/missing family lists, and keep provider credentials server-side. The site OpenAPI document now includes `CommerceProviderCertification` and `CommerceStorefrontContract`, generated SDK types export `GeneratedBackyOpenApiCommerceProviderCertification` and `GeneratedBackyOpenApiCommerceStorefrontContract`, and `test:commerce-provider-certification-preflight-contract` source-guards the public contract, SDK fixtures, and docs so custom storefront clients receive the live-certification blocker explicitly while real provider execution remains Partial until credentialed gates pass.
- **Products provider certification command builder**: `/products` now renders a guarded `products-provider-certification-command-builder` inside the live-provider certification panel. Operators can select payment, tax, shipping, discount, catalog, subscription, and webhook families, choose provider selectors such as `BACKY_COMMERCE_CERTIFY_PAYMENT_PROVIDER`, `BACKY_COMMERCE_CERTIFY_TAX_PROVIDER`, `BACKY_COMMERCE_CERTIFY_SHIPPING_PROVIDER`, `BACKY_COMMERCE_CERTIFY_DISCOUNT_PROVIDER`, `BACKY_COMMERCE_CERTIFY_CATALOG_PROVIDER`, Stripe, TaxJar, Avalara, EasyPost, Shippo, Stripe promotion-code discounts, Shopify, Magento, HTTP, or generic webhooks, add an optional `BACKY_COMMERCE_CERTIFICATION_BASE_URL` external target, keep `BACKY_RELEASE_CERTIFICATION_DOCTOR_REQUIRED=1`, and copy a non-secret command for `npm run ci:commerce-provider-certification`. Public commerce `providerCertification.operatorCommandTemplate`, OpenAPI `CommerceProviderCertification`, generated SDK types, SDK smoke assertions, and `apps/admin/scripts/commerce-smoke.mjs` source/runtime guards now carry the same command template while the row remains Partial until the selected live provider run succeeds with real credentials.
- **Orders provider certification command builder**: `/orders` now renders a guarded `orders-provider-certification-command-builder` inside its live-provider certification panel. Operators can select payment/refund, tax, shipping/label, discount, subscription, webhook, and fulfillment evidence families; choose `BACKY_COMMERCE_CERTIFY_PAYMENT_PROVIDER`, `BACKY_COMMERCE_CERTIFY_TAX_PROVIDER`, `BACKY_COMMERCE_CERTIFY_SHIPPING_PROVIDER`, `BACKY_COMMERCE_CERTIFY_DISCOUNT_PROVIDER`, `BACKY_COMMERCE_CERTIFY_SUBSCRIPTION_PROVIDER`, and `BACKY_COMMERCE_CERTIFY_WEBHOOK_PROVIDER` selectors; add an optional `BACKY_COMMERCE_CERTIFICATION_BASE_URL` external target; keep the release doctor; and copy a non-secret `npm run ci:commerce-provider-certification` command while seeing the selected required aliases, including Settings `fulfillmentProviderUrl` evidence for warehouse/3PL dispatch. The Orders handoff JSON now carries `providerCertification.operatorCommandTemplate`, and `apps/admin/scripts/orders-smoke.mjs` plus `test:commerce-provider-certification-preflight-contract` source-guard the UI strings while `/orders` remains Partial until the selected live provider run succeeds with real credentials.
- **Commerce storefront provider certification response guard**: `apps/admin/scripts/commerce-smoke.mjs` now response-tests public order-contract, catalog, and manifest commerce payloads for `backy.commerce-provider-certification-handoff.v1`, `external-live-provider-gate`, `ci:commerce-provider-smoke`, `ci:commerce-provider-certification`, live-launch requirement text, non-secret credential handling, and representative provider families including Stripe webhooks, TaxJar, Avalara, EasyPost, Shippo, Stripe promotion codes, Magento, Razorpay, and local mocks. `test:commerce-provider-certification-preflight-contract` source-guards those response assertions so generated/custom storefront clients cannot lose the visible live-provider blocker while `/products` and `/orders` remain Partial until credentialed provider gates pass.
- **SDK commerce provider certification response guard**: `packages/sdk-js/scripts/smoke.mjs` now response-tests the generated/custom frontend SDK paths for the same commerce live-provider handoff on `manifest().data.modules.commerce`, `commerceOrderContract()`, `commerceOrderContractCached()`, `commerceCatalog()`, and `commerceCatalogCached()`. The assertions cover `backy.commerce-provider-certification-handoff.v1`, `external-live-provider-gate`, `ci:commerce-provider-smoke`, `ci:commerce-provider-certification`, live-launch requirement text, runtime provider-family readiness booleans, non-secret credential handling, and representative provider families including TaxJar, Avalara, EasyPost, Shippo, Stripe promotion codes, Magento, Razorpay, and local mocks; `test:commerce-provider-certification-preflight-contract` source-guards the SDK smoke so public frontend clients cannot lose the handoff while real-provider certification remains external.
- **Commerce provider required-input handoff**: Products, Orders, public manifest/catalog/order contracts, OpenAPI, and generated SDK types now include non-secret `requiredInputs` on each `backy.commerce-provider-certification-handoff.v1` provider family. The visible `/products` and `/orders` certification matrices list the same payment, webhook, tax, shipping, discount, catalog-sync, subscription-action, refund, fulfillment, and mock-provider input aliases that the public storefront contract exposes, including `BACKY_STRIPE_SECRET_KEY`/`STRIPE_SECRET_KEY`, `BACKY_TAXJAR_API_KEY`/`TAXJAR_API_KEY`, `BACKY_EASYPOST_API_KEY`/`EASYPOST_API_KEY`, `BACKY_COMMERCE_DISCOUNT_PROVIDER_URL`/`COMMERCE_DISCOUNT_PROVIDER_URL`, `BACKY_COMMERCE_PRODUCT_SYNC_URL`/`COMMERCE_PRODUCT_SYNC_URL`, `BACKY_COMMERCE_SUBSCRIPTION_ACTION_URL`/`COMMERCE_SUBSCRIPTION_ACTION_URL`, and `BACKY_COMMERCE_WEBHOOK_SECRET`/`COMMERCE_WEBHOOK_SECRET`. `test:commerce-provider-certification-preflight-contract`, `test:frontend-contract-types`, and the Products/Orders/SDK smoke source guards keep those aliases aligned while real-provider certification remains Partial until credentialed gates pass.
- **Commerce payment-provider handoff enum update**: Settings, the admin Settings API, public manifest/catalog/order contracts, frontend manifest schema, site OpenAPI, and generated SDK types now preserve the selected payment-provider handoff values `none`, `stripe`, `paypal`, `paddle`, `square`, `adyen`, `mollie`, `razorpay`, and `manual` instead of collapsing non-Stripe providers to `none`. `test:commerce-provider-certification-preflight-contract` source-guards the Settings UI options, Settings normalization, public contract union, OpenAPI enum, frontend manifest enum, SDK union, and SDK smoke fixtures, while the live-provider rows remain Partial until credentialed provider execution/certification gates pass.
- **Paddle order refund provider update**: `/orders` now has a first-class Paddle provider-refund adapter beside Stripe, PayPal, Square, Adyen, Mollie, and Razorpay. `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/commerce/orders/:orderId/provider-refund` executes Paddle refund adjustments with server-only `BACKY_PADDLE_API_KEY`/`PADDLE_API_KEY`, optional `BACKY_PADDLE_API_BASE_URL`, a `refund`/`full` adjustment request against the order payment transaction id, sanitized adjustment payload persistence, and `executionMode: "paddle-api"`; `PATCH` refreshes adjustment status through Paddle's Adjustments list API filtered by the stored adjustment id. Settings runtime diagnostics, Settings env validation UI, Orders provider-readiness UI, Commerce provider certification selectors, mock-provider CI env, API docs, and source guards now include Paddle refunds so order refund certification can target that provider without accepting unrelated PayPal/Square/Mollie/Razorpay credentials.
- **Paddle refund refresh reconciliation fix**: `/orders` provider-refund refresh now parses Paddle Adjustments list responses as array-backed `data` payloads before sanitizing status, so filtered `PATCH` refreshes can reconcile `approved`/`refunded` adjustments instead of falling back to an empty handoff payload. `test:commerce-provider-certification-preflight-contract` source-guards the array response path, and the existing Paddle orders mock returns the same filtered list shape.
- **Mollie refund payload preservation fix**: `/orders` provider-refund creation and refresh now sanitize Mollie refund responses with Mollie's native `amount.value`/`amount.currency` shape instead of Square's integer-minor-unit money shape, preserving operator-visible refund amounts in persisted provider payloads. `apps/admin/scripts/orders-smoke.mjs` asserts the mocked Mollie create/refresh payload keeps those fields, and `test:commerce-provider-certification-preflight-contract` source-guards the sanitizer.
- **Orders provider-refund route guard update**: `test:commerce-provider-certification-preflight-contract` now source-guards the full `/orders` provider-refund execution matrix in the route, including PayPal, Paddle, Square, Adyen, Mollie, and Razorpay create paths, Stripe/PayPal/Paddle/Square/Mollie/Razorpay refresh paths, Adyen webhook-pending refresh guidance, provider execution-mode labels, and server-only credential alias families. This keeps the route contract aligned with the visible `/orders` certification panel and API docs while real-provider certification remains Partial until credentialed provider gates pass.
- **Commerce certification provider-alias update**: the standalone Commerce, release, and nested Settings certification workflows now forward provider-native commerce aliases alongside Backy-prefixed envs for payment, tax, shipping, discount, catalog, and subscription providers, including Stripe, TaxJar, Avalara, EasyPost, Shippo, PayPal, Paddle, Square, Adyen, Mollie, Razorpay, Shopify, BigCommerce, WooCommerce, Etsy, Magento, and HTTP endpoint credential/store/API-base families. The commerce, settings, and release preflight contracts source-guard representative alias names so manual live-provider certification matches the runtime diagnostics and release doctor instead of requiring duplicate Backy-prefixed secrets.
- **Commerce webhook alias certification update**: the standalone Commerce, release, and nested Settings certification workflows now also forward `COMMERCE_WEBHOOK_SECRET` beside `BACKY_COMMERCE_WEBHOOK_SECRET`. Local Commerce provider certification stores `env:COMMERCE_WEBHOOK_SECRET` into disposable Settings when that alias is the configured secret, and `test:release-certification-doctor-contract` behavior-tests that provider-managed webhook certification accepts the alias instead of failing the release doctor.
- **Commerce certification summary update**: the standalone Commerce provider certification workflow now writes non-secret `GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY` evidence for the `/products` and `/orders` Partial row, `npm run ci:commerce-provider-certification` gate, aggregate preflight/admin source guard coverage, local/external target mode, external admin-key configured boolean, and selected payment, tax, shipping, discount, catalog, subscription, and webhook provider families, matching the release workflow summary without printing external URLs, admin keys, or provider secrets. The Commerce provider runner rejects required certification runs that select no Commerce provider group before starting certification work, rejects malformed external target URLs before certification work, and external-target workflow runs now use explicit `BACKY_ADMIN_API_KEY` or `BACKY_COMMERCE_CERTIFICATION_ADMIN_KEY` secrets rather than generated local keys.
- **Commerce HTTP certification endpoint guard**: Local Commerce provider certification now accepts non-secret HTTP endpoint vars for selected HTTP provider families: `BACKY_COMMERCE_TAX_PROVIDER_URL`/`COMMERCE_TAX_PROVIDER_URL`, `BACKY_COMMERCE_SHIPPING_PROVIDER_URL`/`COMMERCE_SHIPPING_PROVIDER_URL`, `BACKY_COMMERCE_DISCOUNT_PROVIDER_URL`/`COMMERCE_DISCOUNT_PROVIDER_URL`, `BACKY_COMMERCE_PRODUCT_SYNC_URL`/`COMMERCE_PRODUCT_SYNC_URL`, and `BACKY_COMMERCE_SUBSCRIPTION_ACTION_URL`/`COMMERCE_SUBSCRIPTION_ACTION_URL`. The runner writes those URLs into disposable Settings before checking readiness, the release doctor fails fast when a local required HTTP provider is selected without its endpoint URL, and the standalone Commerce, nested Settings, and release workflows forward the same vars. This keeps HTTP certification from falsely passing the pre-run doctor while still leaving real provider execution Partial until the gate runs against configured endpoints/accounts.
- **Settings HTTP commerce runbook update**: The Settings Infrastructure release-certification runbook now names the same HTTP commerce endpoint aliases as the workflows and release doctor, and the Settings smoke plus provider-certification preflight guard that `COMMERCE_TAX_PROVIDER_URL`, `COMMERCE_SHIPPING_PROVIDER_URL`, `COMMERCE_DISCOUNT_PROVIDER_URL`, `COMMERCE_PRODUCT_SYNC_URL`, and `COMMERCE_SUBSCRIPTION_ACTION_URL` remain visible in the admin UI before operators run selected HTTP provider certification.
- **Backy release certification workflow**: `npm run test:release-certification-preflight-contract` now guards the manual `.github/workflows/backy-release-certification.yml` release gate, root package script wiring, audit evidence, and the `SETUP.md` runbook. The workflow runs local preflight contracts first, writes a non-secret GitHub job summary for requested gates, aggregate preflight/admin source guard coverage, database target-guard configuration, Settings/Commerce external-target booleans, and selected provider families, then optionally runs `ci:forms-postgres`, `ci:sdk-postgres-smoke`, `ci:settings-provider-certification`, and `ci:commerce-provider-certification` gates using explicit manual inputs for database, Settings providers, Commerce providers, storage provisioning, storage credential rotation, Vercel secret planning, notification diagnostics, provider-family selectors for storage, Vercel project/team, notification, payment, tax, shipping, discount, catalog, subscription, and commerce webhook certification, and the `disposable_database_confirmed` database safety acknowledgement. The release env now sets `BACKY_DATA_MODE` to `database` and forwards `BACKY_RELEASE_CERTIFY_DATABASE=1` only when `certify_database=true`, and otherwise leaves provider-only certification in demo mode unless an external certification base URL is configured. It also forwards `BACKY_SETTINGS_CERTIFY_STORAGE_PROVIDER`, `BACKY_SETTINGS_CERTIFY_VERCEL_PROJECT_ID`, `BACKY_SETTINGS_CERTIFY_VERCEL_TEAM_ID`, `BACKY_SETTINGS_CERTIFY_NOTIFICATION_PROVIDER`, and every `BACKY_COMMERCE_CERTIFY_*_PROVIDER` selector so top-level release certification can prove the intended live provider families rather than relying on auto-detected credentials. `SETUP.md` now documents the required `BACKY_DATABASE_URL` or `DATABASE_URL` disposable Supabase/Postgres database, `disposable_database_confirmed=true`, the provider-only demo-mode behavior when database certification is skipped, non-secret local/external certification-target evidence, and the storage/Supabase/S3, Vercel, notification, and commerce provider secret groups needed before the workflow can move the remaining Partial rows to live-certified.
- **Release certification readiness doctor**: `npm run doctor:release-certification` now emits a non-secret `backy.release-certification-doctor.v1` JSON report for database target-guard status, database URL format validity, disposable confirmation state, certification readiness, requested Settings/Commerce provider groups, local/external target mode, selected provider families, missing env variable names, failure labels, and optional redacted certification artifact verification. It emits `partialGateMap` for the current four Partial rows (`/settings`, Settings admin APIs, `/products`, and `/orders`) with each local gate, source-only guard, aggregate preflight, admin source guard, workflow, input-family map, and artifact verification env/schema, and emits `certifiedGates` for the already-certified `/forms` and Frontend manifest/OpenAPI/SDK database gates so those stay in release regression without being mislabeled as remaining Partial work. `BACKY_RELEASE_CERTIFICATION_DOCTOR_REQUIRED=1` turns missing requested groups into a nonzero exit before operators launch the live certification workflows, including missing `BACKY_DATABASE_DISPOSABLE_CONFIRMED=true`, malformed database URL failures, Settings-required/no-provider-group, Commerce-required/no-provider-group, malformed external base URL failures, external admin failures for missing deployed-target Settings or Commerce admin-key aliases, and configured artifact files that are missing or fail schema/no-secret validation. `BACKY_PROVIDER_CERTIFICATION_ARTIFACTS_REQUIRED=1` with `BACKY_SETTINGS_CERTIFICATION_ARTIFACT_PATH` and `BACKY_COMMERCE_CERTIFICATION_ARTIFACT_PATH` can require both saved workflow artifacts after certification, while per-artifact required flags can verify only one side. Artifact verification now rejects raw secret-like values through `noRawSecretValuesReady`; Settings artifact verification also checks `apiHandoffs.settingsAdminApi` and fails stale artifacts unless `settingsApiHandoffReady`, `settingsScenarioEvidenceReady`, and `settingsEvidencePacketReady` prove `GET /api/admin/settings`; Commerce artifact verification also checks `apiHandoffs` and fails stale artifacts unless `apiHandoffReady`, `publicCommerceApiHandoffReady`, `productApiHandoffReady`, and `orderApiHandoffReady` prove the selected site's public commerce contracts, product provider-sync, and order analytics APIs. `test:release-certification-doctor-contract` proves default success plus required database, malformed database URL, missing database disposable confirmation, confirmed disposable database readiness, missing Settings group, selected S3 storage, missing Commerce group, selected Stripe payment credential failure behavior, external Settings/Commerce base-URL/admin-key failures, alias-ready paths, valid/missing/invalid artifact verification, stale Settings artifact verification, raw secret-like value failures, leaked-secret artifact rejection, stale Commerce artifact verification, Settings and Commerce API-handoff artifact readiness, and Settings notification aliases for Resend, SMTP, and HTTP webhook delivery. The standalone Forms, SDK, Settings provider, Commerce provider, and top-level release certification workflows now set required mode before live database/provider gates so missing requested credential groups fail at the non-secret doctor step. The doctor follows the Settings runtime notification contract by accepting `RESEND_API_KEY`, host-only `SMTP_HOST`, and `BACKY_TRANSACTIONAL_EMAIL_WEBHOOK_URL` where the live Settings certification runner accepts them, and the release plus standalone Settings provider workflows forward those alias-named secrets/vars into the certification environment alongside the Backy-prefixed names. The release, standalone Settings provider, and standalone Commerce provider workflows run the doctor after source preflights so manual certification logs include the same safe readiness report before database/provider gates begin.
- **Forms Postgres disposable-target guard**: `npm run ci:forms-postgres` now requires `BACKY_DATABASE_DISPOSABLE_CONFIRMED=true` inside the Forms Postgres smoke itself before any target guard, schema check, or repository write runs. The standalone Forms and release certification workflows forward the existing `disposable_database_confirmed` input into that env var, and the Forms API/UI persistence-certification handoff plus setup/API docs now expose the required confirmation alongside `BACKY_DATABASE_URL`/`DATABASE_URL`. `test:forms-postgres-disposable-guard` proves the smoke rejects a valid-looking Postgres URL without the confirmation and can exit its self-test path before schema work once the confirmation is present.
- **SDK Postgres disposable-target guard**: `npm run ci:sdk-postgres-smoke` now requires `BACKY_DATABASE_DISPOSABLE_CONFIRMED=true` inside `scripts/sdk-smoke-ci.mjs` before expected target guards, schema checks, or public app startup run. The standalone SDK and release certification workflows forward the existing `disposable_database_confirmed` input into that env var, and the public manifest/OpenAPI/schema/SDK handoff plus setup/API docs expose the required confirmation alongside `BACKY_DATABASE_URL`/`DATABASE_URL`. `test:sdk-postgres-disposable-guard` proves the smoke rejects a valid-looking Postgres URL without the confirmation and can exit its self-test path before schema work once the confirmation is present.
- **Release certification provider-matrix guard**: `test:release-certification-doctor-contract` now behavior-tests selected Commerce certification failures across the current provider matrix instead of only representative families: Stripe, PayPal, Paddle, Square, Adyen, Mollie, and Razorpay for subscription/payment credential readiness; TaxJar and Avalara for tax; EasyPost and Shippo for shipping; Shopify, BigCommerce, WooCommerce, Etsy, and Magento for catalog sync; plus provider-managed webhook secrets. The doctor now matches the Commerce certification runner's auto-provider inference more closely by requiring complete provider credential sets before reporting auto payment, subscription, tax, shipping, or catalog readiness, including alias/base-url alternatives for providers such as Razorpay, Avalara, and Shopify. It now also behavior-tests selected provider-native alias success cases across payment, subscription, tax, shipping, and catalog families, including Stripe, PayPal, Paddle, Square, Adyen, Mollie, Razorpay, TaxJar, Avalara, EasyPost, Shippo, Shopify, BigCommerce, WooCommerce, Etsy, and Magento. `test:release-certification-preflight-contract` source-guards those labels so the remaining `/products`, `/orders`, `/settings`, Settings admin API, and live-provider certification blockers cannot silently drift back to broad auto-provider checks.
