- **Public checkout provider quote update**: `POST /api/sites/:siteId/commerce/orders` now supports Settings-selected first-class public checkout quote providers beyond generic HTTP. Storefront checkout can execute TaxJar and Avalara tax quotes, EasyPost and Shippo shipping-rate quotes, and Stripe promotion-code discount lookup using server-only environment credentials and mockable provider base URLs, then persists provider-adjusted subtotal/discount/shipping/tax/total metadata on the private order. `apps/admin/scripts/commerce-smoke.mjs` now includes mock-backed public checkout executions for TaxJar+EasyPost and Avalara+Shippo, both with Stripe promotion-code discounts, and verifies provider-adjusted totals plus the actual mock provider request paths. `npm run ci:commerce-provider-smoke`, `npm run typecheck --workspace @backy/public`, `npm run test:public-security --workspace @backy/public`, and `npm run test:settings-admin-contract` pass after this coverage update.
- **Commerce product workflow webhook update**: `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/commerce/products/:productId/provider-sync` and `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/commerce/products/:productId/subscriptions/:orderId/action` now dispatch `site-updated` webhooks after successful provider catalog sync and product-scoped subscription lifecycle actions in both demo and repository modes. Payloads include compact product/order before/after summaries, provider status, execution mode, subscription action metadata, request id, actor, `content`/`collections`/`commerce` changed-key metadata, and route-specific source markers so storefront builds, product automation, subscription status panels, cache workers, and external commerce integrations can react to Backy-owned provider workflow mutations without scraping order/product records.
- **Commerce order workflow webhook update**: `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/commerce/orders/:orderId/quote`, `/tracking`, and `/fulfillment` now dispatch `site-updated` webhooks after successful quote refreshes, tracking refreshes, and fulfillment dispatches in both demo and repository modes. Payloads include compact order before/after summaries, quote totals/provider adjustment status, tracking status/provider metadata, fulfillment dispatch metadata, request id, actor, `content`/`collections`/`commerce` changed-key metadata, and route-specific source markers so custom admin frontends, storefront cache workers, order-status integrations, and provider automation listeners can react to order execution workflow changes.
- **Commerce order label/refund webhook update**: `POST|DELETE /api/admin/sites/:siteId/commerce/orders/:orderId/shipping-label` and `POST|PATCH /api/admin/sites/:siteId/commerce/orders/:orderId/provider-refund` now dispatch `site-updated` webhooks after successful label preparation, label voiding, provider refund requests, and provider refund refreshes in both demo and repository modes. Payloads include compact order before/after summaries, label or refund status/provider/amount metadata, request id, actor, `content`/`collections`/`commerce` changed-key metadata, and route-specific source markers so storefront cache workers, order-status views, fulfillment automation, and refund integrations can react to label/refund execution changes without scraping collection records.
- **Commerce/orders action contract update**: the shipping-label read/create/void API, fulfillment dispatch/read API, tracking refresh/read API, and provider-refund read/create/refresh API now return private/no-store Backy contract headers for `backy.shipping-label.v1`, `backy.fulfillment-dispatch.v1`, `backy.tracking.v1`, and `backy.provider-refund.v1`, matching the quote and analytics endpoints so custom admin clients can rely on consistent schema/cache metadata across order execution actions.
- **SDK commerce admin operations bridge**: `packages/sdk-js` now exposes typed authenticated methods for the remaining commerce admin handoff routes: order quote read/refresh, tracking read/refresh, provider-refund read/create/refresh, fulfillment read/dispatch, shipping-label read/create/void, site reconciliation dry-runs, product provider-sync, and product subscription lifecycle read/action calls. Generated type fixtures and SDK smoke coverage exercise the read/dry-run paths without exposing provider secrets or mutating live provider accounts, making the `/products` and `/orders` custom-admin API bridge closer to the Wix/WordPress-style external frontend goal while live provider certification remains Partial.
- **Commerce/orders reconciliation contract update**: site reconciliation, platform batch reconciliation, and cron-readiness APIs now expose private/no-store Backy contract headers with `backy.commerce-reconciliation.v1`, `backy.commerce-reconciliation-batch.v1`, and `backy.commerce-reconciliation-readiness.v1`, closing the remaining order-operations response metadata gap for scheduled/manual repair workflows.
- **Commerce/orders webhook contract update**: commerce provider webhook settlement now returns private/no-store Backy contract headers for `backy.commerce-webhook.v1` on success, duplicate, and error paths so provider integrations and custom dashboards can rely on the same response metadata as the rest of the order execution surface.
- **Commerce/orders handoff contract update**: `/orders` now renders an Order API response contracts panel and includes an `apiContracts` block in the exported handoff manifest, mapping analytics, quote, shipping-label, fulfillment, tracking, provider-refund, webhook settlement, site reconciliation, platform reconciliation, and cron-readiness endpoints to their `backy.*.v1` schema versions, private cache scope, supported methods, and response headers for custom admin frontends.
- **Commerce/orders smoke stability update**: `/orders` now honors `orderId` deep links even when the target order is not present in the first paginated order batch, fetching the record directly and prepending it to the workspace state. The route also refreshes the selected deep-linked order when the URL search state changes, which keeps provider-refund refresh cards visible after smoke/API mutations update the record outside the initial list load. `apps/admin/scripts/orders-smoke.mjs` now deep-links to the mutated order before asserting the provider-refund refresh action, and `npm run ci:commerce-provider-smoke` passes end-to-end for commerce plus orders provider mock coverage, including the previously failing provider-refund refresh path.
- **Template registry admin contract update**: `GET /api/admin/sites/:siteId/templates` now exposes a normalized `backy.template-registry.v1` admin registry backed by persisted `site.settings.frontendDesign.templates`. The response groups page, blog post, form, product, collection, and reusable-section templates by type, includes content summaries, binding hint counts, canvas metadata, and returns exact `frontendDesignTemplateId` clone payloads for the existing page/blog/form/reusable-section/collection/product create APIs. `test:frontend-contract` source-guards the route, helper, clone targets, and API docs so admin surfaces and custom frontend builders can discover the template registry without parsing the full frontend-design contract. `test:template-registry` behavior-tests registry grouping, filtering, summaries, and clone payloads for page, form, and product templates.
- **Template registry admin UI update**: `/sites/:siteId` now surfaces a Template registry summary inside the Frontend design contract panel, including the registry endpoint, `backy.template-registry.v1` schema, `frontendDesignTemplateId` clone field, clone target count, and supported page/blog/form/product/collection/section template types. `test:site-detail` checks that the operator-facing panel renders the registry summary beside capture/save controls, while typechecks keep the admin response contract aligned with the public API response.
- **Template registry operator list update**: the same `/sites/:siteId` Frontend design contract panel now lists captured templates from the current design contract with type labels, clone endpoints, and route patterns, so operators can inspect page/blog/form/product/collection/section template availability without editing raw JSON. `test:site-detail` verifies the list after saving a custom frontend-design contract, including visible page/blog template names and clone-target URL text.
- **Template registry workspace action update**: captured templates listed on `/sites/:siteId` now include operator actions that deep-link page and blog templates into `/pages/new` and `/blog/new` with the selected `frontendDesignTemplateId`, matching the `backy.template-registry.v1` clone field, while form, product, collection, and section templates route to their existing site-scoped template workspaces with a `frontendTemplate` search id that highlights the selected captured template on arrival. `test:site-detail` checks all six captured page/blog/form/product/collection/section template types and their operator actions beside the saved custom frontend-design contract so the registry is not only informational.
- **Template registry deep-link guard update**: `test:frontend-contract` now source-guards that `/sites/:siteId` passes `frontendTemplate` into form/product/collection/section workspaces, that those workspaces validate and highlight the selected captured template, and that the collection draft route preserves the selected frontend template when `draft=new` resets the schema form.
- **Template registry runtime smoke update**: `test:frontend-design` now verifies unauthenticated `/api/admin/sites/:siteId/templates` access is rejected, the frontend-design response advertises `endpoints.templates` plus the `backy.template-registry.v1` summary, and authenticated registry reads return grouped page/form/product clone payloads, field/element summaries, clone targets, and filtered `type=product&search=product` results after a six-template frontend-design contract is patched.
- **SDK frontend-design/template admin bridge update**: `packages/sdk-js` now exposes `adminFrontendDesign()`, `updateAdminFrontendDesign()`, `importAdminFrontendDesign()`, `captureAdminSiteDefaults()`, `captureAdminContentTemplate()`, and `adminTemplates()` for custom builder shells. The helpers use the same admin-session/API-key/bearer auth options as live-management, type the normalized `backy.template-registry.v1` registry, preserve `frontendDesignTemplateId` clone bodies for page/blog/form/product/collection/section creation, and are covered by SDK generated-type checks plus smoke read assertions.
- **Blog list revision summary update**: `/blog` now matches `/pages` list parity for revision visibility. The blog post table loads `/api/admin/sites/:siteId/blog/:postId/revisions?limit=1`, renders count/latest-note/latest-status cells with `#blog-editor-revisions` deep links, refreshes row summaries after list-row SEO edits, and includes revision metadata in CSV plus frontend handoff JSON. `apps/admin/scripts/blog-list-smoke.mjs` now seeds a saved post revision and checks the rendered revision summary beside row SEO/comment controls.
- **Blog template handoff alias update**: `/blog/new` now accepts `designTemplate`, `frontendDesignTemplateId`, and `frontendTemplate` as equivalent captured frontend-design blog template query fields. The template-apply effect waits until blog-create edit readiness before inserting the frontend wrapper/content into the canvas, preventing deep-linked templates from only selecting the panel card while leaving the canvas on the starter article. `apps/admin/scripts/blog-create-smoke.mjs` source-guards the aliases and full browser smoke now exercises `frontendDesignTemplateId` through canvas insertion, autosave/recovery, mobile override authoring, and persisted post metadata.
- **Forms template export update**: `/forms` now exports the full built-in plus captured frontend form template library as a downloadable `backy.form-template-pack.v1` JSON artifact. The handoff manifest advertises `templateExport` counts/format, the template panel exposes a permission-gated `forms-template-pack-download-button`, and `apps/admin/scripts/forms-smoke.mjs` source-guards the schema, handoff field, and generated `*-backy-form-template-pack.json` filename so form-builder presets are exportable instead of only copyable one card at a time.
- **Settings provider-certification handoff export**: `/settings` now exposes the provider certification matrix as its own downloadable/copyable `backy.settings-provider-certification-handoff.v1` JSON artifact in the release-certification runbook. The handoff includes non-secret runtime evidence, saved provider metadata, required input aliases, gate commands, and evidence expectations for Supabase/Postgres, storage, Vercel, notification, and commerce provider families so live-certification runs can attach the exact Settings readiness contract without exporting provider secrets.
- **Products provider-certification handoff export**: `/products` now exposes its live commerce provider matrix as a dedicated downloadable/copyable `backy.commerce-provider-certification-handoff.v1` JSON artifact. The artifact includes non-secret site/catalog readiness, visible product counts, storefront/order/provider-sync endpoint evidence, Settings/runtime commerce evidence, provider-family configured/missing lists, required credential aliases, mock/live gate names, the required-mode operator gate, preflight/doctor commands, selected provider-family env hints, and expected evidence for payment, tax, shipping, discount, catalog-sync, webhook, and subscription provider certification without leaking provider secrets.
- **Orders provider-certification handoff export**: `/orders` now exposes its live commerce provider matrix as a dedicated downloadable/copyable `backy.commerce-provider-certification-handoff.v1` JSON artifact. The artifact includes non-secret order readiness, metrics, visible order counts, provider analytics, runtime readiness checks, configured/missing provider-family evidence, missing runtime aliases, admin/order/webhook/reconciliation endpoint evidence, required credential aliases, mock/live gate names, the required-mode operator gate, preflight/doctor commands, selected provider-family env hints, and expected evidence for checkout settlement, quote recalculation, carrier label/tracking, fulfillment dispatch, provider refunds, webhooks, and reconciliation without leaking provider secrets.
- **Orders operation action-plan handoff**: `/orders` now renders a visible `orders-action-plan` summary and includes `operationActionPlan` in its handoff manifest with `backy.order-operation-action-plan-summary.v1`. Each exported order row carries a `backy.order-operation-action-plan.v1` plan that classifies quote refresh, label preparation, tracking refresh, fulfillment dispatch, provider refunds, and provider-refund refresh as provider-ready, manual-handoff, or blocked; order buttons surface the same disabled reasons and recommendation text.
- **Commerce subscription certification handoff alignment**: the public commerce storefront contract plus `/products` and `/orders` provider-certification exports now expose the same subscription lifecycle provider family that the live gate and command builders can certify, including Stripe, PayPal, Paddle, Square, Adyen, Mollie, Razorpay, HTTP, and manual handoff evidence. The same handoff now names the explicit HTTP discount alias `BACKY_COMMERCE_DISCOUNT_PROVIDER_URL`/`COMMERCE_DISCOUNT_PROVIDER_URL` instead of only referring to a Settings endpoint, so custom admin and storefront clients can render the exact remaining credential inputs for discount and subscription certification.
- **Forms persistence-certification handoff export**: `/forms` now exposes its Supabase/Postgres persistence certification metadata as a dedicated downloadable/copyable `backy.forms-persistence-certification.v1` JSON artifact. The artifact names the local Forms/admin and repository checks, the exact operator gate `BACKY_DATABASE_DISPOSABLE_CONFIRMED=true npm run ci:forms-postgres`, the lower-level `npm run test:forms-postgres --workspace @backy/db` and `npm run ci:forms-postgres` gates, preflight/disposable guard commands, workflow path, accepted `BACKY_DATABASE_URL`/`DATABASE_URL` aliases, `BACKY_DATABASE_DISPOSABLE_CONFIRMED=true`, target-guard env vars, disposable migrated database confirmation, migration/RLS requirements, API-derived runtime readiness evidence, expected non-secret evidence attachments, missing runtime inputs, and non-secret credential boundary without exporting database URLs.
- **Dashboard handoff contract update**: `/` now exports a named `backy.dashboard-handoff.v1` JSON contract instead of an anonymous dashboard blob. The handoff includes generated timestamp, backend/fallback source, selected site, public/admin endpoint coverage, API consumer readiness, persistence/deployment health, commerce and moderation metrics, module routes, and operator attention issues so custom frontend/admin shells can consume dashboard state and launch blockers without scraping the UI. `apps/admin/scripts/dashboard-smoke.mjs` source-guards the schema/version/source/attention fields beside the existing browser layout, RBAC, deployment, infrastructure, mobile, and navigation checks.
- **Sites workspace coverage reconciliation**: the stale platform-gap Sites note has been reconciled against the current source. `/sites` exposes labelled backend and permission error states with retry, filter recovery, and permission retry actions; `/sites/new` exposes owner/team creation inputs, status selection, custom-domain DNS verification metadata, backend duplicate-slug/custom-domain validation notes, starter-page seeding, readiness checks, and a downloadable creation handoff manifest; `/sites/:siteId` exposes the site command center, domain verification panel, theme/publish status controls, readiness, navigation, redirects, SEO, frontend-design template registry, and downloadable workspace handoff manifest. `apps/admin/scripts/sites-smoke.mjs` and `apps/admin/scripts/site-detail-smoke.mjs` cover those workflows, while the admin site APIs enforce team scope, slug uniqueness, and domain verification persistence.
- **Site workflow offline-state update**: `/sites/:siteId` automation queues now route comment/report/event public API HTML, offline, and JSON parser failures through a guarded workflow response reader and a shared Notice with the expected public API origin. The panel names `VITE_BACKY_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL` as the configuration handoff instead of surfacing raw parser text when the public API server is unavailable, and `test:site-detail` source-guards the setup message, guarded reader, and workflow error test id.
- **Pages workspace coverage reconciliation**: the stale platform-gap Pages note has been reconciled against the current source. `/pages` uses the admin pages APIs for list, delete, bulk publish/archive/delete, page quota enforcement, route/readiness/delivery diagnostics, revision summary links, hierarchy display, viewer RBAC disabling, labelled backend/permission error states with retry, filter recovery, user-access handoff actions, and handoff/CSV export evidence, plus direct starter links for landing, contact, newsletter, survey, registration, member-login, member-account, storefront, product-detail, pricing, services, booking, portfolio, gallery, events, privacy, terms, cookie-policy, accessibility-statement, refund-policy, shipping-policy, cart, checkout, order-confirmation, help-center, FAQ, testimonials, blog-index, blog-post, team, careers, and about pages. `/pages/new` persists title, slug, status/schedule, parent placement, SEO/social metadata, dataset imports, starter templates including the landing page, newsletter signup page, survey response page, email-only member-login access page, authenticated member-account page, buy-ready product-detail page, pricing plan page, services/booking page, dedicated appointment booking page, media-backed portfolio page, central-media gallery page, events/RSVP page, privacy/legal policy page, terms/legal conditions page, cookie policy page, accessibility statement page, refund policy page, shipping policy page, cart review page, safe provider-handoff checkout page, customer-safe order confirmation page, self-service help-center page, FAQ answer page, testimonials/reviews page, blog index page, blog article detail page, team/profile page, careers/open-roles page, and about/story page, frontend-design templates, and navigation placement through `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/pages` before opening the backend-backed editor. `/pages/:pageId/edit` and the admin page APIs cover conflict-safe `expectedUpdatedAt` saves, `PAGE_VERSION_CONFLICT`, preview, publish, archive, rollback, revision history, webhook/cache invalidation, and public delivery handoff. `apps/admin/scripts/pages-list-smoke.mjs`, `apps/admin/scripts/page-create-smoke.mjs`, and the editor smoke source cover those workflows.
- **Page create submit-state observability**: `/pages/new` now exposes a visible `page-create-submit-blocker` status plus machine-readable submit/preview `data-state`, `data-blocker`, `data-can-submit`, and `data-can-preview` attributes on the create controls. This keeps disabled Create Page and Save draft/preview states explainable to authors, smoke tests, and custom admin clients while route, permission, schedule, collection, and SEO readiness checks are pending or blocked.
- **Hosted public route-state update**: the public Next app now provides Backy-owned route-state boundaries for 404 `NOT_FOUND`, 401 `AUTH_REQUIRED`, 403 `FORBIDDEN`, and runtime `INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR` instead of framework-default hosted page states. The shared boundary exposes `data-backy-public-route-state`, status, error code, and `backy.public-route-state.v1` contract attributes so custom frontend shells and analytics can recognize recovery states, while `apps/public/scripts/public-security-regression-smoke.mjs` source-guards the contract.
- **Public localized route-resolution update**: public render and resolve APIs now apply the manifest-advertised localization rules instead of resolving raw paths only. Path-prefix locales strip `/fr`-style prefixes before matching pages, blog posts, redirects, and collection routes, domain locales resolve from `host`/`domain` query context or forwarded host headers, route resources return localized canonical/render URLs, and render payloads expose localized `site.locale`, `content.locale`, `route.params.locale`, and canonical path metadata. `apps/public/scripts/public-security-regression-smoke.mjs` source-guards the route wiring, while `npm run test:localized-routes --workspace @backy/public` covers prefix/domain behavior.
- **Hosted custom/locale domain route update**: hosted catch-all HTML pages now read request host context through `next/headers`, resolve demo and database sites by custom domain, Settings verification domain, or locale domain before falling back to the managed slug, and pass that host into the shared route resolver for both metadata and rendered pages. The public security smoke source-guards the host-aware hosted resolver path, while the localized route smoke covers the shared host normalization/matching helper.
- **Page editor focus-mode availability update**: `/pages/:pageId/edit` now keeps the Focus canvas/Show panels workspace toggle available while preview generation and readiness checks run, matching the blog editor's uninterrupted design workflow. Loading, restore/publish/archive workflow, and permission bootstrap locks still disable the toggle, and `apps/admin/scripts/editor-drag-smoke.mjs` source-guards that narrower focus-mode busy state while page-create browser coverage verifies the rendered focus transition.
- **Page create template-selection availability update**: `/pages/new` now keeps starter and captured frontend template selection available while background route checks run, so authors can keep exploring designs during non-destructive readiness work. Page creation, preview draft creation, loading, and permission bootstrap locks still block mutations, and `apps/admin/scripts/page-create-smoke.mjs` source-guards the narrower template-selection busy state.
- **Blog workspace coverage reconciliation**: the stale platform-gap Blog note has been reconciled against the current source. `/blog` uses the admin blog APIs for post list/delete/bulk actions, category/tag/author filtering, taxonomy CRUD, public search/archive/feed snippets, SEO/comment row controls, revision summaries, CSV export, handoff JSON, and labelled backend/permission error states with retry, filter recovery, and user-access handoff actions. `/blog/new` persists title, slug, excerpt, featured media, author, taxonomy, status/schedule, SEO, frontend-design template provenance, and canvas content through the backend before preview/edit work. `/blog/:postId` and the admin blog APIs cover canvas editing, focus mode, template provenance, taxonomy/readiness/publish/comments/handoff/revisions panels, conflict-safe `expectedUpdatedAt` updates, preview, publish, archive, rollback, revisions, RSS/public feed delivery, webhook dispatch, and cache invalidation. `apps/admin/scripts/blog-list-smoke.mjs`, `apps/admin/scripts/blog-create-smoke.mjs`, and `apps/admin/scripts/blog-editor-smoke.mjs` cover those workflows.
- **Blog editor focus-mode availability update**: `/blog/:postId` now keeps the Focus canvas/Show panels workspace toggle available while route availability and readiness checks run, so authors can keep designing during backend diagnostics. Loading, save, workflow, and permission bootstrap locks still disable the toggle, and `apps/admin/scripts/blog-editor-smoke.mjs` source-guards that narrower focus-mode busy state while the browser smoke verifies the rendered focus transition.
- **Collections workspace coverage reconciliation**: the stale platform-gap Collections note has been reconciled against the current source. `/collections` uses the admin collections APIs for schema/record CRUD, CSV import/export, backup import/export, dynamic page/template handoff, relationship browsing, audit activity, and labelled backend/permission error states with retry, filter recovery, user-access handoff actions, and permission-contract retry actions. `apps/admin/scripts/collections-smoke.mjs` source-guards the empty-state, backend-error, and permission-error contracts.
- **Forms workspace error-state update**: `/forms` now exposes source-guarded backend and permission error states with alert semantics, retry, filter recovery, user-access handoff actions, and permission retry actions after the broader Forms row moved to Ready with external Supabase/Postgres persistence certification on 2026-05-21.
- **Products workspace error-state update**: `/products` now exposes source-guarded backend and permission error states with alert semantics, retry, catalog filter recovery, user-access handoff actions, and permission retry actions while the broader Products row remains Partial for credentialed live commerce provider certification.
- **Orders workspace error-state update**: `/orders` now exposes source-guarded backend and permission error states with alert semantics, retry, order filter recovery, user-access handoff actions, and permission retry actions while the broader Orders row remains Partial for credentialed live payment, refund, shipping, and fulfillment provider certification.
- **Settings workspace permission-state update**: `/settings` now exposes source-guarded permission error states with alert semantics and user-access handoff actions through the shared Notice primitive, while the broader Settings rows remain Partial for credentialed Supabase, Vercel, storage, notification, and commerce provider certification.
- **Editor history shortcut metadata update**: undo and redo toolbar controls now expose stable action ids plus shortcut metadata for Cmd/Ctrl+Z, Cmd/Ctrl+Y, and Shift+Cmd/Ctrl+Z. `apps/admin/scripts/editor-drag-smoke.mjs` source-guards the history command affordances alongside the existing keyboard handler.
- **Editor zoom shortcut update**: the shared canvas editor now supports Cmd/Ctrl+=, Cmd/Ctrl+-, and Cmd/Ctrl+0 for zoom in, zoom out, and fit canvas, with matching `aria-keyshortcuts` and nonvisual HUD metadata for custom admin clients. `apps/admin/scripts/editor-drag-smoke.mjs` source-guards the keyboard handler and zoom control metadata.
- **Editor pan shortcut update**: the shared canvas editor now supports H for persistent hand/pan mode while preserving the existing hold-Space temporary pan path. The pan HUD exposes matching shortcut metadata for custom admin clients, and `apps/admin/scripts/editor-drag-smoke.mjs` source-guards the keyboard handler plus pan control contract.
- **Editor grid/snap shortcut update**: the shared canvas editor now supports G for grid visibility and S for snapping while preserving Cmd/Ctrl+S save. The grid/snap HUD exposes matching shortcut metadata for custom admin clients, and `apps/admin/scripts/editor-drag-smoke.mjs` source-guards the keyboard handler plus grid/snap control metadata.
- **Editor shortcut runtime coverage update**: focused browser smokes now exercise Ctrl/Cmd zoom in/out/fit, H persistent pan, hold-Space temporary pan, G grid visibility, and S snapping against live HUD state and canvas behavior, while the source-only guard verifies those runtime assertions stay wired.
- **Editor shell panel shortcut update**: the shared canvas editor now exposes stable shell actions and shortcut metadata for Components (B), Inspector (I), Layers (L), and Focus (F). The component, inspector, layers, and focus commands share explicit handlers, and Layers/Inspector keyboard commands exit focus mode to show the requested panel. `apps/admin/scripts/editor-drag-smoke.mjs` source-guards the metadata and exercises the panel/focus transitions in the keyboard shortcuts browser smoke.
- **Editor grouping shortcut accessibility update**: the editor group and ungroup toolbar plus inspector controls now expose stable action ids and `aria-keyshortcuts` for Cmd/Ctrl+G and Shift+Cmd/Ctrl+G, including the single selected-group ungroup action. `apps/admin/scripts/editor-drag-smoke.mjs` can run a source-only guard for the grouping shortcut accessibility contract.
- **Editor nested child-selection update**: the shared page/post/section canvas editor now exposes a direct child-layer selection command for selected containers and groups. Authors can use the toolbar button, inspector button, or Shift+Cmd/Ctrl+A to select all visible unlocked direct children before grouping, alignment, duplication, delete, or layer-order commands, and `apps/admin/scripts/editor-drag-smoke.mjs` source-guards the toolbar, inspector, and shortcut contract.
- **Editor sibling-scope selection update**: select-all-siblings now exposes Cmd/Ctrl+A shortcut metadata in the toolbar and is available from both single and multi-layer inspector cards. This keeps same-parent selection setup local to the properties workflow before grouping, alignment, duplicate, delete, and layer-order operations.
- **Editor distribution control labelling update**: multi-selection inspector distribution controls now expose explicit horizontal and vertical labels, and the editor source guard covers toolbar plus inspector distribution controls so spacing operations remain discoverable during nested component layout work.
- **Editor multi-group ungroup update**: the shared canvas editor now treats ungroup as a true multi-selection command when every selected layer is an unlocked editor group in the same parent scope. Shift+Cmd/Ctrl+G, the toolbar, and the inspector can expand all selected groups in one history-tracked operation, preserve child geometry/responsive metadata, and select the expanded children for the next alignment, grouping, duplicate, or delete action.
- **Editor common-parent selection update**: multi-selected child layers in the same nested scope now keep a parent-selection command in the inspector and through the existing Shift+Enter shortcut path. Single-selection inspector parent, child, and child-scope controls now expose shortcut metadata for Shift+Enter, Enter, and Shift+Cmd/Ctrl+A, keeping child-scope selection, grouping, ungrouping, alignment, duplicate, and delete workflows navigable in complex page components.
- **Editor multi-selection action update**: selected-layer clipboard and destructive actions now derive enabled states from the active sibling scope rather than just the primary selection, label single versus multi-layer actions in the toolbar, and expose copy, duplicate, cut, paste, and delete controls in the multi-selection inspector. This keeps nested child selections and grouped component selections actionable without forcing authors back to the top toolbar.
- **Editor paste-target and shortcut metadata update**: the shared canvas editor now exposes shortcut metadata for copy, cut, paste, duplicate, and delete controls in the toolbar and multi-selection inspector. Paste also publishes whether it will insert into the selected unlocked container/group or onto the canvas root via stable `data-paste-target` and target-id metadata, keeping nested component authoring behavior inspectable for smoke coverage and custom editor clients.
- **Editor single-selection inspector action update**: the selected-layer inspector now exposes copy, duplicate, cut, paste, and delete actions for a single selected layer, including shortcut metadata and the same paste-target attributes used by the toolbar. This makes common layer operations available while authors stay in the properties panel for nested component editing.
- **Editor deterministic clone-id update**: duplicate and copy/paste now remap root and nested element ids from the source id using collision-safe `-copy` suffixes instead of timestamp/random ids. The focused clipboard smoke verifies deterministic paste, redo, and duplicate selection ids, and the source guard blocks the clone helper from returning to `generateId()`.
- **Editor inspector visibility/lock update**: single and multi-layer inspector cards now expose hide/show and lock/unlock controls backed by the same selected-layer handlers as the toolbar. The controls preserve selected-layer labels, disabled states, and pressed state metadata so layer state changes remain local to the properties workflow during nested component editing.
- **Editor inspector layer-order update**: toolbar arrange controls now expose bracket shortcut metadata, and single plus multi-layer inspector cards expose local send-to-back, send-backward, bring-forward, and bring-to-front controls backed by the same `handleZOrderChange` path. This keeps layer stacking changes available while authors work in the properties panel.
- **Editor dataset action-plan update**: the Data panel now renders and exports selected-element `backy.editor.dataset-binding-action-plan.v1` and repeater `backy.editor.repeater-dataset-action-plan.v1` contracts. Authors can see collection, field, target, preview, query handoff, slot coverage, records URL, layout, and join-strategy readiness before publishing or handing a collection-bound section to a custom frontend.
- **Editor composition handoff update**: the shared canvas editor inspector now renders `backy.editor-composition-readiness.v1` metrics for total layers, groups, nested child layers, breakpoint overrides, and current selection scope, plus a copyable `backy.editor-composition-action-plan.v1` so grouped page/post/section component trees can be handed to custom admin or frontend builders without reverse-engineering the editor UI.
- **Media ownership coverage reconciliation**: the stale platform-gap Media note no longer treats per-site ownership enforcement as missing. The media admin route tree under `/api/admin/sites/:siteId/media` imports `requireAdminAccess` across list/upload, asset detail, signed URLs, folders, bind/unbind, transforms, retained versions, and provider analytics, with `media.view`, `media.create`, `media.edit`, `media.delete`, and `media.configure` gates applied by route type. `apps/admin/scripts/media-smoke.mjs` covers denied `media.edit` and `media.configure` workflows plus source-guards labelled delete confirmation dialogs, confirm/cancel action labels, visible focus rings for destructive asset, bulk asset, and folder actions, and the copyable media attribution handoff contract, while `apps/public/scripts/admin-site-scope-smoke.mjs` denies non-member access to nested media list/create/provider-analytics routes with `FORBIDDEN_SITE_SCOPE`. Remaining Media risk is external cloud provider-account automation, not ownership scoping or custom-frontend attribution handoff.
- **Blog authoring gap-summary reconciliation**: the stale top-level gap prose no longer lists categories, tags, authors, featured images, scheduling, revisions, or feeds as missing Blog primitives. Those are covered by the verified Blog list/create/editor routes and their admin/public APIs; the remaining Blog risk is now documented as canonical editor/content contract convergence and visual QA rather than absent editorial workflow controls.
- **Public delivery gap-summary reconciliation**: the stale public-delivery note no longer treats custom-domain discovery, CORS, domain metadata, or locale route variants as missing primitives. `GET /api/sites?identifier=` resolves published sites by id, slug, or custom domain in demo and repository modes; `GET /api/sites/:siteId/manifest` exposes managed/custom/verification domains, canonical bases, locale strategy, locale route variants, sitemap/robots URLs, and SDK database-certification metadata; `apps/public/src/proxy.ts` applies configured-origin CORS headers. Remaining public-delivery risk is hosted custom-domain/edge certification, with future feed additions required to stay inside the public envelope/cache contract.
- **Settings custom-frontend CORS handoff update**: `/settings` now includes `BACKY_CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS` in the deployment env contract, release-certification handoff manifest, and visible runbook copy so operators can configure exact browser origins for custom frontends without storing those origins as Backy settings. `GET /api/admin/settings` also returns non-secret `runtimePublicApi` evidence for normalized exact origins, origin count, wildcard denial, and exposed Backy contract headers, so the Settings env contract can show server-side CORS readiness. `apps/admin/scripts/settings-smoke.mjs` and `apps/public/scripts/settings-admin-contract-smoke.mjs` source-guard the public API env profile, CORS env key, runtime summary, and release runbook text while public CORS execution remains enforced by `apps/public/src/proxy.ts`.
- **Preview-token actor-binding update**: page and blog preview-token creation now binds the token record and tokenless `previewToken.create` audit metadata to the resolved admin session actor or service-key actor instead of trusting caller-provided actor headers. The admin contract smoke asserts page and post preview audits keep redacting the raw token while carrying concrete actor-binding metadata. Remaining preview risk is usage-audit hardening for public preview reads, not unaudited creation.
- **Preview-token usage audit update**: successful page, blog, render, resolve, and hosted HTML preview-token reads now record tokenless `previewToken.use` audit events with `actorId: "public-preview"`, target type/id, surface, path/slug metadata, and `tokenStored: false` in demo and repository modes. The admin contract smoke exercises tokenized page and post public API, render, and resolve reads after creating preview tokens, reads back the matching use-audit entries, and verifies the raw token is not stored in use metadata. Remaining public-delivery risk is hosted custom-domain/edge certification, with future public feed additions required to keep the same contract/cache headers.
- **Hosted feed error-contract update**: hosted sitemap, robots, and RSS route 404s for missing or unpublished sites now return through `publicContractResponse` with `x-backy-contract-version`, `x-backy-request-id`, `x-backy-cache-scope: error`, and `no-store` caching instead of bare text responses. `apps/public/scripts/admin-contract-smoke.mjs` guards the three hosted route sources against reintroducing bare `new NextResponse('Not found')` feed errors.
- **Frontend comments discovery update**: `GET /api/sites/:siteId/manifest` now exposes a structured `backy.comments-discovery.v1` comments module with resolved comment policy, public approved-list status, moderation statuses, report reasons, report/blocklist endpoint templates, and spam-protection field names. `frontend-manifest.schema.json`, generated SDK contract types, `BackyManifestCommentsModule`, `frontend-contract-smoke`, and SDK smoke source now guard the shape so generated/custom frontends can build discussion and moderation UI without hardcoding Backy comment route details.
- **SDK comment input builder update**: `packages/sdk-js` now exports `buildBackyCommentInput()` for generated/custom page and blog comment forms. The helper normalizes content aliases, author identity aliases, reply/thread ids, guest/user identity, honeypot, started-at, captcha-token, moderation-mode, request-id, and rate-limit metadata into the canonical payload accepted by `submitPageComment()` and `submitBlogComment()`. SDK generated-type checks, SDK smoke assertions, API docs, and the SDK README guard the helper after the Frontend manifest/OpenAPI/SDK APIs row moved to Ready with the disposable Supabase/Postgres SDK smoke gate on 2026-05-21.
- **SDK comment report builder update**: `packages/sdk-js` now exports `buildBackyCommentReportInput()` for generated/custom comment moderation and report UIs. The helper normalizes reason/category aliases, reporter identity aliases, details/message/note aliases, and request ids into the canonical `reportComment()` payload. The public report route now returns the normalized report object promised by OpenAPI and records report details in `comment-reported` event metadata for custom moderation dashboards. SDK generated-type checks, SDK smoke assertions, API docs, and the SDK README guard the helper after the Frontend manifest/OpenAPI/SDK APIs row moved to Ready with the disposable Supabase/Postgres SDK smoke gate on 2026-05-21.
- **SDK media binding builder update**: `packages/sdk-js` now exports `buildBackyMediaBindingInput()` and `bindMedia()` for authenticated custom editors that need to attach or detach central media-library assets from pages and blog posts. The helper normalizes target aliases such as `target.type`, `target.id`, `pageId`, `postId`, and `blogId`, usage/context aliases, editor/actor attribution, bind/unbind action aliases, and request ids into the canonical audited admin media binding payload. SDK generated-type checks, SDK smoke assertions, API docs, and the SDK README guard the helper after the Frontend manifest/OpenAPI/SDK APIs row moved to Ready with the disposable Supabase/Postgres SDK smoke gate on 2026-05-21.
- **SDK media signed URL builder update**: `packages/sdk-js` now exports `buildBackyMediaSignedUrlInput()` and `createMediaSignedUrl()` for authenticated custom editors that need temporary private media delivery links. The helper normalizes disposition aliases such as `download` into `attachment`, TTL aliases such as `ttl` and `expiresIn` into `expiresInSeconds`, request ids, and per-call admin credentials before using the existing `media.view` signed-url API. SDK generated-type checks, SDK smoke assertions, API docs, and the SDK README guard the helper after the Frontend manifest/OpenAPI/SDK APIs row moved to Ready with the disposable Supabase/Postgres SDK smoke gate on 2026-05-21.
- **SDK media admin management update**: `packages/sdk-js` now exposes authenticated central media-library management for custom editors through `adminMedia()`, `uploadMedia()`, `updateAdminMedia()`, `replaceMedia()`, `deleteAdminMedia()`, `adminMediaFolders()`, `createMediaFolder()`, `updateMediaFolder()`, and `deleteMediaFolder()`. The SDK request layer supports multipart `FormData` without forcing JSON content type, and the methods preserve folder, scope, visibility, font, tag, metadata, quota, and cache-invalidation envelopes used by the admin Media page. SDK generated-type checks, SDK smoke read assertions, API docs, and the SDK README guard the methods after the Frontend manifest/OpenAPI/SDK APIs row moved to Ready with the disposable Supabase/Postgres SDK smoke gate on 2026-05-21.
- **SDK media workflow admin bridge update**: `packages/sdk-js` now exposes retained-version list/restore/delete, responsive transform preparation, and provider analytics ingestion through `adminMediaVersions()`, `restoreMediaVersion()`, `deleteMediaVersion()`, `prepareMediaTransforms()`, and `ingestMediaProviderAnalytics()`. These helpers use the same admin-session/API-key/bearer auth options as live-management, return the audited media/quota/cache/provider-delivery envelopes, and add generated-type fixtures plus read-only retained-version smoke assertions so external media consoles can manage history, responsive assets, and delivery evidence without scraping `/media`.
- **SDK central media runtime coverage update**: `packages/sdk-js/scripts/smoke.mjs` now executes the central media lifecycle bridge against a temporary backend site, covering folder create/update/delete, public image upload, metadata update, page binding/unbinding, responsive transform preparation, file replacement with retained-version listing, private PDF upload, signed download URL creation, and media cleanup through the same SDK methods custom editors use.
- **Frontend media discovery update**: `GET /api/sites/:siteId/manifest` now exposes a structured `backy.media-discovery.v1` media module with list/font/detail/file/transform endpoint templates, type/scope/filter metadata, and capability flags for public assets, signed private files, responsive images, transforms, font manifests, references, and editable metadata. `frontend-manifest.schema.json`, generated SDK contract types, `BackyManifestMediaModule`, `frontend-contract-smoke`, and SDK smoke source now guard the shape so generated/custom frontends can build central media-library pickers without hardcoding Backy media route details.
- **Frontend media file-category discovery update**: the same manifest media module now exposes `fileCategories` under `backy.media-file-categories.v1` plus a `deliveryPolicy` for custom media pickers, and the site-scoped OpenAPI document mirrors it through `x-backy-media-file-categories` plus `MediaFileCategoryDiscovery`, `MediaFileCategory`, and `MediaDeliveryPolicy` schemas. It advertises image, video, audio, document/file, font, and other upload categories, accepted MIME/extensions, picker intent, transform/responsive/font-manifest eligibility, signed private-file URL requirements, accepted dispositions, and downloadable/transformable type groups. The public schema, OpenAPI source, SDK convenience type, generated SDK OpenAPI types, generated SDK contract fixtures, frontend source guard, SDK smoke assertions, API docs, and SDK README now guard the contract after the Frontend manifest/OpenAPI/SDK APIs row moved to Ready with the 2026-05-21 SDK Postgres service-data certification gate.
- **Frontend theme discovery update**: `GET /api/sites/:siteId/manifest` now exposes a structured `backy.theme-discovery.v1` theme module and uses the same compiled `backy.theme.v1` token contract as render payloads. The shared `@backy-cms/core` compiler emits ready-to-apply `--backy-*` CSS variables, a compiled stylesheet, token references, root/scoped selectors, editable theme field paths, element-level `tokenRefs` plus document-level `themeTokenRefs` inheritance metadata, and capability flags for custom CSS, typography families, spacing scale, live editing, frontend-design overrides, token references, motion animation refs, and compiled stylesheets. The admin property panel can now bind element styles to theme token refs while mirroring the resolved CSS variable into element props, bind animation duration/easing through `animation.tokenRefs`, and the public renderer resolves `tokenRefs` against the same compiler output before rendering. `frontend-manifest.schema.json`, generated SDK contract types, `BackyManifestThemeModule`, `frontend-contract-smoke`, SDK smoke source, and editor smoke source guards cover the shape so custom frontends can style from Backy without reimplementing theme compilation.
- **Frontend live-management discovery update**: `GET /api/sites/:siteId/manifest` now exposes a structured `backy.live-management.v1` module for authenticated custom-frontend editing. The module advertises the live page and blog post management bridges, GET/PATCH methods, session/API-key headers, `pages.view`/`pages.edit` permissions, site-scope enforcement, editable target paths, optimistic `expectedUpdatedAt` update guidance, and stable error codes including `PAGE_VERSION_CONFLICT` and `BLOG_VERSION_CONFLICT`. Hosted blog post routes now opt into the same resource-aware live overlay via `?backyManage=1`, using `/api/sites/:siteId/manage/blog/:postId` for title/status and inline canvas element updates. `frontend-manifest.schema.json`, generated SDK contract types, `BackyManifestLiveManagementModule`, `frontend-contract-smoke`, and SDK smoke source guard the shape so custom frontends can wire inline editing without scraping admin internals.
- **Frontend live-management media update**: The hosted live overlay now exposes inline editing for `video`, `embed`, and `map` canvas elements in addition to text, link, image, layout, and appearance controls. Authorized editors can patch media source URLs, video poster/playback flags, embed allowed-host/fullscreen/loading metadata, and map address/marker/zoom fields through `/api/sites/:siteId/manage/pages/:pageId` or `/api/sites/:siteId/manage/blog/:postId`, with the same lock handling, `expectedUpdatedAt` conflicts, and admin route validation as the full canvas editor. `apps/public/scripts/admin-contract-smoke.mjs` source-guards the media editor hook, supported element set, and media patch helper.
- **Frontend live-management form-control update**: the hosted live overlay now exposes inline editing for `form`, `input`, `textarea`, `select`, `checkbox`, and `radio` canvas elements. Authorized editors can patch form id/title/submit label/action/success message/active state plus field label/name/placeholder/help/default/options/input type/rows/required/disabled settings through the same live page/blog management bridges with lock handling, `expectedUpdatedAt` conflicts, and admin route validation. Manifest live-management `editableTargets`, OpenAPI `x-backy-live-management` plus `LiveManagementDiscovery`, generated OpenAPI update request types, SDK live-management helpers with per-call admin-session/API-key/bearer auth options, SDK external-editor element inventory plus single, bulk, direct render `editableMap`-style, keyed editable-map, editable-map values object, and conflict-safe update-body builder helpers for `props.*`, `styles.*`, `layout.*`, and `visibility.*` editable target paths, SDK smoke assertions for page and blog live-management reads/updates, API docs, and `apps/public/scripts/admin-contract-smoke.mjs` source-guard the form-control hook and patch helper so custom frontends can wire form-control editing without scraping the full admin editor.
- **Page/blog design-state persistence update**: `@backy-cms/core` now exposes `canvasContentPayloadToBackyContentDocument()` and the admin/live page and blog create/update paths use it in repository and demo-store modes. Canvas payloads preserve page-builder design state as canonical `backy.content.v1`: nested elements, responsive overrides, styles, canonical element animations with custom `from`/`to`, scroll triggers, and motion token refs, actions, data bindings, token refs, document assets/interactions/SEO/data bindings/editable maps/metadata, `customCSS`, `customJS`, and optional `contentDocument`. The site OpenAPI `PageUpdateRequest` and `BlogPostUpdateRequest` canvas-object schemas now advertise those fields, generated SDK types compile representative page/blog design-state updates, and SDK/frontend contract smoke guards the shape so Wix/Canva-style custom editors can save and later reopen each page or blog post without losing design metadata.
- **Frontend pages runtime discovery update**: `GET /api/sites/:siteId/manifest` now exposes a structured `backy.pages-discovery.v1` module for custom frontend page routing, public page reads, preview-token reads, render payloads, and live-management handoff. The module advertises page list/detail/resolve/render/live-manage endpoint templates, GET/PATCH methods, public page paths, published/scheduled counts, SEO/frontend-design capabilities, public/private cache boundaries, preview-token privacy rules, query filters, and stable page error markers including `PAGE_NOT_FOUND`. `frontend-manifest.schema.json`, generated SDK contract types, `BackyManifestPagesRuntimeModule`, `frontend-contract-smoke`, and SDK smoke source guard the shape so custom frontends can wire page routers and preview UI without hardcoding Backy page route details.
- **Frontend collections runtime discovery update**: `GET /api/sites/:siteId/manifest` now exposes a structured `backy.collections-discovery.v1` module for custom frontend collection schema, dynamic route, record read, and visitor-write UI. The module advertises collection/record endpoint templates, GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE methods, public read/create/update/delete counts, supported field types, cache/privacy policy, write-token/draft-create policy, visitor write-policy metadata, and stable validation/slug-conflict markers. `frontend-manifest.schema.json`, generated SDK contract types, `BackyManifestCollectionsRuntimeModule`, `frontend-contract-smoke`, and SDK smoke source guard the shape so custom frontends can build collection-driven pages and forms without hardcoding Backy collection route details.
- **SDK collection record input builder update**: `packages/sdk-js` now exports `buildBackyCollectionRecordWriteInput()` for custom CMS/dataset frontends. The helper maps UI labels or field keys through a public collection schema, supports generated OpenAPI field option objects, applies `metadata.visitorWritePolicy` create/update allowlists, reports ignored fields, keeps slug/request/write-token metadata outside `values`, and returns the `{ values, options }` pair used by `createRecord()` and `updateRecord()`. The Settings workflow, command builder, required-alias list, operator evidence packet, and source guards now expose this as a first-class custom frontend API boundary gate while `/settings` and Settings admin APIs remain Partial until all credentialed live-provider checks are complete.
- **Settings admin client provider-certification bridge update**: `apps/admin/src/lib/adminContentApi.ts` now preserves `providerCertification` on `getSettings()`, `updateSettings()`, API-key rotation, admin-service-key issuance, and service-key revocation responses. The typed `SettingsProviderCertificationHandoff` includes the Settings scenario evidence, `operatorEvidencePacket`, command/env templates, runtime gaps, and no-secret boundary returned by `GET /api/admin/settings`, so custom admin clients do not need raw HTTP calls to render the remaining Settings live-provider gate. Settings smoke now has a serverless `BACKY_SETTINGS_SOURCE_ONLY=1`/`BACKY_SETTINGS_SMOKE_SOURCE_ONLY=1` `settings-source-only` mode that runs these route/client/source guards without launching the admin/public servers, and `test:settings-provider-certification-preflight-contract` guards that entry point while `/settings` and Settings admin APIs remain Partial until credentialed live-provider certification passes.
- **Settings launch-readiness handoff update**: `/settings` now renders a command-center `settings-launch-readiness` panel with copyable `backy.settings-launch-readiness.v1` JSON and a `backy.settings-launch-action-plan.v1` action plan. The handoff summarizes delivery mode, public/admin API contract counts, media storage, database/Supabase, CORS contract headers, security, notification, commerce, interactive runtime, and the exact release/database/provider gates without exporting provider secret values. `apps/admin/scripts/settings-smoke.mjs` source-guards the schema, copy action, action plan, no-secret boundary, and rendered command-center layout while the Settings rows remain Partial until credentialed provider and disposable database certification gates pass.
- **Commerce provider certification gate wording update**: the operator-facing `/products` and `/orders` completion gate is `npm run ci:commerce-provider-certification` or the manual Commerce Provider Certification workflow with explicitly selected payment, tax, shipping, discount, catalog, subscription, and webhook provider families. That CI path runs `test:commerce-provider-certification-preflight-contract`, the non-secret release certification doctor, a safe local or external target summary, and then the live provider readiness checks, while repeatable mock coverage remains `npm run ci:commerce-provider-smoke`. The Products/Orders Partial rows therefore stay tied to real provider-family execution and selected credential/input readiness, not mock coverage alone. `ci:commerce-provider-certification` now writes `backy.commerce-provider-certification-artifact.v1` to `BACKY_COMMERCE_CERTIFICATION_OUTPUT`; the Commerce workflow, the optional nested Commerce step in the Settings provider workflow, and the release workflow upload `artifacts/backy-commerce-provider-certification.json` as `backy-commerce-provider-certification-evidence` so successful live-provider runs leave an attachable redacted artifact. `npm run test:commerce-provider-certification-preflight-contract` source-guards this audit wording.
- **Public commerce provider env template update**: public manifest `data.modules.commerce.providerCertification`, commerce catalog, commerce order contract, site-scoped OpenAPI, generated SDK types, and the convenience SDK commerce contract now expose `operatorEnvTemplate` for `.env.backy-commerce-provider-certification` using `backy.commerce-provider-certification-env-template.v1`. The template mirrors the guarded Commerce provider command with the release doctor, all `BACKY_COMMERCE_CERTIFY_*` selectors, and `BACKY_COMMERCE_PROVIDER_CERTIFICATION_REQUIRED=1` while keeping real provider credentials in CI/local shell variables, so custom storefronts can display the same live-provider setup handoff that `/products` and `/orders` expose in admin.
- **Public commerce provider manifest schema update**: `specs/ai-frontend-contract/frontend-manifest.schema.json` now treats `data.modules.commerce.providerCertification` as a required first-class custom storefront contract instead of an untyped additional property. Generated SDK contract types export `GeneratedBackyFrontendManifestCommerceProviderCertification`, require `providerCertification` on the commerce manifest module, and type the command template, `.env.backy-commerce-provider-certification` handoff, runtime readiness booleans, provider-family choices, and evidence groups so third-party storefronts cannot lose live-provider setup guidance while still passing manifest type checks.
- **SDK commerce order input builder update**: `packages/sdk-js` now exports `buildBackyCommerceOrderInput()` for generated/custom storefront checkouts. The helper accepts the commerce runtime alias contract advertised by manifest/OpenAPI (`items`, `lineItems`, `cartItems`, `cart.items`, product slug/id aliases, `qty`, top-level customer aliases, payment objects, coupon codes, and checkout-session objects), then emits the canonical private-order payload with normalized email, discount code, checkout session id, and quantity bounds before `createCommerceOrder()`. SDK generated-type checks, SDK smoke assertions, API docs, and the SDK README guard the helper while `/products` and `/orders` remain Partial for credentialed live-provider certification.
- **SDK commerce admin handoff methods update**: `packages/sdk-js` now exposes `commerceOrderAnalytics()`, `commerceProductProviderSync(productId)`, and `syncCommerceProductProvider(productId, { provider })` for authenticated custom admin clients. These methods use the same per-call admin auth options as Settings/live-management, read private/no-store order analytics, inspect product catalog-sync state, execute the audited product provider-sync route, and return bounded provider-certification evidence without exposing provider credentials or raw private order payloads. SDK generated-type checks, SDK smoke read assertions, API docs, and the SDK README guard the helpers while `/products` and `/orders` remain Partial for credentialed live-provider certification.
- **SDK commerce platform reconciliation bridge update**: `packages/sdk-js` now exposes `commerceReconciliationReadiness()` and `scheduledPlatformCommerceReconciliation({ dryRun, limit, siteId })` for external operations dashboards. The helpers wrap the existing private `/api/admin/commerce/reconcile/readiness` and `/api/admin/commerce/reconcile` contracts, returning cron-readiness evidence, platform batch reconciliation summaries, per-site skipped/error results, and private/no-store schema metadata without importing the admin UI or exposing provider secrets. SDK generated-type checks, SDK smoke assertions, API docs, and the README guard the bridge while `/products` and `/orders` remain Partial for credentialed live-provider certification.
- **SDK commerce record admin bridge update**: `packages/sdk-js` now exposes first-class product and order CRUD helpers for external commerce dashboards: `adminCommerceProducts()`, `createAdminCommerceProduct()`, `adminCommerceProduct()`, `updateAdminCommerceProduct()`, `deleteAdminCommerceProduct()`, `adminCommerceOrders()`, `createAdminCommerceOrder()`, `adminCommerceOrder()`, `updateAdminCommerceOrder()`, and `deleteAdminCommerceOrder()`. The helpers default to Backy's audited `products` and private `orders` collection-record APIs, support `collectionId` overrides for custom commerce schemas, and keep the existing collection validation, RBAC, audit, webhook, cache-invalidation, and dynamic-content behavior instead of adding a parallel commerce record store. SDK generated-type fixtures, read-only smoke assertions, API docs, and the README guard the bridge while `/products` and `/orders` remain Partial for credentialed live-provider certification.
- **SDK CMS/commerce batch record bridge update**: `packages/sdk-js` now exposes the existing audited collection-record CSV export, CSV import, and bulk delete/status-update workflows through `adminCollectionRecordsCsv()`, `importAdminCollectionRecordsCsv()`, and `bulkAdminCollectionRecords()`, plus commerce aliases `adminCommerceProductsCsv()`, `importAdminCommerceProductsCsv()`, `bulkAdminCommerceProducts()`, `adminCommerceOrdersCsv()`, `importAdminCommerceOrdersCsv()`, and `bulkAdminCommerceOrders()`. These helpers keep product/order and generic CMS batch operations on the same RBAC, validation, audit, webhook, import-error, cache-invalidation, and collection schema paths used by the admin UI, giving external WordPress-like admin consoles real batch management without importing `apps/admin`.
- **SDK CMS backup portability bridge update**: `packages/sdk-js` now exposes `exportAdminCollectionsBackup()` and `importAdminCollectionsBackup()` for custom CMS migration, staging, and multi-site cloning tools. The helpers wrap the audited `backy.collections.backup.v1` JSON export/import routes, preserve schema-plus-record payloads, support selected collection ids, record inclusion toggles, upsert imports, request ids, and admin auth options, and return typed import counts plus cache-invalidation metadata so external WordPress-like admin consoles can move collection schemas and data without scraping `/collections`.
- **SDK CMS/commerce batch runtime coverage update**: `packages/sdk-js/scripts/smoke.mjs` now executes the CMS and commerce batch bridge against a temporary backend site instead of only asserting read/export surfaces: generic collection CSV import, generic bulk status update, generic bulk delete, product/order alias CSV imports with `collectionId` overrides, product/order alias bulk deletes, portable backup import, include-record backup export, and cleanup through `deleteAdminCollection()`. This keeps external WordPress-like CMS dashboards and commerce consoles covered on Backy's audited API paths while `/products`, `/orders`, `/forms`, and Frontend SDK database rows remain Partial for external provider/database certification.
- **SDK Forms admin handoff methods update**: `packages/sdk-js` now exposes typed custom-admin access for Forms CRUD, Forms analytics, contact segments, saved contact lists, submission moderation, and contact status operations through `adminForms()`, `createAdminForm()`, `adminForm()`, `updateAdminForm()`, `deleteAdminForm()`, `formsAnalytics()`, `formContactSegments()`, `formContactLists()`, `saveFormContactList()`, `deleteFormContactList()`, and per-call admin auth options on the existing submission/contact methods. The documented `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/forms/:formId/submissions/:submissionId/review` route now delegates to the same audited review implementation as the existing PATCH route, keeping moderation, contact-share, collection-write, audit, and request-id behavior aligned across admin UI and SDK callers after `/forms` moved to Ready with the external Supabase/Postgres gate on 2026-05-21.

- **SDK Forms runtime action coverage update**: `packages/sdk-js/scripts/smoke.mjs` now executes the safe temporary-site Forms action bridge workflows instead of only checking method presence: reusable embed block creation, form cloning, submission review, backend delivery-retry form create/update/delete, submission webhook retry through an ephemeral local receiver, safe local-outbox email retry, manual contact creation, contact CSV import, saved contact-list create/delete, qualified contact-to-user promotion with SDK user cleanup, qualified contact-to-customer promotion, selected-contact webhook sync through an ephemeral local receiver, and dry-run form/contact consent retention. `/forms` is Ready after the external Supabase/Postgres gate passed on 2026-05-21.

- **Frontend completion-status handoff update**: manifest `data.contract.completionStatus`, site OpenAPI `x-backy-completion-status`, generated SDK types, SDK fixtures, source guards, API docs, and README now publish `backy.completion-status.v1` for custom admin clients. - **Products launch-readiness handoff update**: `/products` now renders a selected-product `products-launch-readiness` panel with a copyable `backy.product-launch-readiness.v1` JSON handoff. - **Products storefront handoff update**: `/products` now renders a selected-product `backy.product-storefront-handoff.v1` copy action beside launch readiness for custom storefronts and generated product pages. The handoff packages storefront-safe product identity, pricing, inventory state, media references, merchandising metadata, delivery/subscription booleans, checkout mode, public endpoint URLs, provider-sync status without provider product/price IDs, and launch next steps while excluding provider secrets, provider responses, private orders, customer payloads, raw checkout sessions, and digital delivery URLs. `apps/admin/scripts/commerce-smoke.mjs` source/layout guards the schema, copy button, privacy boundary, and rendered launch panel while `/products` remains Partial for credentialed live-provider certification.
- **Products design-state durability update**: `/products` now preserves frontend product template custom CSS/JS, content documents, element trees and animations, canvas size, theme-token references, assets, interactions, data bindings, editable maps, SEO, and metadata when creating or saving product records. The public commerce catalog exposes the bounded design payload on `product.design`, so custom storefront editors can reopen product templates and keep the same editable/animated state instead of flattening products to plain CMS values. Product design contracts preserve array-shaped and record-shaped asset manifests, animation timelines, and interaction maps, and `designReadiness` counts both forms for imported builder payloads. - **Products provider-sync OpenAPI/SDK contract update**: site-scoped OpenAPI now exports `CommerceProductProviderSync` and `CommerceProductProviderSyncEnvelope` beside `CommerceProductStorefrontHandoff`, and the SDK `BackyCommerceProductProviderSyncResponse` now types `data.sync` from the generated provider-sync payload shape. - **Products subscription OpenAPI/SDK contract update**: site-scoped OpenAPI now exports `CommerceProductSubscriptionLifecycle`, `CommerceProductSubscriptionsEnvelope`, `CommerceProductSubscriptionAction`, and `CommerceProductSubscriptionActionEnvelope`, and the manifest/OpenAPI commerce management policy now requires the matching product subscription lifecycle/action response contracts. - **Products provider certification evidence update**: `/products` now renders `products-provider-certification-evidence` inside the live provider certification handoff, includes non-secret `backy.product-provider-certification-evidence.v1` scenario coverage in the exported provider handoff, and mirrors the same scenario-evidence handoff from `GET`/`POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/commerce/products/:productId/provider-sync` for custom admin clients. The evidence covers catalog publication, checkout settlement, quote adjustments, provider catalog sync, webhook settlement, subscription lifecycle, inventory automation, and customer/performance signals, names the `BACKY_COMMERCE_PROVIDER_CERTIFICATION_REQUIRED=1 npm run ci:commerce-provider-certification` gate, reports covered/missing scenario counts, and keeps product payloads, customer payloads, private orders, and provider secrets private. `apps/admin/scripts/commerce-smoke.mjs` source/runtime guards the panel and provider-sync API response so the remaining `/products` Partial row stays tied to credentialed live-provider execution evidence rather than a broad checklist.
- **Orders provider certification evidence update**: `/orders` now renders `orders-provider-certification-evidence` inside the live provider certification panel, includes non-secret `backy.order-provider-certification-evidence.v1` scenario coverage in the exported provider handoff, and mirrors the same scenario-evidence handoff from `GET /api/admin/sites/:siteId/commerce/orders/analytics` for custom admin clients. The packet records selected payment/refund, tax, shipping/label, discount, fulfillment, subscription, and webhook/reconciliation families, required input aliases, target/admin-key inputs, capture sources, expected artifacts, scenario attachments, command preview, runtime credential gaps, and a redaction policy that excludes provider secrets, customer payloads, raw order payloads, payment references, addresses, and webhook bodies. `apps/admin/scripts/orders-smoke.mjs` and `test:commerce-provider-certification-preflight-contract` guard the UI/export contract while `/orders` remains Partial until credentialed live-provider certification passes.
