- **Commerce provider certification API-handoff update**: `npm run ci:commerce-provider-certification` now verifies the selected site's concrete public and admin Commerce APIs through `BACKY_COMMERCE_CERTIFY_SITE_ID`. Certification checks `GET /api/sites/:siteId/manifest`, `GET /api/sites/:siteId/commerce/catalog`, and `GET /api/sites/:siteId/commerce/orders` for public provider-certification handoffs, checks `GET /api/admin/sites/:siteId/commerce/products/:productId/provider-sync` for provider-certification evidence, operator evidence packets, storefront handoff, and `backy.product-design-readiness.v1`, then checks `GET /api/admin/sites/:siteId/commerce/orders/analytics` for `backy.order-analytics.v1`, provider-certification evidence, and order operator evidence packets. The redacted result includes top-level `target.siteId` and `target.siteSelectorEnv` plus `apiHandoffs.publicApis`, `apiHandoffs.product`, and `apiHandoffs.orders`; the standalone Commerce workflow, nested Settings workflow, and release workflow expose the site selector while `/products` and `/orders` remain Partial until credentialed live-provider certification passes.
- **Commerce provider certification artifact privacy hardening**: `npm run ci:commerce-provider-certification` now refuses to emit its redacted `backy.commerce-provider-certification-artifact.v1` JSON when the final payload contains forbidden sensitive artifact field names or credential-bearing URLs, matching the release doctor's `noForbiddenArtifactFieldsReady` verifier. This prevents Commerce evidence for `/products` and `/orders` from being archived if future changes accidentally add admin keys, external target URLs, database URLs, webhook bodies, provider credentials, payment references, customer payloads, raw order payloads, or URL-embedded credentials to the certification artifact.
- **Orders analytics OpenAPI/SDK contract update**: site-scoped OpenAPI now exports `CommerceOrderAnalytics`, `CommerceOrderAnalyticsEnvelope`, `CommerceOrderAnalyticsProviderCertification`, `CommerceOrderProviderCertificationEvidence`, and `CommerceOrderProviderCertificationEvidencePacket`, and the manifest/OpenAPI commerce management policy now requires `responseContracts.orderAnalytics = "backy.order-analytics.v1"`. Generated SDK types and the `BackyCommerceOrderAnalyticsResponse` helper now type the private analytics payload, provider-certification evidence, endpoint evidence, and evidence-packet redaction flags so custom Wix/Webflow-style admin consoles can build order dashboards without reverse-engineering the admin UI while `/orders` remains Partial only for credentialed live provider certification.
- **Orders operation OpenAPI/SDK contract update**: site-scoped OpenAPI now exports `CommerceOrderQuoteEnvelope`, `CommerceOrderTrackingEnvelope`, `CommerceOrderFulfillmentEnvelope`, `CommerceOrderShippingLabelEnvelope`, and `CommerceOrderProviderRefundEnvelope`, and the manifest/OpenAPI commerce management policy now requires the matching quote, tracking, fulfillment, shipping-label, and provider-refund response contracts. The SDK order-operation payload helpers now reuse the generated OpenAPI payload shapes, giving external admin consoles typed operation responses for tax/shipping/discount quote recalculation, carrier tracking, fulfillment dispatch, label purchase/void, and provider refund create/refresh without scraping the `/orders` UI. `/orders` remains Partial only for credentialed live provider certification.
- **Products/Orders source-only provider guard update**: `apps/admin/scripts/commerce-smoke.mjs` now exposes a JSON-emitting `commerce-source-only` guard through `BACKY_COMMERCE_SOURCE_ONLY=1`, `BACKY_PRODUCTS_SOURCE_ONLY=1`, or `BACKY_COMMERCE_SMOKE_SOURCE_ONLY=1`, and `apps/admin/scripts/orders-smoke.mjs` exposes `orders-source-only` through `BACKY_ORDERS_SOURCE_ONLY=1`, `BACKY_ORDERS_SMOKE_SOURCE_ONLY=1`, or `BACKY_COMMERCE_ORDERS_SOURCE_ONLY=1`. These modes run the Products/Orders provider-certification UI, API, SDK, and contract source guards without launching browsers or dev servers, and `test:commerce-provider-certification-preflight-contract` guards the entry points while `/products` and `/orders` remain Partial until credentialed live-provider certification passes.
- **Aggregate provider source-only guard update**: root `npm run test:partial-gate-preflights` now runs `npm run test:provider-source-only`, which chains `npm run test:settings-source-only`, `npm run test:commerce-source-only`, and `npm run test:orders-source-only` before the Settings/Commerce provider preflight contracts and release certification contracts. This keeps the remaining `/settings`, Settings admin API, `/products`, and `/orders` source/API/UI provider handoffs in the aggregate Partial gate without launching browsers, dev servers, live databases, or provider accounts; `test:release-certification-preflight-contract` guards the package wiring and `SETUP.md` documents the direct source-only commands.
- **Orders status-handoff update**: `/orders` now renders a selected-order `orders-status-handoff` panel with a copyable `backy.order-status-handoff.v1` JSON handoff for custom storefront order-confirmation pages, customer portals, and support widgets. The handoff keeps raw orders private while exposing a bounded customer-safe projection with masked contact data, payment state, fulfillment/tracking state, refund state, private-queue safety checks, and the selected order operation action plan. `apps/admin/scripts/orders-smoke.mjs` source-guards the schema, copy action, action-plan bridge, and rendered Orders layout so customer order-status projections cannot silently drift while the row remains Partial for live provider certification.
- **Orders status-handoff privacy hardening**: the same `backy.order-status-handoff.v1` selected-order handoff now reports customer/profile, shipping-label, and provider-refund references as booleans instead of exporting raw internal customer IDs, shipping label IDs/URLs, or provider refund IDs/references. Its privacy block explicitly marks raw contact, provider execution IDs, payment references, addresses, and internal notes as excluded, and `apps/admin/scripts/orders-smoke.mjs` source-guards those flags and field exclusions so customer-facing order-confirmation and support widgets stay bounded while `/orders` remains Partial for credentialed live-provider certification.
- **Orders status-handoff API/SDK bridge**: custom authenticated dashboards and support portals can now read the same bounded `backy.order-status-handoff.v1` projection through `GET /api/admin/sites/:siteId/commerce/orders/:orderId/status-handoff` or `commerceOrderStatusHandoff(orderId)` in `packages/sdk-js`. The endpoint requires `commerce.view`, returns private/no-store Backy contract headers, omits raw order records, and carries the same masked-contact, boolean-reference, excluded-field, and action-plan boundary as the rendered `/orders` copy action. SDK smoke now creates a temporary private `orders` collection, creates a paid/tracked order through admin commerce helpers, verifies the status handoff masks email/phone, exposes safe tracking/frontend binding/action paths, excludes raw payment references, label ids, addresses, and internal notes, verifies updated fulfilled/tracking state, and deletes the order before cleanup.
- **Orders status-handoff SDK contract update**: `BackyCommerceOrderStatusHandoff` now aliases the generated OpenAPI `GeneratedBackyOpenApiCommerceOrderStatusHandoff` shape instead of a looser hand-written interface, so SDK consumers get the required `frontendBindings`, endpoint, action-plan, and privacy fields exactly as the authenticated status-handoff API documents them. The Orders source-only guard now fails if the SDK helper drifts away from the generated OpenAPI type while `/orders` remains Partial only for credentialed live provider certification.
- **Orders public status-access bridge**: public checkout now issues a one-time returned `backy.order-status-access.v1` token contract, stores only `statusaccesstokenhash`/issued/expiry fields on the private order, and verifies tokenized `GET /api/sites/:siteId/commerce/orders?orderId=:orderId&statusToken=:statusToken` requests before returning the bounded `backy.order-status-handoff.v1` projection. Manifest, OpenAPI, generated SDK types, SDK source guards, and Orders source-only smoke now cover `CommerceOrderStatusAccess`, `CommerceOrderStatusHandoffEnvelope`, `post-checkout-status-token`, token-hash privacy, and `commerceOrderPublicStatusHandoff(orderId, statusToken)` so custom customer portals can refresh status without admin auth or raw order access.
- **Orders status-access endpoint-template hardening**: `/orders`, public checkout status handoffs, and authenticated status-handoff API responses now advertise tokenized public refresh URLs with `statusToken={statusToken}` placeholders instead of tokenless order-status URLs. Custom storefronts and customer portals can wire the exact private/no-store refresh contract from Backy's handoff JSON while Backy still stores only token hashes and never returns the raw token outside the checkout response.
- **Revision rendered-pixel comparison update**: page and blog editor revision cards now include sampled rendered-pixel comparison metrics in the shared visual diff, with changed-pixel counts, changed ratio, added/removed/updated pixel buckets, and the same metadata inside the copyable page/blog revision comparison handoff. `apps/admin/scripts/editor-drag-smoke.mjs` and `apps/admin/scripts/blog-editor-smoke.mjs` source-guard the shared `RevisionCanvasVisualDiff` pixel comparison block plus the page/blog diff model wiring.
- **Blog revision branch graph parity update**: the blog editor now matches the page editor's revision branch graph by exposing `backy.blog-revision-branch-graph.v1` in the editor handoff, rendering branch lanes, pending restore branches, rollback-target edges, per-node branch roles, and a copyable branch-graph JSON action. `apps/admin/scripts/blog-editor-smoke.mjs` source-guards the branch graph schema, persisted lineage metadata, handoff bridge, copy control, branch graph panel, rollback edge panel, and per-node branch-role metadata.
- **Persisted revision lineage update**: page/blog update, publish, archive, and rollback revisions now persist `parentRevisionId`, `operation`, `restoreTargetRevisionId`, and `backy.content-revision-metadata.v1` in demo and database modes. The admin page/blog revision handoff manifests include those fields, the revision action label prefers persisted operation metadata, and OpenAPI/SDK/source guards expose the same fields for external custom editors.
- **SDK editor command surface update**: custom frontends and external page builders can now use SDK helpers for the rest of the Canva-style content command set: `addBackyContentElement()`, `duplicateBackyContentElement()`, `deleteBackyContentElements()`, and `transformBackyContentElements()` alongside the existing list/find/patch/group/ungroup helpers. Manifest `data.modules.liveManagement.editorComposition`, OpenAPI `x-backy-live-management.editorComposition`, generated SDK contract types, SDK smoke coverage, and frontend contract guards now advertise add/duplicate/delete/move/resize/group/ungroup commands with locked-layer defaults, responsive breakpoint transforms, parent-id rewrites, and conflict-safe live-management update compatibility.
- **SDK editor composition metadata refresh update**: the SDK element command helpers now recompute `metadata.editorComposition` after local add/duplicate/delete/transform/group/ungroup/patch mutations, matching the canonical `backy.editor-composition-summary.v1` contract for top-level and nested content documents before external builders call live-management update APIs. `packages/sdk-js/scripts/smoke.mjs` asserts grouped layer counts, stale group-id removal after ungrouping, and responsive override counts after editable-field patches; `npm run ci:sdk-smoke` passed on 2026-05-22 with SDK generated types, SDK typecheck/build, public app discovery, and full SDK runtime smoke.
- **Editor composition design-state metrics update**: canonical `@backy-cms/core` migrations and the SDK live-management mirror now add animated/action/data-bound/token-ref/asset-bound/interactive layer metrics plus matching element id lists to `backy.editor-composition-summary.v1`. This makes custom frontend builders able to inspect whether a saved page/blog contains animation token refs, action wiring, dataset bindings, media/font/file references, and interactive figures without walking the raw canvas tree. `npm run test:content-contract --workspace @backy-cms/core`, `npm run test:frontend-contract --workspace @backy/public`, `npm run test:generated-types --workspace @backy/sdk-js`, and `node scripts/sdk-smoke-ci.mjs` passed on 2026-05-22 for this contract.
- **Page editor composition handoff UI update**: `apps/admin/src/components/editor/CanvasEditor.tsx` now projects the richer composition design-state metrics into the visible editor inspector, with Motion/Data/Assets/Interactive tiles and matching data attributes for custom smoke tooling and external handoff inspection. The copied `backy.editor-composition-action-plan.v1` includes `animatedLayers`, `actionLayers`, `dataBoundLayers`, `tokenRefLayers`, `assetBoundLayers`, `interactiveLayers`, and `designStateLayerCount` alongside the existing layer tree metrics. `BACKY_EDITOR_SOURCE_ONLY=1 npm run test:editor-drag --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `npm run test:editor-smoke-coverage --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, and `npm run typecheck --workspace @backy-cms/admin` passed on 2026-05-22.
- **Editor command registry handoff update**: `apps/admin/src/components/editor/CanvasEditor.tsx` now embeds `backy.editor-command-registry.v1` inside the composition readiness/action-plan handoff and renders a copyable `editor-command-registry` panel. Custom admin/frontends can discover stable command ids, test ids, shortcuts, target scopes, current ready/disabled/hidden state, and reasons for grouping, ungrouping, clipboard, sibling/child selection, layer state/order, layout, grid/snap/pan/zoom, shell, save, publish, settings, and reload actions without scraping toolbar markup.
- **Editor command registry API discovery update**: public manifest and site OpenAPI now expose the same command surface at `editorComposition.commandRegistry`. The contract carries `backy.editor-command-registry.v1`, category groupings, state-model fields clients must compute, stable test ids, shortcuts, SDK/API helper mappings, editable target paths, and privacy flags, with SDK manifest/OpenAPI smoke coverage and generated SDK typings updated for external Wix/Webflow-style builders.
- **SDK editor command registry evaluator update**: `packages/sdk-js` now exposes `evaluateBackyEditorCommandRegistry()`, which turns a manifest/OpenAPI `backy.editor-command-registry.v1` handoff plus local content descriptors, selected ids, clipboard count, history position, permissions, preview/save state, and editor mode into `backy.editor-command-registry-evaluation.v1` ready/disabled/hidden command states. SDK smoke coverage asserts grouping, ungrouping, paste, viewport, and save command evaluation so custom frontends can bind Backy-compatible toolbars while storing page/blog design details through the existing live-management update APIs.
- **Editor prop-action composition parity update**: `actionLayers` and `actionElementIds` now include common prop-based page-builder actions (`href`, `url`, `action`, `actionUrl`, `formId`, `successRedirectUrl`, and `redirectUrl`) in `@backy-cms/core`, the SDK live-management helper summary, and the page editor handoff UI. This keeps link/button/form action wiring visible to custom frontend APIs even when the canvas element stores it as normal props instead of a separate `actions[]` array. `npm run test:content-contract --workspace @backy-cms/core`, `npm run test:frontend-contract --workspace @backy/public`, `BACKY_EDITOR_SOURCE_ONLY=1 npm run test:editor-drag --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `npm run test:editor-smoke-coverage --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `npm run test:generated-types --workspace @backy/sdk-js`, and `node scripts/sdk-smoke-ci.mjs` passed on 2026-05-22.
- **Page/blog editor design-envelope handoff update**: the copyable page and blog editor handoff manifests now include `backy.custom-frontend-design-envelope.v1`, built from a current canonical content document. Custom frontend/admin clients receive custom CSS/JS, content-document id/kind/schema/status/slug, theme-token ref counts, asset counts, interaction groups, data-binding and editable-map coverage, metadata, and `editorComposition` without scraping raw editor state. `npm run typecheck --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `BACKY_EDITOR_SOURCE_ONLY=1 npm run test:editor-drag --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `BACKY_BLOG_EDITOR_SOURCE_ONLY=1 npm run test:blog-editor --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, and `npm run test:editor-smoke-coverage --workspace @backy-cms/admin` passed on 2026-05-22.
- **Page/blog editor full design-document handoff update**: the same copyable page and blog editor handoff manifests now carry the complete current `backy.content.v1` document, current element tree, canvas size, a separate compact `contentDocumentSummary`, and root animation timeline counts inside `backy.custom-frontend-design-envelope.v1`. This makes external/custom frontends able to reopen and mutate editable page/blog designs, grouped layers, animations, token refs, assets, data bindings, and editable maps from the handoff itself instead of reconstructing state from summary counters.
- **SDK interactive-component release bridge update**: `packages/sdk-js` now exposes `uploadAdminInteractiveComponentBundle()`, `migrateAdminInteractiveComponentVersion()`, and `rollbackAdminInteractiveComponentVersion()` so external builder consoles can drive the audited signed bundle, content migration, and rollback APIs without importing the Backy admin app. The helpers preserve admin auth options and request-id headers, return typed `backy.interactive-component-bundle.v1`, migration summary, and rollback envelopes, and keep custom component execution on Backy's sandbox/integrity boundary.
- **SDK comment delivery retry bridge update**: `GET /api/sites/:siteId/openapi` now names the comment delivery retry request/envelope for `POST /api/sites/:siteId/comments/:commentId/delivery-retry`, and `packages/sdk-js` exposes `retryCommentDelivery(commentId, { eventId })` with the same per-call admin auth options as live-management. External moderation/support consoles can retry audited failed comment webhook/email events without importing the admin UI, while generated contract checks and SDK smoke guard the bridge.
- **SDK comment analytics bridge update**: `GET /api/sites/:siteId/openapi` now names the private comment moderation analytics envelope for `GET /api/sites/:siteId/comments/analytics`, and `packages/sdk-js` exposes `commentAnalytics({ days, targetType, targetId })` with per-call admin auth options. External moderation/community dashboards can read status totals, report reasons, thread pressure, target hotspots, and daily activity without scraping `/comments`; generated contract checks and SDK smoke guard the bridge.
- **SDK comment moderation coverage update**: `packages/sdk-js` now names the site-wide comment queue, bulk moderation/report-clear, blocklist, single-comment, and delete response envelopes as exported SDK types and generated return-contract assertions. The SDK smoke write flow now creates a temporary page comment, reports it, verifies the report event, clears reports, bulk-blocks the author, reads and deletes the blocklist entry, and deletes the moderated comment, so external moderation consoles have runtime coverage for queue cleanup and blocked-author workflows without importing `/comments`.
- **Page/blog frontend-template design-state update**: the shared frontend-design seeding contract now keeps custom JS, content-document metadata, theme-token refs, asset manifests, interaction/animation timelines, document data bindings, editable maps, SEO, and template metadata when `/pages/new`, `/blog/new`, or direct admin create APIs seed content from a connected frontend template. The same fields are stored in `meta.frontendDesign*` provenance and normalized into `backy.content.v1`, keeping custom frontend page/post editors aligned with Backy's canvas editor.
- **Site frontend-design partial-patch durability update**: site-scoped Settings and `/api/admin/sites/:siteId/frontend-design` PATCH writes now merge partial frontend-design updates against the existing `backy.frontend-design.v1` contract, preserving omitted source/tokens/chrome/template registry/editable-map fields and per-template content. Nested template content records now deep-merge interactions, data bindings, editable maps, SEO, metadata, and animation/design envelopes, keeping existing triggers, datasets, editor state, custom CSS/JS, content documents, assets, and template metadata intact when a custom admin client or Wix-like builder saves one design setting instead of resubmitting the whole contract.
- **Page/blog publish/archive design-state response update**: `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/pages/:pageId/publish`, `/archive`, `POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/blog/:postId/publish`, and `/archive` now return `content.customJS` alongside `content.customCSS` and `content.contentDocument` in repository mode. This keeps lifecycle responses compatible with the normal page/blog editor APIs, so external builders do not have to re-read the page/post just to recover custom frontend script design state after changing publish status.
- **Page/blog frontend animation provenance update**: `PageMeta` and the shared Backy store `normalizeFrontendDesignMetaFields()` path now persist `frontendDesignAnimations` alongside `frontendDesignInteractions`. The page/blog create smoke templates seed animation timeline descriptors and assert they are available in page/post meta, so external builders can reopen custom frontend templates with both animation provenance and interaction triggers intact.
- **Public page/blog custom-JS projection update**: public repository-mode page and blog reads now expose `content.customJS` alongside `content.customCSS` and the canonical `content.contentDocument`, matching the already-generated OpenAPI/SDK resource shape. This keeps published custom frontend reads aligned with Backy's editor responses and avoids forcing external renderers to scrape `contentDocument.metadata.customJS`.
- **Public frontend-design provenance projection update**: public page, blog, reusable-section, and manifest summaries now hydrate `frontendDesign` with the same rich captured-template design state stored under `meta.frontendDesign*` or `metadata.frontendDesign*`, including custom JS, content documents, element trees, canvas size, token refs, assets, animations, interactions, data bindings, editable maps, SEO, metadata, and binding hints. OpenAPI and generated SDK types expose the expanded `ReusableSectionFrontendDesign` fields for typed custom frontend renderers and editors.
- **Public media organization breadcrumb update**: public media list/detail responses now include `organization` using `backy.media.organization.v1` with folder id, name, path, segments, ancestor folder ids/parents, depth, sort order, root, and missing-folder flags. The list/detail routes attach folder catalogs in both demo and repository mode before calling `toPublicMediaAsset()`, and OpenAPI/generated SDK/SDK runtime types expose the same field so custom media browsers can render and reconstruct organized file, image, and font picker folder trees directly from media assets. `npm run test:media-upload-contract --workspace @backy/public`, `npm run test:frontend-contract --workspace @backy/public`, `npm run test:generated-types --workspace @backy/sdk-js`, `npm run typecheck --workspace @backy/public`, and `npm run typecheck --workspace @backy/sdk-js` passed on 2026-05-22.
- **Media upload category allowlist update**: Settings storage `allowedFileTypes` now accepts Backy media-category aliases in addition to raw MIME types and extensions. `document/*` and `document` cover PDFs, Office documents, CSV/text/markdown, `file/*` and `file` cover document plus generic `other` downloadable files, `other/*` covers unknown safe uploads, and `*/*` is an explicit allow-all rule. The upload-policy smoke guards category matching for generic browser MIME fallbacks such as `application/octet-stream`, so central media storage can enforce user-facing image/font/document/file categories instead of forcing admins to enumerate every MIME value. `npm run test:media-upload-policy --workspace @backy/public`, `npm run test:media-upload-contract --workspace @backy/public`, `npm run typecheck --workspace @backy/public`, `npm run typecheck --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `npm run test:settings-admin-contract --workspace @backy/public`, and `BACKY_SETTINGS_SOURCE_ONLY=1 npm run test:settings --workspace @backy-cms/admin` passed on 2026-05-22.
- **Admin media organization handoff update**: admin media list/upload/update/replace responses now mirror the same `backy.media.organization.v1` breadcrumb as public media assets, and the admin media API helper preserves it on `MediaAsset`. The `/media` copyable placement handoff and aggregate media handoff now include the versioned organization envelope beside folder display paths, so custom frontend builders and the rich editor can place files/fonts/images from a stable folder contract instead of reconstructing hierarchy from `folderId`. `npm run test:media-upload-contract --workspace @backy/public`, `npm run test:frontend-contract --workspace @backy/public`, `npm run test:generated-types --workspace @backy/sdk-js`, `npm run typecheck --workspace @backy/public`, and `npm run typecheck --workspace @backy-cms/admin` passed on 2026-05-22.
- **Editor media picker organization persistence update**: the editor media picker now exposes folder path, folder segments, and organization schema data attributes for selectable assets, and selected image/file/font placements persist `mediaOrganization`, `mediaFolderPath`, segment/name/depth metadata, and matching fallback/font organization props into the saved element design state. This lets custom frontend renderers and external builders reopen page/blog designs with organized media placement metadata instead of only a raw `mediaFolderId`. `BACKY_EDITOR_SOURCE_ONLY=1 npm run test:editor-drag --workspace @backy-cms/admin` and `npm run typecheck --workspace @backy-cms/admin` passed on 2026-05-22.
- **Editor custom animation authoring update**: the animation builder's `Custom` option now exposes editable GSAP `from` and `to` JSON object controls with validation and a reset action, instead of only selecting a custom type while leaving the custom motion payload uneditable. The saved animation config can now carry authored custom motion state through the same canonical page/blog design-state persistence and public `AnimationHydrator` path that already preserves custom `from`/`to` records. `BACKY_EDITOR_SOURCE_ONLY=1 npm run test:editor-drag --workspace @backy-cms/admin` and `npm run typecheck --workspace @backy-cms/admin` passed on 2026-05-22.
- **Private downloadable-file editor handoff update**: the editor media picker now allows private media selection only when a button/link is choosing a downloadable file. That path persists `fileSignedUrlRequired`, the signed-URL endpoint, file identity, folder/scope/visibility metadata, and `assetIds`; normal image/video/embed/font insertions still block private media. Public rendered download anchors expose `data-backy-file-*` metadata so custom frontends can detect signed-delivery requirements without scraping editor internals.
- **Responsive media reference indexing update**: the core content migration collector, SDK patch asset sync, public runtime media usage index, manifest live-management discovery, and OpenAPI `x-backy-live-management` targets now recognize plural asset arrays (`mediaIds`, `fontMediaIds`, image/video/audio/file/document/icon ID arrays, fallback/background/poster media arrays) anywhere in element design state, including mobile/tablet responsive overrides. A focused public smoke creates and updates a page with desktop and responsive plural refs, verifies central media `pageIds` are added, and verifies stale usage refs are pruned after save; SDK smoke coverage also patches responsive poster/background arrays and confirms returned layer descriptors expose those target paths.
- **Page/blog design-state schema hardening**: `@backy-cms/core` now types `SiteFrontendDesignTemplateContent` plus the full `PageMeta.frontendDesign*` provenance envelope for custom JS, content documents, element trees, canvas size, token refs, assets, animations, interactions, data bindings, editable maps, SEO, and metadata. The site OpenAPI exposes the same template-content schema and explicitly advertises top-level `animations` in `PageUpdateRequest` and `BlogPostUpdateRequest` canvas payloads, and generated SDK types compile page/blog update examples with animation arrays. This closes the contract gap where runtime persistence already accepted the design state but custom frontend builders could not rely on typed discovery for animation/template content.
- **Page/blog admin design-envelope round-trip update**: admin page/blog create, detail, and update APIs plus the local Backy store now preserve and expose top-level custom frontend design state (`themeTokenRefs`, `assets`, `animations`, `interactions`, `dataBindings`, `editableMap`, `seo`, and `metadata`) beside `contentDocument`, `customCSS`, and `customJS`. Root animation arrays are stored in canonical content-document metadata, and partial design-only page updates keep existing canvas elements instead of replacing them with an empty tree. The public frontend contract smoke now guards these runtime persistence paths so external Wix-like editors can safely reopen and modify page/blog designs.
- **Product/CMS partial-patch design-envelope update**: admin collection-record PATCH now preserves existing values before applying partial updates in both local/demo and Supabase/Postgres repository mode, so product SKU/pricing/media fields, hidden frontend-design state, and arbitrary custom CMS JSON are not wiped when an external editor patches one field. Public commerce catalog and `/products` storefront handoffs also understand a clean `values.design` product envelope, mirror it into the normalized `product.design` aliases, and keep legacy `frontendDesign*` provenance compatible. Public/admin/source guards now cover clean design-envelope binding hints and partial product design updates that must retain the existing content document.
- **OpenAPI frontend launch-readiness parity update**: `GET /api/sites/:siteId/openapi` now mirrors manifest `data.contract.frontendLaunchReadiness` for custom frontend operators, including the content/design, media/font, visitor-interaction, commerce, live-management, routing/render, and database-certification check keys plus page, blog-post, media, and font module counts. SDK smoke and public source guards assert the manifest/OpenAPI parity so launch readiness remains discoverable without scraping docs or admin UI state.
- **Product custom-frontend readiness update**: the `/products` selected-product launch-readiness handoff now includes a `frontend-design` check for the product's editable custom frontend envelope. It reports template id, content document presence, element count, animation count, asset count, editable-map/data-binding presence, and binding-hint count, and recommends attaching a product frontend template or saving `contentDocument`/`elements` plus `editableMap`/`dataBindings` when the product is sellable but cannot yet be reopened as a rich custom storefront design. The Commerce smoke source/runtime guards cover the new check while `/products` remains Partial only for credentialed live-provider certification.
- **Product provider-sync design-readiness API update**: authenticated `GET/POST /api/admin/sites/:siteId/commerce/products/:productId/provider-sync` storefront handoffs now include `designReadiness` using `backy.product-design-readiness.v1`. The handoff mirrors the `/products` custom-frontend readiness signal for API clients by reporting template id, content-document/editable-map/data-binding booleans, element/animation/asset/binding-hint counts, missing design-envelope keys, evidence strings, next action, and a no-secret boundary. OpenAPI exports `CommerceProductDesignReadiness`, the SDK handoff type exposes `designReadiness`, and SDK/Commerce/Public contract guards assert the field so external product-page builders do not have to scrape the admin UI to decide if a product page can be edited as a rich custom frontend design.
- **Product storefront handoff design-readiness parity update**: the copyable `/products` `backy.product-storefront-handoff.v1` JSON now includes the same `designReadiness` block as the provider-sync API. The admin launch checklist and storefront handoff share one `buildProductDesignReadiness()` path, so copied operator handoffs, SDK/API responses, and source guards all agree on template id, content tree, editable-map/data-binding, animation, asset, and binding-hint readiness before a custom product page is treated as rich-editable.
- **Product storefront handoff API/privacy parity update**: the copyable `/products` `backy.product-storefront-handoff.v1` JSON now mirrors the authenticated provider-sync API handoff by including the selected product `providerSync` endpoint and explicit `includesProviderResponses: false`, `includesCustomerPayloads: false`, and `includesRawCheckoutSessions: false` privacy flags. External builders can use either the UI handoff or provider-sync API response without losing the provider-sync action URL or the no-secret/no-private-order boundary.
- **Public product design-readiness update**: public commerce catalog product records now include `designReadiness` beside the rich `design` envelope. The non-secret `backy.product-design-readiness.v1` summary reports template id, content-document/editable-map/data-binding booleans, element/animation/asset/binding-hint counts, missing design-envelope keys, evidence, and next action, so generated storefronts can decide from `commerceCatalog()` whether a product can be reopened as a rich editable custom page without calling admin APIs. OpenAPI now links `CommerceProduct.designReadiness` to `CommerceProductDesignReadiness`, SDK product types expose the field, and SDK/public/frontend-contract guards assert the runtime and generated contract.
- **Product dynamic route/render design-readiness update**: product-backed dynamic item routes now carry the same `backy.product-design-readiness.v1` signal on `/api/sites/:siteId/resolve` and `/api/sites/:siteId/render` at `route.resource.designReadiness`. The public render payload schema, OpenAPI `DynamicItemRouteResource`, SDK route/render types, generated contract types, SDK write smoke, public source guard, Commerce source guard, and API docs now cover the route/resource projection, so custom storefronts that consume render payloads directly get the same editable product design-state readiness as `commerceCatalog()` and provider-sync storefront handoffs.
- **Live overlay actions/bindings update**: hosted page/blog live editing now includes an advanced selected-element editor for `actions`, `dataBindings`, and `bindingSlots` JSON arrays alongside the existing text/link/media/form/layout/animation/appearance controls. The overlay validates the arrays before patching the canonical content document through the authenticated live-management bridge, so custom frontend behavior, collection bindings, and preset binding slots can be edited without losing the richer page-builder design envelope.
- **Dynamic collection template design-state update**: Backy-authored collection list/detail template captures now retain the richer page-builder envelope from the source canvas: custom CSS/JS, content documents, theme-token refs, assets, animation arrays, interactions, data bindings, editable maps, SEO, metadata, and root elements. Public dynamic list/item render payloads pass those fields into `content.contentDocument` plus top-level custom-frontend content fields, so collection-backed pages can reopen as editable custom frontend designs instead of flattening to generated elements only.
- **Render payload design-state schema update**: `specs/ai-frontend-contract/content-payload.schema.json` now accepts the same rich `content.*` design-state fields that dynamic collection render payloads can emit: custom CSS/JS, canonical content documents, theme-token refs, assets, animation arrays, interactions, data bindings, editable maps, SEO, and metadata. Generated SDK render payload types now include the nested content-document schema and `BackyRenderContent` helper, and the public frontend contract guard fails if the runtime, schema, or SDK type surface drifts apart.
- **Auth recovery inline-validation update**: `/forgot-password` and `/reset-password` now use source-guarded custom validation instead of relying on disabled submit buttons or browser-native validation. The recovery form keeps Request Recovery reachable, marks invalid email with `aria-invalid`/`aria-describedby`, exposes stable form/input/message test ids, and blocks invalid submissions with field-level copy. The reset form keeps Reset Password reachable, reports missing-token, password-length, and confirmation errors inline, and keeps status/error messages accessible for secure user-account recovery flows.
- **Editor inspector findability update**: the page editor property panel now has a compact section search that filters and expands matching inspector sections, exposes search-status and clear states, and resets back to Essentials when the selected element changes so focused inspector modes do not make the next layer look empty. No-match searches now show the active query in `editor-property-section-search-empty` and expose `editor-property-section-empty-reset`, so a dense inspector search miss returns directly to the core Content/Layout/Style controls instead of leaving operators on a passive empty chip. The source guard fails if the empty-state query/reset hooks disappear, and the rendered inspector smoke verifies `not-a-real-control` yields zero visible sections, shows `Show essentials`, then restores an empty query, Essentials mode, and content/layout/style expanded sections through that reset path. `BACKY_EDITOR_SOURCE_ONLY=1 npm run test:editor-drag --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `node --check apps/admin/scripts/editor-drag-smoke.mjs`, `npm run typecheck --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, and `BACKY_CDP_PORT=9557 BACKY_ADMIN_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:5173 BACKY_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3001 npm run test:editor-inspector --workspace @backy-cms/admin` passed on 2026-05-26. In-app Browser validation was attempted but the Browser runtime reported `iab` unavailable, so the repo CDP smoke was used as the rendered fallback.

- **Editor canvas quick-add UX update**: the in-canvas context bar now includes an accessible quick-add menu for Heading, Text, Image, Button, Section, and Form so common Wix-like insertions can happen directly from the visible canvas without reopening or scanning the component library. The menu reuses the existing component-library insertion path, so added layers keep the same defaults, responsive metadata, nesting rules, selection behavior, disabled-state handling, and save persistence as drag/drop or sidebar click-add. Source guards cover the quick-add inventory and handler contract, and the component-library rendered smoke now opens the context quick-add menu, inserts a heading, verifies exactly one selected canvas element was added, and confirms the popover closes after insertion. `BACKY_EDITOR_SOURCE_ONLY=1 npm run test:editor-drag --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `npm run test:editor-smoke-coverage --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `npm run typecheck --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, and `BACKY_CDP_PORT=9504 BACKY_ADMIN_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:5173 BACKY_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3001 npm run test:editor-library --workspace @backy-cms/admin` passed on 2026-05-25.
- **Editor canvas visible text-edit UX update**: selecting one unlocked visible text, heading, quote, paragraph, or list layer now exposes a dedicated context-bar Edit text action, so inline copy editing is reachable from the canvas itself instead of requiring a hidden double-click/custom-event path. The action dispatches the existing `backy-open-text-editor` flow, preserves single-selection state, opens the properties panel for the selected layer, and stays disabled with accessible copy for non-text, locked, hidden, or mutation-disabled selections. Source guards cover the editable-type contract, command registry entry, context metadata, button wiring, and smoke coverage, and the component-library rendered smoke verifies that selecting the smoke heading enables the action and activates the inline contenteditable editor from the visible context bar. `BACKY_EDITOR_SOURCE_ONLY=1 npm run test:editor-drag --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `npm run test:editor-smoke-coverage --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `npm run typecheck --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, and `BACKY_CDP_PORT=9505 BACKY_ADMIN_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:5173 BACKY_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3001 npm run test:editor-library --workspace @backy-cms/admin` passed on 2026-05-25.
- **Page/blog creation entrypoint verification and editor navigation guard update**: `/pages` and `/blog` create entrypoints now have fresh rendered coverage proving New Page/New Post remain primary, stay reachable during background sync, carry the active `siteId`, and open the correct create/focused-canvas workspaces. The Pages rendered smoke also found and closed a Chrome intervention where the editor tried to request a `beforeunload` confirmation during programmatic list-route navigation before real browser user activation; `CanvasEditor` now keeps unsaved-change protection for activated user sessions while avoiding false intervention errors from initial editor hydration or automation. Pages and Blog list/create smokes verify the 33 page starters, focused page/post editor handoff, responsive public previews, autosave/recovery, media picker, preview flows, and persisted custom frontend design metadata for page and blog templates. `npm run typecheck --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `BACKY_PAGES_LIST_SOURCE_ONLY=1 npm run test:pages-list --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `BACKY_ADMIN_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:5173 BACKY_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3001 npm run test:pages-list --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `BACKY_ADMIN_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:5173 BACKY_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3001 npm run test:blog-list --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `BACKY_ADMIN_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:5173 BACKY_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3001 npm run test:page-create --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, and `BACKY_ADMIN_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:5173 BACKY_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3001 npm run test:blog-create --workspace @backy-cms/admin` passed on 2026-05-25.
- **Pages preview busy-scope/reachability update**: `/pages` now treats library-wide busy state as only the blocking initial load, while preview, row mutation, and bulk mutation have their own scoped flags. Holding one page preview request pending now disables only preview-specific controls; New Page entry points, search/status/readiness filters, control-map links, Copy handoff, and export controls remain reachable instead of making the whole page library feel dead during a row preview. Bulk selection and mutation controls still lock during bulk work, and publish/archive/delete row mutations still prevent conflicting row mutations. Pages source guards fail if `isPageLibraryBusy` regresses to include `isPageMutationBusy`, and a targeted headless Chrome CDP check held the preview POST pending while asserting New Page, filters, handoff, and control-map anchors stayed enabled. `BACKY_PAGES_LIST_SOURCE_ONLY=1 npm run test:pages-list --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `npm run typecheck --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, the targeted `BACKY_PAGES_BUSY_SCOPE_CDP_PORT=9524` rendered preview-hold check against `http://127.0.0.1:5173/pages?siteId=site-demo`, `BACKY_ADMIN_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:5173 BACKY_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3001 npm run test:pages-list --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `git diff --check -- apps/admin/src/routes/pages.tsx apps/admin/scripts/pages-list-smoke.mjs specs/page-completion-audit/backy-page-surface-audit.md`, and `npm run doctor:release-certification` passed on 2026-05-25. In-app Browser validation was attempted but the Browser runtime reported `iab` unavailable, so the targeted CDP rendered check and full repo CDP smoke were used as fallback.
- **Blog preview busy-scope/reachability update**: `/blog` now keeps preview busy state separate from the broader editorial mutation lock. Starting a post preview disables duplicate preview and bulk-apply actions, but no longer makes New Post, search/status/taxonomy/author filters, handoff/API copy, CSV/JSON export, public/edit links, table sorting, pagination, and unrelated editorial controls feel dead. The Blog source guard fails if `isPostPreviewBusy` is folded back into `isBlogWorkflowBusy`, while rendered Blog list smoke still verifies taxonomy CRUD, SEO/comment/revision controls, bulk publish, public search/archive feeds, tokenized preview, focused canvas edit navigation, and no horizontal overflow. `BACKY_LOGIN_SOURCE_ONLY=1 npm run test:login --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `BACKY_BLOG_LIST_SOURCE_ONLY=1 npm run test:blog-list --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `npm run typecheck --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, and `BACKY_ADMIN_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:5173 BACKY_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3001 npm run test:blog-list --workspace @backy-cms/admin` passed on 2026-05-25. In-app Browser validation was attempted but the Browser runtime reported `iab` unavailable, so the repo CDP smoke was used as rendered fallback.

Collection and Media APIs plus final permission matrices remain authoritative, and the RBAC panel now shows the temporary role-default sync state. The Collections source guard fails if collection/media permissions or permission status badges regress to permission-hydration gating, and the rendered Collections smoke verifies schema creation, record creation/import/export, bulk selection, rich custom-frontend dynamic template design-state preservation, list/detail dynamic page APIs, media picker access, relationship/audit panels, public render paths, and cleanup. `npm run typecheck --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `BACKY_COLLECTIONS_SOURCE_ONLY=1 npm run test:collections --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, `BACKY_ADMIN_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:5173 BACKY_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3001 npm run test:collections --workspace @backy-cms/admin`, and `npm run doctor:release-certification` passed on 2026-05-25.
